observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) prepare the RCD for the above unrecorded ORs and submit the same to the Accounting Office for the preparation of the journal entry voucher; (ii) ensure that all collections are recorded in the RCD; and (iii) deposit intact and promptly all collections made with the authorized depository bank to prevent the possible loss and/or misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: (i) Implemented For item (i), the Municipal Treasurer has already prepared the RCDs for the unrecorded ORs. Moreover, the (ii) Implemented Municipal Accountant has (iii) Not prepared the For item (iii), the Municipal Treasurer still failed to deposit the collections, as well as the collections turned over to him by the collectors due to the Municipality’s geographical location.
observations.reason: For item (iii), the Municipal Treasurer still failed to deposit the collections, as well as the collections turned over to him by the collectors due to the Municipality’s geographical location.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the BRS for June to December 2023 under the GF and submit the same to the Audit Team in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011; and (ii) account for the unreconciled difference between the FS and BRS and draw the necessary adjusting entries to faithfully present the correct balance of the CIB-LCCA account.
observations.managementAction: (i) Not Implemented For item (i), the Municipal Accountant still failed to prepare the BRS for June to December 2024 due to voluminous workload. As regards item (ii), the Accounting personnel were having difficulty tracing the reconciling items from previous years.
observations.reason: For item (i), the Municipal Accountant still failed to prepare the BRS for June to December 2024 due to voluminous workload. As regards item (ii), the Accounting personnel were having difficulty tracing the reconciling items from previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require all accountable officers and employees who are still connected to the agency to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances and henceforth, liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period provided in Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002; b. exert extra efforts to recover outstanding receivables from the former Disbursing Officer who had already retired from the service; and c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the cash advances of the former Disbursing Officer totaling to P276,826.65 from Advances to Payroll to Other Receivables account.
observations.managementAction:
a. Not Implemented Outstanding cash advances still exists due to failure to demand liquidation.
b. Not Implemented Outstanding receivables from the former Disbursing Officer remain unrecovered due to lack of collection efforts.
c. Implemented The Municipal Accountant has already reclassified the cash advances of the former Disbursing Officer from Advances to Payroll to Other Receivables account.
observations.reason: Outstanding cash advances still exists due to failure to demand liquidation. Outstanding receivables from the former Disbursing Officer remain unrecovered due to lack of collection efforts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. organize the Municipal Inventory Committee which is tasked to conduct a complete physical count of all the inventory items and PPE at year-end to determine the validity, reliability and existence of the Municipality’s inventories and PPEs. Thereafter, the Inventory Committee shall reconcile the results of the count with the property records and accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. Also, the Committee is advised to avail of the guidelines/procedures set forth under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 relative to the one-time cleansing of the enormous amount of discrepancies in the PPE account balances of the Municipality. However, it is emphasized that the said circular was issued for one-time cleansing of PPE account balances; thus, in no case shall the procedures be used to further derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs and/or cleanse subsequent discrepancies or unreconciled balances in PPE accounts;
b. direct the Accounting Office and Treasurer’s Office to maintain the prescribed property records and ensure that these records are regularly reconciled to achieve a timely and reliable financial reports; and
c. require the Municipal Accountant to (i) transfer the Infrastructure asset from Trust Fund to the General Fund and provide allowance for depreciation therefor; and (ii) determine the estimated useful life of the PPE accounts and provide the corresponding allowance for depreciation.
observations.managementAction:
a. Implemented An Inventory Committee was organized in CY 2024.
Thereafter, the Inventory Committee Not Implemented The Inventory Committee is not yet well-versed with the procedures on one-time cleansing.

b. Implemented A supply officer was designated by the Municipality during the year, however, she failed to prepare and maintain the prescribed property records due to lack of awareness.

c. (i) Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant has difficulty retrieving the documents of the completed projects.
(ii) Not Implemented Some PPEs are no longer existing. As to the non-provision of allowance for depreciation was due to the failure of the Accounting Office to maintain PPE Cards and PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee is not yet well-versed with the procedures on one-time cleansing. A designated supply officer failed to prepare and maintain prescribed property records due to lack of awareness. The Municipal Accountant had difficulty retrieving the documents of the completed projects. Some PPEs are no longer existing and the Accounting Office failed to maintain PPE Cards and PPE Ledger Cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to (i) exert efforts in identifying the cause/s of negative balances of the three fund transfers; if necessary, make representation with the source agencies and reconcile with their records in order to come up with the correct balance of the Due to NGAs account; and (ii) submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents on the payments made above; and b. BAC to (i) strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 on the procurement of infrastructures; and (ii) submit the lacking documents and justifications on the noted lapses in the conduct of the above procurements.
observations.managementAction:
a. (i) Not Implemented The Accounting Office is having difficulty tracing the cause of the negative balance. (ii) Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant overlooked the need to comply.

b. Implemented For item b (i), no procurement of infrastructure was made by the LGU for CY2024 from fund transfers from NGAs. For item b (ii), the BAC failed to monitor the Audit Team’s recommendations.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office is having difficulty tracing the cause of the negative balances. The Municipal Accountant overlooked the need to submit lacking documents. The BAC failed to monitor the Audit Team’s recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the: a. MPDC, together with all other employees concerned, to closely monitor the development projects identified in the AIP and see to it that these projects are promptly and timely implemented for the benefit of the constituents. b. BAC to strictly adhere to the requirements of 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 (updated as of July 15, 2023) in all of the Municipality’s procurements; and c. the Municipal Accountant to (i) submit to the Audit Team the noted lacking documents; (ii) require the contractor to post CARI for infrastructure projects with contract cost above one million; (iii) require the supplier to post warranty security for the procured goods in compliance with Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (iv) ensure that all the documentary requirements are complete before processing the claims of the contractors/suppliers.
observations.managementAction:
a. Not Implemented The non-implementation was due to insufficient planning, coordination and monitoring among the responsible offices.

b. Not Implemented The BAC overlooked some of the processes in the procurement of infrastructure projects.

c. the Municipal Accountant to (i) (i) Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant overlooked the need to comply due to voluminous workload and failure to monitor the Audit Team’s recommendations. (ii) Implemented (iii) Not Implemented (iv) Implemented
observations.reason: Non-implementation was due to insufficient planning, coordination and monitoring among the responsible offices. The BAC overlooked some procurement processes. The Municipal Accountant overlooked some requirements due to voluminous workload and limited manpower; some documentary requirements were still lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. MDRRMO to (i) properly classify the allotment class of each PPA in the AIP and the LDRRMFIP; and (ii) facilitate the prompt implementation of the PPAs under the CY2024. b. Municipal Accountant to (i) comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002; (ii) review the regularity and completeness of supporting documents of disbursement vouchers before processing claims for payment in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445;(iii) submit the aforementioned lacking documents to the Audit Team to prove the transaction’s validity, accuracy, regularity and propriety; (iv) withhold the correct amount of tax from VAT-registered suppliers by dividing first the gross amount by 1.12 to get the vatable sales; and (v) henceforth, stop the practice of paying the supplier for undelivered items and comply with the terms of payment stated in the PO; and c. BAC to (i) strictly adhere to the requirements of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (ii) furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Purchase Order/Contract Agreement and its attachments within five working days from the issuance/execution of the contract as required under Items 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction:
a. MDRRMO to (i) Implemented The MDRRMO has properly classified the allotment class of each PPA in the AIP and LDRRMFIP; and (ii) Not Implemented The MDRRMO failed to optimally implement the allocated PPAs for CY 2024 because of lack of turnover made by the former MDRRMO to the current MDRRMO.

b. Municipal Accountant to (i) Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant failed to transfer the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF – MOOE to the STF for CY2024 due to voluminous transactions in December 2024. (ii) Not Implemented The Accounting Office has limited manpower. (iii) Not Implemented The Accounting Office has limited manpower. (iv) Implemented The Municipal Accountant has properly computed the tax withheld from the payments made to suppliers and contractors. (v) Implemented Not only paid items were delivered.

c. BAC to (i) Not Implemented The BAC overlooked some of the processes in procurement. (ii) Not Implemented This is due to the significant distance of the Municipality from the Auditor’s office.
observations.reason: Non-implementation due to lack of proper turnover of MDRRMO post, voluminous transactions and limited manpower in the Accounting Office; procurement monitoring hampered by geographic distance from Auditor’s office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Chairperson, GPFS-TWG)/GAD Focal Person to (i) prepare a revised GPB, if necessary, to ensure conformance of the GAD Budget per GPB with the budget allocation in the approved Annual Budget; and (ii) ensure the correctness of all the information provided in the GAD AR; b. GAD Focal Person and the Municipal Budget Officer to be guided by Item 3(C.4)(5)(c) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 in the attributions for GAD related projects; c. Municipal Accountant to (i) check the completeness and veracity of supporting documents prior to processing of claims in compliance with Section 474(b)(5) of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445; and (ii) strictly adhere to the requirements of COA Circular No. 2021-001 in processing the claims for travel of LGU officials and employees; and d. BAC and its Secretariat to be guided by COA Circular No. 2009-001 in the submission of contract/purchase order and the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services.
observations.managementAction:
a. (i) Not Implemented The GPB for CY 2024 was already prepared prior to the conduct of the exit conference for CY 2023 audit. (ii) Not Implemented The GAD Focal Person failed to monitor the Audit Team’s recommendations.

b. Implemented GAD PPAs were properly attributed.

c. Municipal Accountant to (i) Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant lacks manpower. (ii) Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant overlooked the need to comply.

d. Not Implemented Due to significant distance of the Municipality from the Auditor’s office.
observations.reason: GPB prepared prior to audit exit conference; GAD Focal Person failed to monitor recommendations; Municipal Accountant constrained by limited manpower; geographic distance impeded timely submission of procurement documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the unsubmitted accounts immediately. It is to be emphasized that non-submission of the lacking disbursement vouchers and payroll, as well as relevant supporting documents, shall warrant the outright issuance of Notices of Disallowance without prejudice to any administrative disciplinary action that may be instituted against the accountable officers as provided under Section 39 (3) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant overlooked the need to comply.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant overlooked the need to comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account in the General Fund for the operation of the Water Works System and the Power Supply System of the Municipality through the use of a complete set of subsidiary ledgers in accordance with Section 313 of R.A. No 7160 and 107 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I to facilitate the audit and verification of the transactions incurred by the said economic enterprise and to enable the Management to readily determine and evaluate its results of operation and financial condition.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant had overlooked the need to comply.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had overlooked the need to comply.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze and review the accounting entries made on the accounts affected, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect appropriate adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the agency’s financial position.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The Accounting Office lacks manpower.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office lacks manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to: a.1 Reconcile the PPE balance of CY 2023 as of December 31, 2023; a.2 Facilitate the preparation of the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards respectively, and reconcile the same, in accordance with Sections 119 and 120, Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual; a.3 Request for additional manpower, when warranted and feasible, to assist in the preparation and reconciliation of PPE records and reports; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to: b.1 Reflect the correct Depreciation Expense and Accumulated Depreciation by considering the salvage value of PPEs and correct depreciable amount, in accordance with to Paragraph 71 of IPSAS 17 and PAG 6 of IPSAS 17; b.2 Reclassify tangible items with acquisition cost of more than ₱50,000.00 recorded under the liability and expense accounts to PPE accounts in compliance with the capitalization threshold of COA Circular No. 2022-004; b.3 Reclassify tangible items with acquisition cost of less than ₱50,000.00 initially classified as PPE to semi-expendable properties and the related accumulated depreciation to be closed in the books of accounts in accordance with Item Nos. 4.2 and 4.3 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022; and c. Require the Inventory Committee to complete the one-time cleansing of the PPE in accordance with the procedures of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish the PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction: PPE Ledger Cards were maintained by Accounting Office but updated while Property Cards and were not updated by the GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to reconcile the variance of ₱3,680,141.10 in the Inventory balance of the RPCI and the Statement of Financial Position; and b. The Inventory Committee to submit the RPCI to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
observations.managementAction: RAAF submitted did not reflect the actual inventory count. Inventory cards not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The Municipal Assessor’s Office to update their record of parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government and furnish the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer of the supporting documents for recording purposes; b. The Supply Officer to make corrections in the RPCPPE to reflect the Land actually owned by the Municipality based on the corrected list from the Office of the Municipal Assessor; and c. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to present the correct balance of the Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements in compliance with Section 72 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: PPE Ledger Cards were maintained by Accounting Office but not updated while Property Cards and were not prepared by the GSO.
observations.reason: RPCPPE for revision pending installation of Inventory System

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: exhaust efforts to retrieve the supporting documents and reconcile the discrepancy of ₱3.508 million between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances to present reliable data in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.reason: Process of retrieving documents and records based on the program

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Exhaust efforts to identify the accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances and demand immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advance; b. Enforce strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 on the grant and liquidation of cash advances; and c. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of cash advances to determine long outstanding and dormant receivables and prepare necessary reports together with the supporting documents for possible request for write-off, if warranted, as indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records of 2010 since the automation of accounting starts 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The Supply Officer to locate the properties amounting to ₱269,600.00, validate the serviceability of the properties, and include in the IIRUP and request for disposal, when warranted, in consonance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445; b. The Disposal Committee to adhere to Section 125 of NGAS Volume I in preparing the IIRUP upon inspection that the properties are considered unserviceable; c. The General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer to request in writing for the disposal of unserviceable properties, to the Office of the Auditor, upon submission of the IIRUP in accordance with Section 167 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 22, 1992; and d. The Municipal Accountant, thereafter, to prepare necessary journal entries to derecognize from the books of account all the disposed unserviceable properties to reflect the correct balance of the recorded PPE.
observations.reason: Still on process of locating, of cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The designated personnel to make a follow up request for the necessary papers/documents of donated vehicles from the donors and the LTO, and expedite the transfer of ownership of these vehicles in the name of the Municipality; and b. The Municipal Accountant to establish the value of the donated vehicles based on the supporting documents gathered and record the donated PPE in the books of accounts in accordance with IPSAS 23.
observations.managementAction: No documents turned over yet.
observations.reason: Still on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The Municipal Accountant to: a.1 Submit immediately the lacking documents in Annexes G-1 and G-2; a.2 Examine the completeness of the supporting documents and ensure that the documents are completely filled out prior to signing of Box A of the Disbursement Voucher; b. The Municipal Engineering Officer to adeptly develop the Program of Works to minimize/eliminate inconsistencies with the actual implementation and prepare the Job Order contracts based on the approved Program of Works; c. The Municipal Budget Officer to completely fill out the Obligation Request form by including a series number; and d. All the officials concerned to strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No.1445 and Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-001 by ensuring that proper and complete documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before processing of payment.
observations.managementAction: Lacking supporting documents noted for some transactions.
observations.reason: Some transactions lacked supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The concerned Implementing Offices to expedite the implementation of the programs/activities to utilize the fund transfers within the prescribed period and strictly observe the guidelines set in the Memorandum of Agreement, otherwise, secure a written and approved amendment to the terms of the MOA, when warranted; b. The Municipal Accountant to: b.1 Exhaust efforts to retrieve the supporting documents and expedite the return of the unexpended balances to the Source Agencies in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; b.2 Send confirmation letter to the Source Agency to confirm the balance per books of prior years’ unutilized fund transfers, and consequently, submit the liquidation report or refund the same, whichever is applicable; and c. The Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the concerned implementing offices upon receipt of funds transferred from various NGAs for the timely implementation by the said offices of the programs, projects and activities, and prompt recording of its subsequent liquidation.
observations.reason: Retrieval of documents in progress; verification to various source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LEE exhaust efforts to increase its collections and/or adopt cost-cutting measures to produce revenue and/or refund the amount of fund transfers from the General Fund of ₱9.028 million.
observations.managementAction: No enough funds to pay the whole amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Convene the municipal officials/personnel responsible to formulate a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented PPA amounting to ₱6.399 million; b. Direct the MPDO to ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready in accordance with Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020; and c. Require the responsible officers to formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement the programmed development projects within the required timeline.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The General Service Office to locate the properties amounting to ₱269,600.00, validate the serviceability of the properties, and include in the IIRUP and request for disposal, when warranted, in consonance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445; b. The Disposal Committee to adhere to Section 125 of NGAS Volume I in preparing the IIRUP upon inspection that the properties are considered unserviceable; c. The General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer to request in writing for the disposal of unserviceable properties, to the Office of the Auditor, upon submission of the IIRUP in accordance with Section 167 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 22, 1992; and d. The Municipal Accountant, thereafter, to prepare necessary journal entries to derecognize from the books of account all the disposed unserviceable properties to reflect the correct balance of the recorded PPE.
observations.reason: Still on process of cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The Municipal Accountant to prepare and certify the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF following the Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and b. The OIC MDRRMO to: b.1 Adhere with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and b.2 Furnish the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) and the Department of Interior and Local Government Operations Office (DILG-OO) copies of the LDRRMFIP and the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, in compliance with Section 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Direct the Accountable Officers (AOs) and the Liquidating Officer to prepare the RAAF monthly and submit to the Office of the Auditor, in accordance with Section 50 of NGAS Manual, Volume II; and b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: b.1 Prepare the CRAAF in accordance with Section 51 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume II by recording and consolidating the data in the RAAF of each AO, computing the balances using the number of units, including the checks and the cash tickets in the accountable forms, if there are any; and b.2 Submit the CRAAF, together with the RAAF, to the Office of the Auditor, not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Designate an official/employee to: a.1 Consolidate the PPA data from each department, and at the beginning of the year, provide the Office of the Auditor of the list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, consistent with Item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; a.2 Prepare and submit the quarterly Report on Government PPA, including all of the ongoing, for implementation, and fully implemented PPA, to the Audit Team; and b. Direct all department heads concerned to prepare the list of all ongoing, for implementation, and fully implemented PPA and submit it to the designated official/employee for consolidation.
observations.managementAction: For submission of list of all on-going PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the GAD PPDO Focal Point System to: a. Ensure the accuracy of the balances and totals presented in the GAD Plan and Budget; b. Submit, henceforth, the GPB to the PPDO and DILG PO for review and approval, following Item 4.0 (3) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016; and c. Adhere with Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 on the submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from the end of January.

We recommended that the amounts received by the former Secretary to the Sangguniang Bayan and Municipal Treasurer since the time they were relieved from their original functions shall be refunded to the Municipality. Thus, in the former SB Secretary shall refund the RATA he received from March 1 to December 31, 2011 for a total of ₱88,000.00 while the former Municipal Treasurer shall refund a total of ₱96,800.00 representing the RATA she received since February 1 to December 31, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The total amount due from the Municipal Treasurer was deducted from her terminal pay. Likewise, the total amount due from the former SB secretary will be deducted from her terminal pay upon her retirement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to fast-track the completion of the one-time cleansing of PPE following the procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to fairly present the PPE balances in the financial statements;
b. Designate a full-time General Services Officer (GSO) who will be responsible for the preservation, maintenance, and safekeeping of the PPEs of the LGU; and
c. Pending the cleansing of the PPE account, require the municipal accountant and the GSO to maintain separate ledger and property cards and PAR for acquisitions of PPE in C.Y. 2024 onwards, for PPE acquisitions for CY 2024 where both records reconcile.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant strictly observe the timeline of submission of BRS to the Audit Team as laid under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: Though, the Municipal Accountant prepared the required BRS up to December 2024, the required timeline has still not yet observed, incurring delays in submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Accountant that transactions pertaining to the Due to BIR account be traced to prior years’ accounting entries to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of tracing reconciling items to address the abnormal balance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the GAD Focal Point System to:
1. Ensure that the planned GAD activities are implemented as scheduled;
observations.managementAction: The planned GAD activities were implemented as scheduled; The GAD Accomplishment Reports still showed few unimplemented activities.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile their records to avoid further accumulation of errors/reconciling items. Thereafter, observe the necessary monthly reconciliation for early detection of errors and/or omissions of transactions.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented. The Municipal Accountant prepared the required BRS up to December 2024, the required timeline has still not yet observed, incurring delays in submission.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the validation and adjustment of the above reconciling items presented in the bank reconciliation statements. observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management:
a. Instruct the Motorpool In-Charge to approve time records when the heavy equipment indicated in trip tickets filled-up by customers; b. Direct the Municipal Engineer/Municipal Agriculturist, and the GSO to conduct pre and post-inspection for every lease transaction; c. Require the GSO to verify details of trip tickets vis-à-vis the amount paid and require the contractor for payment of deficiencies, if there’s any; d. Instruct the GSO to maintain custody of equipment keys; e. Execute a lease contract with a contractor to cover the terms and conditions of the lease agreement; and f. Directing the Municipal Treasurer to set income targets and prepare separate income reporting for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction:
a. Logbook for the Motorpool In-Charge to approve time records when the heavy equipment departed and returned is maintained by a staff of the Municipal Treasurer's Office
b. The Municipal Engineer and the designated Supply Officer, per inquiry, that no pre and post inspection in every lease transaction of heavy equipment was undertaken.
c. No action taken to implement the audit recommendation.
d. The keys of the heavy equipment are still in the custody of the Municipal Treasury Office staff.
e. No action taken to implement the audit recommendation.
f. No action taken to implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: There is no pre/post inspection; The Municipal Treasurer's Office staff maintains custody of equipment keys; No lease contracts executed; No separate income reporting prepared.

observations.recommendation:
a) Municipal Treasurer (i) include a detailed, year-by-year
breakdown of
tax dues and payments in the RPTAR to facilitate accurate validation,
reconciliation, and monitoring of taxpayer accounts.; (ii) furnish the Municipal
Accountant at the beginning of each year with a certified list of taxpayers and
the amount due and collectible to support the recognition of RPT and SET
receivables; (iii) maintain updated records of delinquent taxpayers and their
respective tax dues and (iv) intensify collection efforts on long outstanding RPT
and SET receivables prioritizing accounts with significant amounts or those
long overdue; and
b) Municipal Accountant (i) establish and maintain complete SLs for RPT
and SET receivables in accordance with Volume I of the MNGAS for LGU and
(ii) utilize SLs for periodic reconciliation with the General Ledger to validate
the accuracy of reported balances; and
c) Municipal Treasurer and Accountant conduct regular reconciliations
of their respective records to ensure consistency between the RPTAR, SLs, and
the General Ledger

(a) LCE create an Inventory Committee that will(i) conduct a complete and
timely physical count of all inventory items in accordance with Section 124
of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for
Local Government Units (LGUs); and (ii) prepare and submit the
corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) within
the prescribed deadlines and ensure that it accurately reflects only the
inventory items physically on hand at year-end;
(b) offices with custody of inventories (i) designate a Property Custodian
responsible for maintaining the required Stock Cards (SCs) for each
inventory item; (ii) implement a standardized and complete inventory
documentation system that records receipts, issuances, consumption, and
balances; and (iii) submit monthly Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) and
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) reflecting the movement
and balances of inventory items;
(c) GSO consolidate the submitted RISs and SSMIs and transmit them to
the Accounting Office promptly to facilitate timely and accurate recording
of inventory transactions in the books of accounts;
(d) Municipal Accountant consistently update the Supplies Ledger Cards
(SLCs) to ensure accurate accounting records; and
(e) Accounting Office and GSO coordinate regularly and perform periodic
reconciliation of their respective inventory records to ensure consistency and
accuracy of reported inventory balances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the: a) GSO and Accounting
Office to reconcile their property records to come up with accurate balances of the
PPE accounts; b) Inventory Committee to (i) submit the RPCPPE submit the
complete RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year duly providing all the
necessary information; and (ii) determine the missing or unlocated PPE items and
identify persons or offices responsible for them; and c) GSO and Disposal Committee
to prepare and submit the IIRUP together with the necessary supporting documents
for the disposal of unserviceable PPE items to prevent further loss and deterioration
of the same.

a) Municipal Accountant (i) adopt a methodology based on historical data to
estimate the portion of leave credits expected to be used or paid within 12
months and classify them as current liabilities, and the remaining
accumulated leave benefits, expected to be payable upon separation, be
recognized as long-term liabilities in compliance with IPSAS 39; and (ii)
recognize leave benefits yearly as they are earned, rather than recording
the entire liability in a single year;
b) Municipal Budget Officer establish an Employee Benefits Reserve Fund
on a yearly basis to ensure sufficient cash reserves for future monetization;
c) Human Resource Department to coordinate with the City Accounting
Office and City Budget Office to ensure adherence to Rule XVI of the
Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (EO) No. 292
on the yearly accounting and funding of leave benefits.

We recommended and the management agreed to continue to implement
programs, projects, and activities financed through fund transfers from various
government agencies that are beneficial to the LGU’s constituents.

We also recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to moving forward,
and in light of the insufficient guidelines, record the twenty percent (20%) BFP Fire
Code Fee share under the GF rather than TF. Furthermore, the utilization of the said
fund should undergo the regular appropriation process and obtain approval from the
local Sanggunian.

We reiterated the audit recommendations and the management agreed ensure
that: i) all claims against LGU are supported with complete documentation; (ii)
proper coordination with the Office of the President takes place to bring their records in
agreement with the records of the latter’s Finance/Accounting Section; and iii)
negotiation/condonation of accumulated interest are carried out, otherwise, book up
the interest expense arising from the loan.

We also reiterated and the management agreed to consider the write-off of
dormant accounts in accordance with the procedures set forth in COA Circular No.
2023-008.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to (i) strictly enforce
compliance with the Municipal Revenue Code to ensure that the correct amount of
business tax is assessed and collected and (ii) require the submission of a sworn
statement of the business’ gross sales/receipt or copy of income tax return to ensure
the accuracy and reliability of declared tax bases.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) ensure the full delivery of
DF PPAs such that optimal use of the fund is achieved and that the LGU constituents
could immediately enjoy its intended benefits; (b) continue to comply the guidelines
mandated by DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-1 in the utilization of the 20% DF; and
(c) submit the documentation deficiencies to the Audit Team.

Also, we recommended, and management agreed to ascertain that the CSHP
is approved by the DOLE, as part of the contract and agreement between the
Municipality and the contractor, before entering into contract with the winning
bidder.

(a) MDRRMO (i) ensure that all proposed PPAs are properly planned and
aligned with eligible disaster risk reduction and management activities as
prescribed by law and (ii) submit a written explanation establishing the
direct relevance of the noted activities to disaster prevention and
mitigation, in accordance with the provisions of JMC No. 2013-1, to justify
the eligibility of their funding under the LDRRMF;
(b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly comply with the provisions of
the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services; and
(c) Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of supporting documents
as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing payments
for contracted projects.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) agreed to: (i)
actively participate and complete the scheduled capacity-building sessions on the
HGDG and PIMME tools and (ii) institutionalize the use of HGDG and PIMME tools
in the formulation, implementation, monitoring, and evaluation of GAD PPAs
beginning with the CY 2026 GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to exert efforts to prepare the lacking BRS for the abovementioned
periods and henceforth ensure the timely and regular preparation of BRS for all
bank accounts of the Municipality in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011;

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal
Mayor direct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) ensure that all RCDs are accurately prepared
and reviewed prior to submission to the Accounting Office; (ii) strictly monitor fund
balances prior to depositing collections to prevent over deposit; and (iii) deposit intact
all collections made; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) henceforth review the
accuracy of the RCD submitted by the Treasury Office prior to the recording thereof;
and (ii) draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the CLT and other affected
account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to look into the cause of the variance amounting to P373,162.22 and
prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require all accountable officers and employees who are still connected to
the agency to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances and
henceforth, liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period
provided in Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002;
b. exert extra efforts to recover outstanding receivables from the retired AO;
and
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the cash advances of the
retired AO totaling P26,456.00 from Advances to Officers and Employees
to Other Receivables account.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant
to undertake one-time cleansing of PPE accounts prescribed under COA
Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with correct balances of PPE accounts in
the financial statements;
b. direct the Supply Officer to prepare and maintain the PCs for each item of
PPEs to promote transparency in asset management; and
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure the preparation and
maintenance of PPELC for all PPE; (ii) provide depreciation for
depreciable PPEs in compliance with Paragraphs 43 and 71 of IPSAS 17;
and (iii) exert efforts to look for the documents pertaining to the
Infrastructure Assets and prepare the necessary journal entry for the
transfer thereof to the GF books.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count
semestrally for inventories. Also, reconcile the results of the count with the
accounting records and submit the complete RPCI to the Audit Team in
compliance with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
b. direct the designated Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to
maintain SCs and SLCs, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to exert efforts in identifying the cause/s of negative balances of the
two fund transfers. If necessary, make representation with the source agencies
and reconcile with their records in order to come up with the correct balance of
the Due to NGAs account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) be guided by COA
Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 which provide for the Revised
Chart of Accounts for the LGUs in the recording of transactions; and (b) draw the
necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balance of the accounts
affected.

1.17 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct:
a) The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the General Services
Office, to investigate the discrepancy noted between the books and the
RPCPPE, reconcile the recorded amounts, and make the necessary
adjustments;
b) The GSO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to exert
effort in locating documents needed to provide the details of each PPE
item and to indicate the corresponding Property Item Number in PPE
ledger cards;
c) The GSO and Accounting Office to ensure the regular maintenance of
Property Cards (PC) and PPE Ledger Cards, respectively, for all PPE
items; and
d) The Municipal Accountant to reclassify completed projects from the
Construction-in Progress account to their appropriate PPE accounts
and provide the corresponding depreciation.
1.18 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA Circular No.
2020-006 to finally establish the PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability.

2.16 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a) Inventory Committee to regularly conduct the physical count of
inventories and semi-expendable properties, prepare the corresponding
reports using the required format and submit them to the Auditor
within the reglementary period;
b) General Services Officer to prepare the appropriate stock/property
cards and reconcile the supply/property records with the accounting
records; and
c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that necessary adjusting entries be
supported with Requisition Issue Slips, Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued and other pertinent documents.

3.14 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Undertake measures in identify the undetermined prior year's balances
of the accounts Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs;
b) Make appropriate adjusting entries to transfer special purpose funds to
the Trust Liabilities accounts and maintain a subsidiary ledger for each
fund;
c) Identify the cash prizes with or without specific utilization
guidelines/conditions imposed by the awarding body, and record them
accordingly, as either Miscellaneous Income or Trust Liabilities with the
necessary Prior Period Adjustments;
d) Make appropriate adjusting entries on the following transactions:
• To record fund transfers from the various funds of the Municipality
to the Trust fund and to record and ensure that the subsequent
liquidations are recorded in the appropriate account; and
• To record refunds made by the DSWD KALAHI CIDSS NCDDP AF,
for the excess of funds previously transferred by the Municipality.
e) Reconcile the negative balances of the subsidiary ledgers of Due to
LGUs account for the CTC and RPT shares amounting to ₱2,718.87 and
₱74,012.38, respectively, and make adjusting entries, if warranted.

4.4 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Prepare adjusting entries for the erroneous recording of the
undelivered items as per PO No. 011-029-0612, and overtime pay claims
of the officers and employees of the Municipality, as specified in Annex
F;
b) Coordinate with the General Services Office and determine the correct
date of receipt of the equipment, included in PO No. 011-019-0768;
c) Adhere to the provisions of Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445, COA Circular
No.2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and the pertinent provisions of the
MNGAS for LGUs relative to the supporting documents necessary in the
recording of Accounts Payable of the Municipality and ensure that the
appropriate account are correctly used in the recording of the financial
transactions of the Municipality.

1.9. We reiterate our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:

1.9.1 Enjoin the Office of the Accounting and Treasury to reconcile their
records regularly to avoid accumulation of variance; and

1.9.2 Require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal vouchers for valid
reconciling items.

2.6. We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive:

2.6.1. Enjoin the Inventory Committee to:
2.6.1.1. Conduct physical inventory count at least once a year and sub-
mit to the Audit Team the corresponding Report on Physical
Count of PPE (RPCPPE); and
2.6.1.2. Expedite` the One-Time Cleansing of the PPE account in ac-
cordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular No.
2020-006, to ensure that the PPE balances are fairly presented
in the financial statements;
2.6.2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw necessary correcting entries
for the erroneous depreciation;
2.6.2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to update all the Property Ledger
Cards; and
2.6.3. Direct the Supply Officer to update all the Property Cards

3.6.We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary
correcting entries to effect the dropping of the PPE costing below P50,000.00
including current year acquired inventories duly issued to LGU employees and
officials and provide a copy of the JEV together with the schedule or list of
affected PPE/inventory items to the designated Supply Officer for appropriate
action in accordance with the cited Circular.

4.6. We reiterate our recommendation that:

4.6.1. The Sangguniang Bayan require the Municipal Engineer (ME) to
prepare the Program of Works (POWs) and Detailed Engineering
Designs (DEDs) for all infrastructure programs prior to the approval
of the 20% Development Fund budget. This step is crucial in ensuring
that the projects are implementation-ready and can be executed
efficiently. At the very least, the Municipal Engineer should be
consulted during the budget deliberations to assess the feasibility and
doability of the proposed projects, ensuring that they align with the
available budget and project scope; and

4.6.2. The Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Secretariat to properly fill
out the Annual Procurement Plan.

5.5. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

5.5.1. Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit the Project Logbook,
Weather Chart, Geotagged Pictures or any supporting documents the
work suspension for further evaluation by the Audit Team; and

5.5.2. Henceforth, ensure that any similar requests must be supported with
proper documentation and carefully evaluated to be within the
bounds of law before granted by the Municipality.

6.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to draw correcting entries treating the errors on the transfer of
unspent prior year LDRRMF to the Trust Fund and for the reversion of the
unspent DRRMF CY 2018 to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.

7.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the HRMO to
develop a standardized JO contract template that outlines, among others, the
duration of employment, terms and conditions, specific duties and
responsibilities, office assignments, rates, and work schedules and ensure
that JO personnel signs the contract before starting work, with a copy
provided to the employee.

8.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

8.7.1 Establish and strengthen the gender-responsive monitoring and eval-
uation (M&E) system by issuing the necessary Executive or Adminis-
trative Order and formally constituting the GAD M&E Team;
8.7.2 Conduct regular assessment of institutional mechanisms for gender
mainstreaming to monitor the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD im-
plementation; and
8.7.3 Prepare and ensure the timely submission of the annual LGU Status
Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming to the
DILG Regional Office.

9.6. In the light of the foregoing, we recommended the following:

9.6.1. That the Local Chief Executive (LCE), in coordination with the
Municipal Accountant and other responsible officials, be required to
submit comprehensive written justifications why the subject
transactions should not be subject to disallowance pursuant to Section
33 of PD No. 1445, given the indications of irregular, unnecessary, and
extravagant expenditures;
9.6.2. That the LGU be required to submit the complete and duly approved
Activity Design, indicating the specific funding source, detailed
budget breakdown, program objectives and deliverables, and a clear
monitoring and evaluation mechanism;
9.6.3. That all missing procurement documents—including contracts,
receipts, and quotations for venue rental, meals, and transport—be
submitted to validate compliance with Republic Act No. 9184
(Government Procurement Reform Act) and its Implementing Rules
and Regulations or an explanation why they were not procured by the
Municipality;
9.6.4. That the LGU substantiate the basis for selecting Siargao Island and
its constituent LGUs as the benchmarking destination, including
proof of coordination, written consent, or formal invitation from the
host LGUs and the criteria used in their selection;
9.6.5. That the LGU clarify how the benchmarking activity aligned with its
identified Capacity Development (CapDev) needs and submit

11.15. We recommended that:

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Make corrections on the misclassified accounts totaling ₱1.453 million and reflect the correct cost of the specific asset account.
observations.reason: Lacking transactions with supporting documents noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Revert to the General Fund the amount of ₱1.046 million and this shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefore are completed, in accordance with Item 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and b. Prepare the necessary entries for the reversion of the funds amounting to ₱1.046 million to correct the overstatement of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and the understatement of the Government Equity Account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Exhaust efforts to retrieve the supporting documents as basis for her proposed liability adjustment in connection with the Other Land Improvements; and b. Closely monitor the Local Government Unit’s expenditures to avoid cash insufficiency and see to it that monies held in trust be used solely for the intended purpose, in accordance with Section 305 (e) of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: a. The concerned Implementing Offices to submit additional pictures indicating the date and place taken thereof to support the validity and integrity of the claims amounting to a total of ₱1.705 million; and b. The Municipal Accountant to review the propriety of the supporting documents before certifying in the Box A of the DV in accordance with Section 38, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Require the responsible office to compute the total liquidated damages equal to one-tenth of one percent (0.001) of the cost of the delayed deliveries/unperformed portion for every day of delay in accordance with Section 68 of 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and impose collection thereof from the supplier/contractor concerned; and b. Direct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the timely implementation of PPAs in accordance with the approved Purchase Order/Contract.
observations.reason: Cause of delay was weather condition and negotiation of ROW.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to issue a demand letter to the Retired Collecting Officer and require the Collecting Officers to produce the undeposited collections and deposit the same with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB).

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to: a.1 Indicate in the disbursement voucher of the cash advance the liquidation period and the duration of the project implementation following Item 6.1.5 of Commission on Audit-Department of Budget and Management–Department of the Interior and Local Government–Governance Commission for GOCCs–Department of National Defense (COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND) Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01; a.2 Discontinue the practice of granting cash advance for confidential funds for requirements of more than three months in adherence to Item 6.1.5 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01; a.3 Submit the Disbursement Voucher (DV) of the cash advance for confidential funds to the Office of the Auditor, within seven days after the release of the check in consonance with Item 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01; a.4 Stamp or print on the DV the certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and proper accounting thereof was made in accordance with Item 6.1.5 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01; and a.5 Ensure completeness of the details of the DV and correctness of the statements in the certifications attached to the cash advance for confidential expenses. b. Instruct the certifying officers of the DV and the Obligation Request, to indicate in the DV the date of signing of the documents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to complete the process of the revision/updating of the Market Code to adjust the fees and charges accordingly, consistent with Municipal Ordinance No. 2017-19 or the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer has prepared a Schedule of Unreleased Checks as of December 31, 2024 and has submitted to the Municipal Accounting Office; and
The Municipal Accountant has drawn an adjusting journal entry to record the unreleased checks as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a.Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw correcting entries treating the errors on the transfer of unspent prior year LDRRMF to the Trust Fund; and
b.For the succeeding years, enjoin the Municipal Accountant to timely transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund account.
observations.managementAction:
The error self-corrected upon actual transfer of the fund.
The Municipal Accountant successfully transferred the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund account on December 27, 2024.

The Municipal Assessor has submitted his operational plan to the Sangguniang Bayan
The amount of P100,000 is appropriated on the CY 2025 budget via Appropriation Ordinance No. 2, series of 2024

observations.managementAction:
The amount of P351,680.00 is replenished on Fully CY 2025 budget Implemented via Appropriation Ordinance No. 2, series of 2024

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:

c. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to ensure that the Notice of Award, Purchase Order/Contract of Agreement, and Notice to Proceed, for future procurement through any of the alternative modes, are posted in the PhilGEPS website as provided under Section 54.3 of IRR of RA No. 9184
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat has posted the Notice of Award, Purchase Order/Contract and Notice to Proceed for future procurement through alternative modes to the PhilGEPS.

No. 23

observations.reason: Ongoing processing of the land titling - three lots under evaluation and four lots for release of documents from the Registry of Deeds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the previous Collection Officers to produce the undeposited collections and deposit the same with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) and provide explanation why the said amount was undeposited as of December 31, 2021 or if necessary, initiate proper proceedings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the cash advances, determine long outstanding and dormant receivables and prepare necessary reports together with the supporting documents for the request for write-off, if warranted, as indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the amounts received by the former Secretary to the Sangguniang Bayan and Municipal Treasurer since the time they were relieved from their original functions shall be refunded to the Municipality. Thus, the former SB Secretary shall refund the RATA he received from March 1 to December 31, 2011 for a total of ₱88,000.00 while the former Municipal Treasurer shall refund a total of ₱96,800.00  representing the RATA she received since February 1 to December 31, 2011.
observations.reason: The total amount due from the Municipal Treasurer was deducted from her terminal pay. Likewise, the total amount due from the former SB secretary will be deducted from her terminal pay upon her retirement. However, she is only 48 years old as of the report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant strictly observe the timeline of submission of BRS to the Audit Team as laid under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has seriously endeavored to submit Bank Reconciliation Statements within the prescribed time.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had taken her maternity leave during the year thereby attributing to piled up back logs including the preparation of the BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Accountant that transactions pertaining to the Due to BIR account be traced to prior years’ accounting entries to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Some transactions were already adjusted but the Municipal Accountant is still tracing the transactions which had caused the abnormal balance of the Due to BIR.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had her maternity leave during the year. Hence, she had very limited amount of time, given the handful reports she had to cope up upon her return.

observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of tracing and identifying the source agencies together with the returnable balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management, headed by the Local Chief Executive
(LCE), agreed to continue to (i) ensure optimal use of the DF so that socio-economic
programs and projects that are beneficial to the LGU’s constituents are timely
implemented; and (ii) comply with the rules on the appropriation and utilization of the
20% DF for eligible projects pursuant to Item 3.2.5 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1
dated November 4, 2020.
We also recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Barangay officials of
a) six barangays to (i) explain the reason for non-implementation of their proposed
projects during CY 2023 and (ii) implement in CY 2024 the development projects
sourced from the Municipality’s 20% DF; and b) 22 barangays to liquidate the
financial assistance from the Municipality.
Lastly, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Bids and
Awards Committee and concerned officials of the Municipality (i) comply with the
warranty security requirement for the procurement of goods to assure that
manufacturing defects shall be corrected by the supplier, if any; and (ii) comply with
the requirements under the 2016 revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and submit the lacking
documentary requirements to the Audit Team to prove the propriety and regularity
of disbursement transactions noted.

We recommended that the Management instruct the GAD Focal Point System
(GFPS) to prioritize the implementation of the planned training/seminars on the use
of the HGDG and PIMME tools to measure the actual cost attributed to gender
responsiveness and know if the existing mechanism responds to the basic 5%
requirement of the law.

We commended Management for the efficient collection performance. We
recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) continue to
comply and enforce the Municipal Revenue Code so that correct business tax will accrue for
the benefit of the Municipality and (ii) require the submission of a sworn statement of the
business’ gross sales/receipt or copy of income tax return to ensure that the basis declared
by the taxpayers are reliable and accurate.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (i) Municipal Treasurer
to (a) update the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index Card yearly and subsequently prepare
and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis
of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year; (b)
provide and maintain records of delinquent taxpayers and their respective tax dues
and intensify tax collection efforts on outstanding RPT and SET receivables; (ii)
Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers required in
Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and (iii) Municipal Treasurer and
Accountant reconcile their records to come up with the correct balances of the
RPT/SET Receivables and their corresponding reciprocal accounts.

We reiterated the audit recommendations and the LCE agreed to instruct the
Accounting Office, Budget Office, and other concerned Offices to (i) exhaust all
means to locate documents pertaining to the dormant fund transfers, (ii) determine
whether the purposes can still be achieved, and (iii) consider the write-off of dormant
accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We reiterated the audit recommendations and the Management agreed that
(a) Municipal Accountant and GSO reconcile their respective PPE records and the
latter to update and maintain PCs following the account classification prescribed for
LGUs; (b) Municipal Accountant (i) reconcile his records with the Assessor’s Office
regarding the parcels of land the Municipality owns for proper recognition and/or
reporting; and (ii) determine the fair market value, in coordination with the
Municipal Assessor, of the unrecorded lands owned by the Municipality and book the
same as part of PPE.
We recommended and the Management agreed to create an Inventory
Committee to (i) conduct physical count of PPE and submit RPCPPE not later than
January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs and (ii)
facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation
of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to strictly follow the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the
procurement of goods and services; and (b) Municipal Accountant to check the
completeness of supporting documents provided by COA Circular 2012-001 before
effecting payment for contracted projects. We further recommend the submission of
the revised AIP as required by NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC 2013-1 dated
March 25, 2013.

We recommended that Management (i) conduct a feasibility study for the
operation of the slaughterhouse, including the rates and optimal manning to generate
revenue and reduce expenses; (ii) fully implement the 2020 Market Code in order to
realize an increased revenue that will cover the operating expenses of the market
operations; and (iii) revise the accounting treatment on the sale of burial rights so
that management decisions are based on correct information.

We recommended and the Management agreed to create an Inventory
Committee to (i) conduct the periodic physical count of inventories and (ii) prepare
and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team.
We reiterated the recommendations and the Management agreed that (a)
GSO maintain the SCs to record receipts and issuances of supplies; and (b)
Municipal Accountant maintain the complete SLCs for each type of supplies.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the SWM Board, with
MENRO as lead office, (i) harmonize the SWMP with the annual budgets of the
Municipality; (ii) oversee the implementation and completion of programmed
SWM projects and interventions to ensure that the intended outcomes are fully
achieved; and (iii) consider the possibility of adding additional methods of
processing waste material in the MRF to further reduce residual wastes.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Adhere with the correct recording and classification of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009; and
Prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the overstatement of the Accounts Receivable of the General Fund and Accounts Payable of the LEE by ₱8.144 million, and the understatement of the Due from Other Funds of the General Fund and Due to Other Funds of the LEE by ₱9.028 million.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to reconcile the variance of ₱3,680,141.10 in the Inventory balance of the RPCI and the Statement of Financial Position; and
The Inventory Committee to submit the RPCI to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
The Municipal Assessor’s Office to update their record of parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government and furnish the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer of the supporting documents for recording purposes;
The Supply Officer to make corrections in the RPCPPE to reflect the Land actually owned by the Municipality based on the corrected list from the Office of the Municipal Assessor; and
The Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary journal entries to present the correct balance of the Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements in compliance with Section 72 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the understatement of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds by ₱3.785 million, overstatement of the Accounts Payable by ₱4.537 million and the understatement of the Government Equity by ₱0.752 million;
Follow the correct recording and classification of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009; and
Adhere with Items 5.1.10 and 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, on the transfer of current year unspent QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Trust Fund and the retention in the General Fund of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for CO until the projects funded therefore are completed.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to exhaust efforts to retrieve the supporting documents, reconcile the discrepancy of ₱3.508 million between the Due from Other Funds and Due To Other Funds, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances to present reliable data in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the Construction in Progress account of the agency books the disbursements for construction of assets/infrastructure projects during the construction period and record only the asset account upon completion in compliance with Section 50, Chapter 3, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGU.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make corrections on the misclassified accounts totaling ₱1.453 million to reflect the correct cost of the specific asset account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Revert to the General Fund the amount of ₱1.046 million and this shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefore are completed, in accordance with Item 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Prepare the necessary entries for the reversion of the funds amounting to ₱1.046 million to correct the overstatement of the 
Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and the understatement of the Government Equity Account.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Exhaust efforts to identify the accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances and demand immediate liquidation of the ₱0.884 million outstanding cash advance;
Enforce strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 
No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 on the grant and liquidation of cash advances; and
Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of  cash advances to determine long outstanding and dormant receivables and prepare necessary reports together with the supporting documents for possible request for write-off, if warranted, as indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
The Supply Officer to prepare the IIRUP upon inspection that properties are considered unserviceable, and furnish the Accounting Office with a copy of the report; and
The Municipal Accountant to record impairment loss on unserviceable properties in accordance with the IPSAS 17 and 21.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
The designated personnel to make a follow up request for the necessary papers/documents of the donated vehicles from the donors and the LTO, and expedite the transfer of ownership of these vehicles in the name of the Municipality; and
The Municipal Accountant to establish the value of the donated vehicles based on the supporting documents gathered and record the donated PPE in the books of accounts in accordance with IPSAS 23.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Inventory Committee to fast-track the completion of the one-time cleansing of PPE following the procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to fairly present the PPE balances in the financial statements;
Designate a full-time General Services Officer (GSO) who will be responsible for the preservation, maintenance, and safekeeping of the PPEs of the LGU; and
Pending the cleansing of the PPE account, require the municipal accountant and the GSO to maintain separate ledger and property cards and PAR for acquisitions of PPE in C.Y. 2024 onwards, conduct regular reconciliation of records and physical count.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw correcting entries treating the errors on the transfer of unspent prior year LDRRMF to the Trust Fund; and
For the succeeding years, enjoin the Municipal Accountant to timely transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund account.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Engineer (ME) to prepare the Program of Works (POW) on infrastructure programs, prior to the approval of the budget for 20% Development Fund, to improve implementation efficiency or at least consult the Office of the Municipal Engineer on the doability of the proposed projects during budget deliberations.

We recommended that management:
Replenish the 20% DF account from the General Fund the amount of P351,680.00 improperly utilized for laptops and furniture and incorporate the amount in financing Priority Development Projects of the LGU; and
Enjoin the Municipal Engineer to stop including disallowable items in the Program of Works (POW).

We recommended that management:
Require the JO workers to quantify daily accomplishments in the reports; and
Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to evaluate the delegated tasks to job order workers based on their accomplishments and assess their effectiveness and efficiency to determine reasonableness of their service.

We recommended that management enjoin the Local School Board to incorporate ECCD Programs in the programs, projects and activities appropriated for Special Education Fund pursuant to Section 7(b) of RA No. 10410 to ensure full attainment of the purpose of the fund.

We recommended that management direct the GAD Focal Point System to attach the documents required in paragraph 5 of Section 4.C.8 of JMC 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the GAD PPAs implemented during the year.
