observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require  the concerned Officials to a) be        informed of the reportorial and       monitoring requirement as mentioned   in COA Circular No. 2020-009, in      relation to COA Circular No. 2014-002;  b) prepare and submit said required    reports to the Accounting Office for    recording in the books and for         disclosure in the Notes to the FS, and  to the Audit Team for further          verification; c) strategize the receipt  and distribution of donations-in-kind   identifying the Officers who will be    responsible in the preparation of      acknowledgement receipts and in the   distribution thereof; and d) perform      inventory taking of remaining          undistributed/unconsummated donated   goods for them to be accounted,       valued and recorded in the registries    and to be reported in the FS.

We reiterated the prior year’s audit  recommendation that the LCE direct   the: Municipal Engineering Office to   ensure the completion of the remaining  projects under the 20% DF including   those prior years’ unimplemented      projects so that expected outputs and   outcomes will be realized in the coming  fiscal year; MPDO to i) design fallback/  contingency plans for programs and      activities which cannot be implemented  eventually; and ii) conduct adequate    monitoring of projects so as to ensure  timely bidding of projects and         implementation thereof, thus,         improving the DF utilization and       preventing the accumulation of         unobligated appropriations from Local  DF.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2021 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct    the – BAC, BAC Secretariat and       Accounting Office to submit the lacking  documents to the Audit Team and       observe compliance to the provisions   of GPPB Circular on emergency         procurement; GSO, MDRRMO, MSWDO    and Accounting Office to comply with   the provisions of COA Circular No.     2014-002 and the LGAM on the         management of relief goods; and MSWDO  to submit the complete details of RDS to  the Audit Team for its evaluation and   further audit actions.

We recommended that the LCE instruct  the MHRMO and Accounting Offices to  submit necessary documents as          enumerated under paragraph 9.6 above   to the Audit Team for its review and     evaluation.

We recommended that the LCE a) direct   the MSWDO i) to review the profile of  the 46 individuals whose names         appeared as beneficiaries of two       financial assistance programs and assist  in effecting refund should it be found    out that double assistance was indeed   extended to them; and ii) reconcile the  number of beneficiaries appearing in     the MSWDO and DSWD records; b)       instruct the Treasury Office and MSWDO  to coordinate with the Audit Team for   the purpose of clearing out the five      negative replies received from five     respondents who denied receiving ESP-  SAP assistance.

We recommended that the LCE instruct  the BAC and its Secretariat and the      Accounting and Engineering Offices to   comply with the provisions of the       revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA    Circular No. 2012-001.  Moreover, we   recommended that said Offices         immediately submit the a) ABCs for the  completed three projects; b) certified    copies of minutes of meetings/         conferences (Pre-procurement, Pre-    bid, Opening of Bids), invitation to      observers, BAC resolutions and BAC     Secretariat certification on posting of    ITBs; c) explanation why representative  from COA was invited only in the        opening of bids; d) contractors’ logbook  and as-built plan; e) documents relative  to the delayed awarding of contracts; f)  Performance Security for the project in  Brgy. Sto. Niño; and g) Result of Test     Analysis for the project in Brgy. Pando.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:  MDRRMO to prepare LDRRMIP in         accordance with COA Circular No.      2012-02; BAC, BAC Secretariat and     Accounting Office to i) submit the      lacking documents and/or explanations   for the issues indicated in paragraphs   12.11, 12.16, 12.17 and 12.21; and ii)    adhere with the GPPB Circular 2020-01  concerning emergency procurement of    goods and the revised IRR of R.A. No.  9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 on  the conduct of public bidding; Office who  was assigned to distribute the COVID-19  related items to submit list of recipients  with important details such as the      actual quantity received, signature of   the recipients, date received and         Office/location of recipients for the    proper evaluation of the related         disbursements; Concerned Office in the  preparation of PRs, POs and AIRs to     indicate description of items for the     proper evaluation of procurements; and   Municipal Accountant to check and       ensure the completeness of documents    before effecting payment.

We reiterated the audit recommendations that the a) LCE, together with the MPDO, execute a well-planned programs and activities based on the annual budget of the Municipality, as much as possible; and b) LCE and members of the LFC to strike a balance in maintaining cash surplus without doing away with the implementation of projects that are centered on the delivery of essential public goods and services.

We recommended that the Management: strictly observe the provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the use of alternative methods of procurement if public bidding is not feasible; through the Accounting Office, submit required supporting documents to ascertain the propriety of the disbursements of fund; submit to the Audit Team the documents such as monitoring reports, certificates of completion and pictures which are significant for the proper evaluation of the project; and moving forward, consider adding stipulation on the responsibilities of project beneficiaries to define accountability and ensure sustainability of the program.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC and its Secretariat and the Accounting Office to comply with the provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  Moreover, we recommended that said Offices immediately submit the a) certified copies of minutes of meetings/conferences (Pre-procurement, Pre-bid, Opening of Bids), invitation to observers, BAC resolutions and BAC Secretariat certification on posting of ITBs; and b) explanation why representative from COA was invited only in the opening of bids.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal LSB to a) ensure that approval of SEF Budget falls within the indicative timeline prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and b) comply with the required reportorial submissions to appropriate offices.
We recommended and Management agreed that all taxes withheld be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed that HDMF monthly premiums deducted be fully remitted within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:

give priority to the timely submission of the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the management of the deficiencies noted, if any; and

submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers/payrolls with relevant supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: 28.1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the Department Heads/ employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2015 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/ offices and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.
observations.reason: AOM was issued on this matter.

observations.recommendation: 28.2. We also recommended that henceforth, the LGU of Concepcion, shall within sixty (60) days from their receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.
observations.reason: AOM was issued on this matter.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Management a) initiate the creation of Inventory Committee and conduct a complete annual physical count of Inventories to come up with RPCI with its related reconciliation as required in Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs; and b) maintain SLCs and SCs and observe periodic reconciliation of the two records for proper monitoring of agency assets.

We recommended that the Management a) ensure that GSO is functioning in accordance with the existing laws and regulations; b) provide stockroom for the GSO for it to function properly; and c) observe proper segregation of responsibilities and refrain from assigning incompatible functions to only one person.

We reiterated the audit recommendations that a) the LCE initiate the creation of Inventory Committee; and b) the Inventory Committee i) conduct a complete annual physical count of PPEs; ii) come up with RPCPPE and reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting records.
We further recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Office to a) reconcile its records with the Assessor and Treasurer Offices regarding the parcels of land the Municipality owns for proper recognition and/or reporting; and b) trace transactions relative to lot procurement and record necessary adjustments in the books accounts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct Offices concerned with the operations of the Slaughterhouse and Cemetery to a) coordinate with the Accounting Office, especially on the needed documents, for the proper evaluation of operations and financial reports of the said economic enterprises; and to b) furnish the Audit Team copy of lease contracts for slaughterhouse operation and sales contract for cemetery operations.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned Officials to a) be informed of the reportorial and monitoring requirement as mentioned in COA Circular No. 2020-009, in relation to COA Circular No. 2014-002; prepare and submit said required reports to the Accounting Office for recording in the books and for disclosure in the Notes to the FS, and to the Audit Team for further verification; c) strategize the receipt and distribution of donations-in-kind identifying the Officers who will be responsible in the preparation of acknowledgement receipts and in the distribution thereof; and d) perform inventory taking of remaining undistributed/unconsummated donated goods for them to be accounted, valued and recorded in the registries and to be reported in the FS.

We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendation that the LCE direct the:
Municipal Engineering Office to ensure the completion of the remaining projects under the 20% DF including those prior years’ unimplemented projects so that expected outputs and outcomes will be realized in the coming fiscal year;
Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to i) design fallback/contingency plans for programs and activities which cannot be implemented eventually; and ii) conduct adequate monitoring of projects so as to ensure timely bidding of projects and implementation thereof, thus, improving the DF utilization and preventing the accumulation of unobligated appropriations from Local DF.
We further recommended that the a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat/concerned officials submit copies of contracts entered into by the Municipality and/or issued POs within five days from execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular No. 2009-01; b) BAC, BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office submit the lacking documents and explanations for the other issues noted c) Municipal Accountant check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment; and d) Municipal Engineering Office monitor strictly the implementation of infrastructure projects in order to protect the interests of the government by monitoring contractor compliance with rules and regulations on construction safety.

We commended the effort of the Municipality in complying almost all the requirements on the utilization of the BGCM fund. However, we still recommended that the LCE direct the:
Accounting Office to create a special account for BGCM funds in accordance with the Municipal Ordinance and prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers and registries for the special account pursuant to Section 161, Volume I of the LGAM;
LFC to i) inquire from the DBM or the Bureau of Treasury (BTr) on the proper disposition of unspent BGCM fund for the guidance of the LGU Treasurer and Accountant; and ii) update its Report on Fund Utilization for the BGCM to reconcile with the report prepared by the Accounting Office; and
BAC and its Secretariat to i) submit the lacking documents per GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and/or explanations on the noted issues in the BGCM fund utilization; and ii) faithfully adhere to GPPB Circulars and other relevant laws, rules and regulations specifically in the emergency procurement of goods.

We recommended that the LCE direct the -
BAC, BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance to the provisions of GPPB Circular on emergency procurement;
GSO, MDRRMO, MSWDO and Accounting Office to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and the LGAM on the management of relief goods; and
MSWDO to submit the complete details of RDS to the Audit Team for its evaluation and further audit actions
We recommended that the LCE instruct the MHRMO and Accounting Offices to submit necessary documents as enumerated under paragraph 9.6 above to the Audit Team for its review and evaluation.

We recommended that the LCE a) direct the MSWDO i) to review the profile of the 46 individuals whose names appeared as beneficiaries of two financial assistance programs and assist in effecting refund should it be found out that double assistance was indeed extended to them; and ii) reconcile the number of beneficiaries appearing in the MSWDO and DSWD records; b) instruct the Treasury Office and MSWDO to coordinate with the Audit Team for the purpose of clearing out the five negative replies received from five respondents who denied receiving ESP-SAP assistance.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC and its Secretariat and the Accounting and Engineering Offices to comply with the provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  Moreover, we recommended that said Offices immediately submit the a) ABCs for the completed three projects; b) certified copies of minutes of meetings/conferences (Pre-procurement, Pre-bid, Opening of Bids), invitation to observers, BAC resolutions and BAC Secretariat certification on posting of ITBs; c)  explanation why representative from COA was invited only in the opening of bids; d) contractors’ logbook and as-built plan; e) documents relative to the delayed awarding of contracts; f) Performance Security for the project in Brgy. Sto. Niño; and g) Result of Test Analysis for the project in Brgy. Pando.
We further recommended that the Municipality, henceforth, submit complete set of detailed engineering documents based on Annex A of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and approved bidding documents to support payments of projects; and the BAC Secretariat to completely certify postings of bidding documents performed and submit original copies of documents, otherwise, certify that the submitted documents are photocopies of the original documents.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Office, Budget Officer, MENRO and other concerned Offices to:
exhaust all means to locate documents pertaining to the dormant fund transfers; and
determine whether the purposes can be still achieved.
Likewise, we recommended that in case the purposes of the fund transfers were completed or abandoned, the same Offices should :
evaluate the financial condition of the Municipality and suggest to the LCE whether the Municipality will apply COA Circular No. 2016-005 DILG considering the unutilized funds as at year-end, or it will resort to the JMC 2 of the DBM, DOF and DILG; and
comply with the requirements of the issuance/guidelines proposed to be implemented.
We further recommended that henceforth, the Management furnish the Audit Team with certified copies of agreements with funding source agencies and periodic project status reports for audit purposes.

We reiterated the audit recommendations that the a) LCE, together with the MPDO, execute a well-planned programs and activities based on the annual budget of the Municipality, as much as possible; and b) LCE and members of the LFC to strike a balance in maintaining cash surplus without doing away with the implementation of projects that are centered on the delivery of essential public goods and services. We further recommended that the Municipality should have a list of alternative projects which can be implemented when circumstances prevent the implementation of planned projects.

We recommended that the Management:
strictly observe the provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the use of alternative methods of procurement if public bidding is not feasible.
through the Accounting Office, submit required supporting documents to ascertain the propriety of the disbursements of fund;
submit to the Audit Team the documents such as monitoring reports, certificates of completion and pictures which are significant for the proper evaluation of the project; and
moving forward, consider adding   stipulation on the responsibilities of project beneficiaries to define accountability and ensure sustainability of the program.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC and its Secretariat and the Accounting Office to comply with the provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  Moreover, we recommended that said Offices immediately submit the a) certified copies of minutes of meetings/conferences (Pre-procurement, Pre-bid, Opening of Bids), invitation to observers, BAC resolutions and BAC Secretariat certification on posting of ITBs; and b) explanation why representative from COA was invited only in the opening of bids.
We further recommended that the Municipality, henceforth, submit complete set of approved bidding documents to support payments of procurement and the BAC Secretariat to completely certify postings of bidding documents performed and submit original copies of documents, otherwise, certify that the submitted documents are photocopies of the original documents.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal LSB to a) ensure that approval of SEF Budget falls within the indicative timeline prescribed by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and b) comply with the required reportorial submissions to appropriate offices.
We further recommended that the Accounting Office a) submit immediately the deficient documents pertaining to the noted transactions; and b) secure from the DepEd accountant a certification confirming that all expenses chargeable against the SEF were net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the DepEd budget.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System request trainings/seminars on the use of the subject GAD tools from competent agencies to enhance the gender-responsiveness of the Municipality.
We recommended and Management agreed that all taxes withheld be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed that HDMF monthly premiums deducted be fully remitted within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

1.1.	Create a new Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties and prepare and submit the corresponding inventory report;

1.2.	Require the Inventory Committee, the General Services Office and the Accounting Office to reconcile all the inventory reports for proper presentation in the Financial Statements; and

1.3.	Oblige the General Services Office to maintain complete Property Cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of Annual Inventory Count and in the reconciliation of balances between the Inventory Report and the books of accounts.

We recommended the following:

2.1.	Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to check/verify the difference between the Cashbook and Ledger/FS balances of the Cash Local Treasury account as of December 31, 2019, make necessary adjustments, and henceforth, reconcile their reports monthly; and

2.2.	Management to exert efforts and/or avail of possible legal remedies with regards to the accountability of the previous Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended the following:

3.1.	Local Chief Executive direct all the concerned officials/employees to immediately settle their cash advances within the prescribed period;

3.2.	Municipal Accountant refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance is liquidated, and coordinate with the management of other government agencies to direct the officials/employees previously assigned in the Municipality to immediately settle their outstanding accounts; and

3.3.	Management conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the cash advances, determine long outstanding and dormant receivables and prepare necessary reports together with the supporting documents for the request for write-off, if warranted, as indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

5.1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive with all the members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council:

a)	Include specific and identifiable PPAs for the 20% DF which are attainable within the desired time frame in order to maximize the allocated funds to have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality; and

b)	Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

7.1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and all responsible officials and employees strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4(1) of PD No. 1445 by seeing to it that all disbursements are in pursuance of an appropriation law or other specific statutory authority.

7.2. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop the practice of giving honorarium or extra remuneration that are not supported with legal basis, to personnel who are assigned to perform additional duties and responsibilities.

8.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to release the shares of the barangays from the proceeds of the basic RPT, including the interest thereon, within the prescribed period which is five days after the end of each quarter, pursuant to Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

9.1.	Instruct the concerned officials and employees to communicate with the MLGOO and DILG Regional Office regarding the Copy of the List of Eligible LGUs and the Notice of Eligibility;

9.2.	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation; and

9.3.	Strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2018-01 that prescribe the criteria and conditions for the grant of the PBB for the FY 2018 performance of local government officials and employees.

10.1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with other responsible officials and employees strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-003 and CSC Memorandum Circular No. 01, series of 2001 by seeing to it that awards/token given to officials and employees coincide with the PRAISE objectives.

10.2. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the members of the PRAISE Committee to revisit their LGU PRAISE and update/amend/add some provisions that will correspond to the needs of their officials and employees, if possible.

10.3 We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other officials concerned to stop the practice of giving rings and other tokens to officials and employees and also to the guests without legal basis.

We recommended the following:

11.1.  Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under existing laws, rules and regulations;

11.2.  Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to check/verify the difference between the Cashbook and Ledger/FS balances of the Cash Local Treasury account as of December 31, 2018, make necessary adjustments, and henceforth, reconcile their reports monthly; and

11.3.   Management to exert efforts and/or avail of possible legal remedies with regards to the accountability of the previous Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended the following:

12.1. The Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned employees to gather necessary papers/documents and facilitate the transfer of ownership of the donated vehicles to the Municipality; and

12.2. The OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the donated equipment in the books of account upon completion of the necessary documents to support the Municipality’s ownership of the assets.

13.1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other responsible officials communicate with the owners and settle issues and problems concerning Road Right of Way of the projects, if not yet resolved.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with the other responsible officials and employees:

14.1.   Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under JMC No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization;

14.2.  See to it that projects planned under the 20% Development Fund have specific purpose and indicate in the Annual Investment Plan a brief description of the project, its exact location, target date of implementation and the corresponding budget; and

14.3. Ensure that the projects/programs of other government agencies are among the allowable expenditures under the abovementioned JMC before the Municipality commits the allotted counterpart fund under the 20% DF.

We recommended the following:

15.1.  Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer (1) to collect the necessary market fees and charges from all vendors who occupy space/area in the market; and (2) to see to it that all stall owners pay their rental fees within the first 20 days of the month otherwise, corresponding surcharges and penalties must be imposed and collected;

15.2.   Sangguniang Bayan to consider the need to revise the Market Code 2012 to increase the rates of fees and charges; and

15.3. All concerned officers and employees to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations provided in the Revised Municipal Revenue Code 2017 and Market Code 2012 by collecting corresponding fees and charges, surcharges and penalties from all vendors who do business in the Municipality.

16.1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other concerned officials and employees see to it that all goods/services procured are done in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and supported with complete documentation.

18.1. We recommended that the Management appropriate funds and the Municipal Assessor’s Office be directed to exert effort in titling all parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government. The Accounting Office to set up and maintain complete accounting records and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to keep on file complete records of real properties.

18.2. We also recommended that the offices concerned conduct an inventory of all the parcels of land owned by the Municipality and reconcile with the records in the books for proper presentation in the Financial Statements.

19.1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 1% of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of the LCPC for the promotion of children’s welfare and protection in the locality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person and other responsible officials to conduct gender analysis and assessment in identifying gender issues and appropriate programs, projects and activities (PPAs) and strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA
21.1. We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of JMC No. 2017-3 in order to timely provide the basic services to the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:

22.1. Intensify the tax collection campaign through issuance of follow-up letters/notices of tax collection to delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their tax obligations;

22.2. Coordinate/make representations with the Punong Barangays and seek their assistance to personally deliver the above notices/letters to concerned individuals in the locality and advise them to settle their accounts; and

22.3.  Seek advice of the Provincial Treasurer’s Office regarding applicable remedies on the Comprehensive Agrarian Reform Program (CARP) covered properties.

We recommended the following:

24.1. The LEE to refund the amount of Fund Transfers to the General Fund of the Municipality.

24.2. The Municipality to consider the action/s to be taken as enumerated in Section 3.4 of LBC No. 100; and

24.3. The Municipality to conduct feasibility study to determine whether the operation of the LEE shall be discontinued and consider the reintegration of its functions and services into the regular LGU operations.

25.1. We recommended that the Municipality observe the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and Section 1 of LBC No. 98 by appropriating and expending personal services within the prescribed 45% limit.

27.1. We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials strictly adhere to the rules and regulations of RA No. 5447, Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and Section 3 of Executive Order No. 356, s. 2004 on the utilization of the Special Education Fund. 

We further recommended that the Local School Board prioritize programs, projects and activities as stipulated in the aforementioned provisions in the preparation of the Annual School Board Budget for the SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the Department Heads/ employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2015 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/ offices and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.

We also recommended that henceforth, the LGU of Concepcion, shall within sixty (60) days from their receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

29.1. We recommended that fiscal responsibility in all financial transactions of the Municipality shall be exercised by the concerned officers and employees by ensuring that sufficient/relevant documents are attached to the DVs to show that expenses are for public purpose and that the claims are valid.

30.1. We recommended that the Municipality include in the Report of PPAs, the list of all programs and activities with ongoing implementation and those to be implemented during the year, and diligently adhere to the provisions of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

31.1. We recommended that the financial assistance extended to all the officials and employees, including casuals, contractual and job order workers, of the Municipal Government of Concepcion be refunded and the granting of any allowances, incentives or benefits not authorized by any laws or statutory provision be stopped. Moreover, we recommended that expenditures be classified and charged in accordance with the nature and character of the expense.

32.1. We recommended that the accountant verify the completeness and legitimacy of the documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 before certifying in the disbursement vouchers. We further recommended that the variance between the AIR and AR/PAR be refunded

33.1. We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO to strictly adhere with the provisions of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and must submit regularly to the COA Audit Team the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of the MDRRMF to ascertain the correctness of its utilization.

35.1. We recommended that the LCE require the Human Resource Management Officer, Department Heads and Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA, pertaining to net take home pay in order to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough support for their daily needs until the next pay day.
36.1. We recommended that management cause the refund of the excess of the 2013 PEI in the total amount of ₱1,106,000.00.

37.1. We recommended strict adherence to the provision of Section 9.1 and 9.2 of Budget Circular No. 2012-3. The amount of ₱1,856,000.00, being unauthorized should be refunded by the municipal officials and employees who were recipients of the benefits.

38.1. We recommended that the amounts received by the former Secretary to the Sangguniang Bayan and Municipal Treasurer since the time they were relieved from their original functions shall be refunded to the Municipality. Thus, the former SB Secretary shall refund the RATA he received from March 1 to December 31, 2011 for a total of ₱88,000.00 while the former Municipal Treasurer shall refund a total of ₱96,800.00  representing the RATA she received since February 1 to December 31, 2011.

We have recommended that management:
Instruct the Supply Officer to prepare and submit a complete inventory report to account the undistributed relief goods;
Direct the concerned Offices to validate the completeness of the Distribution Lists and submit the same to the audit team.  Any discrepancy should be satisfactorily justified by the Management; and
Establish clear procedures or flow of the relief operations including proper authorization and ensure that these are properly disseminated to avoid confusion or any misunderstanding in the procurement and distribution of relief goods.

We have recommended that management instruct the MDRRMO thru coordination with concerned municipal officials to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed by the municipality in accordance with Section II-A.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-009.

We have recommended that management:
Organize a validation team who will work in close coordination with barangay officials concerned and the DSWD to conduct verification on the eligibility of the aforementioned beneficiaries based on the filled-up SAC;
Direct the validation team to submit to the audit team a report/status of their verification and the actions taken by management; and
Confer with DSWD-Regional Office No. 10 on the results of the revalidation and obtain a written advise as to the course of action if recipients were declared to be ineligible.

We have recommended that management direct all concerned officials to properly and completely accomplish relevant procurement documents such as PR, RFQ and PO and provide the necessary and relevant specifications of the items to be procured in clear and unambiguous terms for all future procurement projects to serve as reference during inspection and acceptance of deliveries by management.

We have recommended that management require the BAC to observe strict compliance on the mandatory posting of the procurement data for all contracts awarded under Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.

We have recommended that management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly:
Indicate control/reference numbers in the RCDs including the date of receipt of remittance from the Liquidating Officer;
Record each transaction in the cashbook meticulously according to the dates of actual collections and deposits; and
Ensure that all transactions are completely and correctly recorded in the cashbook.
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile their records to avoid further accumulation of errors/reconciling items.  Thereafter, observe the necessary monthly reconciliation for early detection of errors and/or omissions of transactions.

We have recommended that management:
Install and strictly observe internal controls over cash collections to adequately safeguard government funds and records from possible misuse and losses; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the collections and remittances of every collector/liquidating officer including checking their records such as Cashbooks and RCDs.

We have recommended that management:
Conduct actual physical inventory-taking of the properties and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE);
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets, and Real Property Ledger Cards for land; and
Require them further to conduct periodic reconciliation of the ledger card balances in order to achieve accuracy and fair presentation of the financial statements.

We have recommended that management:
Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchases of drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies as inventory;
Require the RHU Inventory In-Charge to submit the monthly report of issuances to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses for inventories consumed or distributed; and
Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis.

We have recommended that management direct the LDRRMO to include and implement in the LDRRM Investment Plan the deficiencies noted relative to compliance on the minimum standards for disaster preparedness. Likewise, we enjoined collaborative discussion thereon with the Sangguniang Bayan on the implementation and augmentation of the LDRRM Fund, if need be.

We have recommended that management:
1.	Refrain from resorting to procurement and disbursements through reimbursement procedure. All payments should pass through regular mode of procurement and disbursement procedure in accordance with the relevant provisions of R.A. 9184 and COA Circular No. 97-002; and
2.	Comply with the requirement of adequate procurement planning in 	accordance with Section 7 of R.A. 9184.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:

direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer/ Inventory Committee to initiate the conduct of the complete physical count of all the inventories and property, plant and equipment of the Municipality at year-end to determine the validity and reliability of existence of its inventories and properties.  The committee shall reconcile the results of the count with the property records and accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.  Coordinated efforts of the employees should be encouraged to be able to submit the required reports; and

direct the Municipal Accountant to effect transfer of completed projects for LGU use from the Trust Fund to the General Fund and prepare the necessary lapsing schedule to support the depreciation expense so as to ensure the accuracy of the amount provided and to monitor the correct balance of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction:
An Inventory Committee has already been created under Executive Order No. 003 s. 2019 dated July 3, 2019 issued by the Municipal Mayor.  The committee also sought the professional expertise of the Municipal Accountant in the conduct of the inventory count for CY 2021 and in the preparation of the corresponding inventory reports.

The lapsing schedule for depreciation of PPE accounts for CY 2020 was also submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee failed to conduct the physical inventory of properties as at December 31, 2020, thus the RPCI and RPCPPE were not prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has already accounted a total of P477,058.51 under the Trust Fund. Necessary adjustments for these transactions were yet to be made.
observations.reason: The unreconciled difference between the books and BRS remained at P8,789,694.25 as at December 31, 2020.  The Accounting Office has yet to look for relevant documents to properly determine the cause of these discrepancies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:

require the strict and uniform implementation of the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to all officials and employees of the Municipality;

require all accountable officers and employees to settle/liquidate all their outstanding cash advances by sending confirmation, notification or demand letters.  For unliquidated cash advances which are non-moving for more than three years, exhaust all remedies to collect or demand to liquidate the unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, apply for write off in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 29, 2016; and

instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly classify each cash advance transaction in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction: Advances for officers and employees totaling P169,037.14 has already been liquidated as at December 31, 2020.
observations.reason: Cash advances for officers and employees and operating expenses amounting toP152,775.89 and P276,826.65, respectively, remained unsettled as at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require:

proper coordination between the Accounting Office and the GAD FPS-TWG in the preparation of reports relative to the implementation of the GAD PPAs to ensure correctness of the information provided therein before submission to oversight agencies and other concerned offices; and

the MSWDO and the Municipal Accountant to submit written explanation/justification and/or the required lacking documents to prove compliance on the aforementioned provisions to prove the regularity and legality of the subject disbursements.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, comparison of actual costs/ expenditures reported in GAD Accomplishment Report and accounting records disclosed only a very minimal discrepancy.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, disbursements relative to the implementation of the GAD related PPAs were still not supported with complete documentation.

observations.managementAction: Most of the documents noted to be lacking were already complied with reference to the current year’s transactions, denoting that the Municipal Accountant is more circumspect in checking completeness of supporting documents before processing claims.
observations.reason: The BAC and its Secretariat and TWG lack trainings and updates on the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and other GPPB issuances thus, various procedural lapses were still incurred in the procurement of infrastructure projects for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Accountant (i) submit the required lacking documents to prove compliance with the aforementioned provisions to prove the regularity and legality of the corresponding disbursements; and (i) observe strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure adherence to the principles of transparency, competitiveness and public monitoring.
observations.managementAction: Most of the documents noted to be lacking were already complied with reference to the current year’s transactions, denoting that the Municipal Accountant is more circumspect in checking completeness of supporting documents before processing claims.
observations.reason: The BAC and its Secretariat and TWG lack trainings and updates on the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and other GPPB issuances thus, various procedural lapses were still incurred in the procurement of goods for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the above observations, we recommended and the management agreed that the:

Municipal Mayor thru the MPDC and LDRRMO (i) prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format prescribed in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; (ii) properly plan the programs, projects and activities to be undertaken, fast track their implementation and optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the Municipality in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters; 

Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to submit written explanation/justification and/or the required lacking documents to prove compliance on the aforementioned provisions to prove the regularity and legality of the subject disbursements; and

Municipal Accountant adhere strictly to the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRM Fund set forth under Items 5.1.10 and 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002 on the transfer of unutilized LDRRMF and proper disclosure thereof in the Municipality’s financial statements, respectively.
observations.managementAction:
The details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF were disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements for CY 2020.

The unexpended/ unobligated balance of QRF and DRRM - MOOE were already transferred to the Special Trust Fund.
observations.reason:
The specific period of implementation of the identified PPAs under the LDRRMF were still not specified in the LDRRMFIP.

Disbursements made out of the LDRRM Fund for CY 2020 were still not supported with complete documents. 

Discrepancies were also noted in the amounts presented in the Notes to Financial Statements and accounting records.

observations.managementAction:
The majority of the payables relative to this observation have been settled as early as the 1stquarter of 2020 supported with complete documentation.

For CY 2020, the LGU has already refrained from recording blind obligations and ensured that recorded payables as at year-end were valid obligations of the Municipality.
For CY 2020, the LGU has submitted and complied with some of the lacking documents and deficiencies noted in the payments made to contractors for implementation of Infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer still failed to submit the documents supporting the grant of time extension to contractors.

observations.managementAction:
PPAs identified to be funded under the 20% DF partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

Explanation/
justification to prove the regularity and legality of the hauling and freight expenses incurred was submitted.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, only one out of five PPAs duly appropriated under the 20% Development Fund (DF) was completed. Moreover, nine PPAs with total appropriation of P9,874,873.07 charged against the 20% DF for CYs 2018 and 2019 are still on-going. Delays in the implementation of these projects were also due to the restrictions imposed in view of the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the provisions given in allowing and disallowing the travel expenses claimed by the officials and employees of the Municipality and see to it that disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents before processing the claims.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, reimbursements of actual expenses was duly supported with certification from the Municipal Mayor as necessary in performance of the travel order.
observations.reason: The incidental expenses of traveling from temporary residence to the actual place of seminar/activities/ assignment and vice versa of several employees were reimbursed separately, in excess of the allowable travel allowance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirement of the perpetual inventory system wherein regular purchases shall be coursed thru the inventory account and issuances thereof shall be recorded as they take place except those purchased out of petty cash fund which shall be for immediate use and not for stock. Such case shall be charged immediately to the appropriate expense accounts. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer regularly prepare and submit accurate and complete Summary of Supplies and Materials Issue (SSMI) with supporting Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) pursuant to Section 121 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to the Accounting Unit for proper recording/posting of issuance in the books of accounts and Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs).

On the other hand, we recommended the Municipal Accountant prepare JEVs to adjust/record issuances on the affected accounts and coordinate closely with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure reconciliation of their inventory records, also in respect of physical inventory taking.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, the Municipality has recorded the purchased Fuel, Oil and Lubricants and Construction Materials in their respective inventory accounts.
observations.reason: The purchased food supplies for COVID-19 patients and affected households amounting to P480,278.00 were directly recorded under food supplies expenses account instead of Welfare Goods for Distribution account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the balances per books with those per bank, determine the cause of the discrepancy and prepare necessary adjustments in the books for all errors committed in order to reflect the adjusted balance and give a reliable data of Cash in Bank balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to identify the discrepancies under the PhilHealth Capitation Fund account and Calamity Trust Fund account amounting to P14,540.86 and P462,517.65 and adjusting entries for both accounts will be made in CY 2021.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to account for the discrepancies noted under the General, Special Education and Trust Funds totaling P4,762,987.77, P201,132.32, and P4,302,632.67 respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the notes to financial statements and submit general ledger and subsidiary ledgers for all funds in accordance with the abovementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: The General and Subsidiary Ledgers were already submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still failed to properly disclose relevant information and details of accounts in the Notes to FS for CY 2020.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipality has secured its approved Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan for the period (2015-2024) from the National Solid Waste Management Commission on June 30, 2015 as per NSWMC Resolution No. 150, Series of 2015.

Executive Order No. 51 series of 2019 entitled Reorganizing the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) of the Municipality of Concepcion, Romblon was issued.

Executive Order No. 38 series of 2020 entitled An Executive Order Designating Focal Person on Solid Waste Management was also issued.

All nine barangays of the Municipality have already established their MRF.

The Municipality is currently operating a controlled dumpsite instead of an open dumpsite.
observations.reason:
Although some Barangays of the Municipality supported the Solid Waste Management Program by reconstructing their MRFs, some of the provisions of the Act were not properly observed by the concerned officials.

Also, garbage collectors are not yet equipped with proper training on handling/dealing with garbage.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Management adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 10121 and see to it that programs and projects of the Municipality identified in the DRRM Plan were fully implemented in order to strengthen the preparedness and readiness of the municipality in case of calamity.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the transfer of unexpended balance of LDRRMF to Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the special trust fund was duly appropriated and incorporated in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2020 and was submitted to the Audit Team.

The unexpended/ unobligated balance of QRF and DRRM - MOOE for CY 2020 were transferred to Special Trust fund as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to abide with the regulations of PCW-DILG DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 in submission of the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of recording transactions charged to the GAD Fund and that the GAD Focal Point Person coordinate with the Accounting Department and reconcile the draft accomplishment report with accounting records before it is finalized for submission to offices concerned.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer to discontinue the practice of including MOOE as part of the Continuing Appropriations.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2020 was duly approved by the DILG Regional Office.

For CY 2020, the comparison of actual costs/ expenditures reported in GAD Accomplish ment Report and accounting records disclosed only a minimal discrepancy.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the Municipal Accountant to set up RPT/SET Receivables in accordance with Section 20 of NGAS Manual, Volume I. Analyze the unreconciled discrepancies in the RPT and SET receivables and take up the correction of the noted differences to present accurate and reliable data in the Financial Statements.

the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a duly certified list of Real Property Taxpayers showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and

the Municipal Assessor to furnish the Treasurer with the latest valuations of property of each taxpayer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

1.1.	Create a new Inventory Committee to conduct the complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties and prepare and submit the corresponding inventory report;

We recommended the following:

2.1.	Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to check/verify the difference between the Cashbook and Ledger/FS balances of the Cash Local Treasury account as of December 31, 2019, make necessary adjustments, and henceforth, reconcile their reports monthly; and
2.2.	Management to exert efforts and/or avail of possible legal remedies with regards to the accountability of the previous Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended the following:

3.1.	Local Chief Executive direct all the concerned officials/employees to immediately settle their cash advances within the prescribed period;

3.2.	Municipal Accountant refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance is liquidated, and coordinate with the management of other government agencies to direct the officials/employees previously assigned in the Municipality to immediately settle their outstanding accounts; and

3.3.	Management conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the cash advances, determine long outstanding and dormant receivables and prepare necessary reports together with the supporting documents for the request for write-off, if warranted, as indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: The management could not request for write-off of the prior years’ balance amounting to ₱884,107.89 since no supporting documents or any report that could show proof of the transactions and the persons accountable.

observations.recommendation: 4.2.	Stop using LDRRM Fund in procuring equipment which are not among those enumerated in the said JMC and in the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73;

4.3.	Instruct the members of the Inspectorate Team to see to it that item/s delivered are personally checked/inspected by them and that all specifications written in the documents are present and the same as the one’s purchased and delivered by the supplier; and

4.4.	Instruct the MDRRM staff and responders not to lend/use the equipment in any other activity and to take good care those equipment/supplies so that it will be readily available in case of calamity/emergency.

5.1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive with all the members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council:

a)	Include specific and identifiable PPAs for the 20% DF which are attainable within the desired time frame in order to maximize the allocated funds to have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality; and

b)	Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management provide legal basis citing that the said payment of honoraria to the MBOC shall be sourced from the LGU fund and submit a certificate of non-payment of honoraria from the COMELEC Central Office Accountant.  Otherwise, bill the COMELEC for the total election expenses incurred during the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections in accordance with Section 10 of the Omnibus Election Code.

Revised recommendation:
Pursuant to COMELEC Minute Resolution No. 19-1054, submit  justification that the honoraria granted to the Municipal Board of Canvassers during the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections is not similar to the honorarium or allowance given by the COMELEC, otherwise the payment shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: 2019 AAR Finding No. 8 | The LGU submitted COMELEC Minute Resolution No. 07-0626 as basis to support the claim.
observations.reason:
COMELEC Minute Resolution No. 07-0626 dated March 27, 2007 is superseded by COMELEC Minute Resolution No. 19-1054 dated October 30, 2019, which resolved that the Sangguniang Bayan, Sangguniang Panlungsod,  Sangguniang Panlalawigan of municipalities, cities and provinces, respectively:
May continue to grant an honorarium or allowance to the members of the municipal/city/provincial board of canvassers for the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections and all subsequent electoral exercises; such honorarium or allowance must not be similar to the honorarium or allowance being given by this Commission; and
May provide in their respective ordinances that of the recipient is already receiving a similar honorarium or allowance for the same service or period, respectively, for which payment is being claimed, the recipient may choose to  receive whichever is higher, but in no case must the recipient receive both amounts.
Hence, the audit team revised the audit recommendation.
