11.2. We also recommended that the offices concerned conduct an inventory of all the parcels of land owned by the Municipality and reconcile with the records in the books for proper presentation in the Financial Statements.

12.1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 1% of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of the LCPC for the promotion of children’s welfare and protection in the locality.
14.1. We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of JMC No. 2017-3 in order to timely provide the basic services to the constituents of the Municipality.

15.2. Coordinate/make representations with the Punong Barangays and seek their assistance to personally deliver the above notices/letters to concerned individuals in the locality and advise them to settle their accounts; and
15.3.  Seek advice of the Provincial Treasurer’s Office regarding applicable remedies on the Comprehensive Agrarian Reform Program (CARP) covered properties.

We recommended the following:

17.1. The LEE to refund the amount of Fund Transfers to the General Fund of the Municipality.
17.2. The Municipality to consider the action/s to be taken as enumerated in Section 3.4 of LBC No. 100; and

17.3. The Municipality to conduct feasibility study to determine whether the operation of the LEE shall be discontinued and consider the reintegration of its functions and services into the regular LGU operations.
19.4.Provide all other requirements of the aforementioned Circular

26.1. We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO to strictly adhere with the provisions of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and must submit regularly to the COA Audit Team the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of the MDRRMF to ascertain the correctness of its utilization.
27.2.  Designate an LGU employee to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption;

31.1.We recommended that the amounts received by the former Secretary to the Sangguniang Bayan and Municipal Treasurer since the time they were relieved from their original functions shall be refunded to the Municipality. Thus, the former SB Secretary shall refund the RATA he received from March 1 to December 31, 2011 for a total of ₱88,000.00 while the former Municipal Treasurer shall refund a total of ₱96,800.00  representing the RATA she received since February 1 to December 31, 2011.

We have reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management direct the LDRRMO to include and implement in the LDRRM Investment Plan the deficiencies noted relative to compliance on the minimum standards for disaster preparedness. Likewise, we enjoined collaborative discussion thereon with the Sangguniang Bayan on the implementation and augmentation of the LDRRM Fund, if need be.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMO has already formulated Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (MDRRMP) for CY2020-2022 and that the said Plan is already approved by the SB.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modification in CY2019 AAR.

observations.managementAction: No concrete action was undertaken by management.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modification in CY2019 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to (i) initiate the creation of Inventory Committee and conduct a complete annual physical count of PPEs and Inventories to come up with RPCPPE and RPCI with its related reconciliation as required in Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGU; (ii) maintain the Supplies/Property Ledger Cards and Stock/Property Cards, respectively, for all LGU properties; and (iii) observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records for proper monitoring of agency assets.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that (i) all claims against LGU are supported with complete documentation in accordance with the fundamental principles in handling financial transactions; (ii) proper coordination with the Office of the President takes place to bring their records in agreement with the records of the latter’s Finance/Accounting Section; and (iii) plans for the negotiation/condonation of accumulated interest are carried out; otherwise, book up the interest expense arising from the loan.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the (i) Municipal Treasurer to regularly furnish the Accounting Office the duly signed certified list of taxpayers with their tax dues every beginning of the year for the latter’s basis in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables. Also, any updates effected in the records of the Treasury Office must be furnished the Accounting Office to maintain a reconciled record; and (ii) Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records and henceforth, establish a productive collaboration in maintaining and supplying financial records pertaining to tax receivables.

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) allocate budget for insurance policies covering all insurable properties of the Municipality and to secure said policies from the GSIS or other reputable insurance companies so that the agency is protected and indemnified should calamities arise; and (ii) conduct a complete annual physical count of PPEs and Inventories and come up with the RPCPPE and RPCI and accordingly prepare PIF to be submitted to the Audit Team and GSIS to secure insurance.

We recommended and Management agreed for the (a) Municipal Engineering Office to ensure completion of the remaining projects under the 20% development fund including those prior years’ unimplemented projects from its continuing appropriation so that expected outputs and outcomes will be realized in the coming fiscal year; (b) Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to (i) design fallback/contingency plans for programs and activities which were identified as priority so as not to derail/defer their implementation; and (ii) conduct adequate monitoring of projects so as to ensure timely bidding of projects and implementation thereof, thus, improving the LDF utilization and preventing the accumulation of unobligated appropriations from Local Development Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 in order to inform the public on matters of public concern with the end in view of promoting good governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the (i) Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of documents before effecting payment; (ii) Bids and Awards Committee to refrain from resorting to splitting of purchases and strictly adhere to the requirement of R.A. No. 9184 and to adopt control measures that would ensure that procurement is enforced in an efficient and economical manner that is most advantageous to the government; and (iii) responsible officers to submit the approved contracts and purchase orders within five days from execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular 2009-01.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) and other related offices to ensure the proper preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan on LDF by providing more specific schedule of procurement activities based on identified priority projects.

We recommended and Management agreed for the Treasury Office to intensify its tax collections efforts thru formulation of more effective strategies and strongly consider enforcing the legal remedies available to LGUs including the filing of an action for levy on real property or by judicial action against the tax delinquents.

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) direct the Local Finance Committee to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local taxation and continually design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue; and (ii) Municipal Treasurer to constantly monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from taxpayers through deployment of demand letters for delinquent taxpayers and tapping the assistance of the barangays for real property tax collections.

We recommended and Management agreed for the (i) Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee in order to address the specific issues that caused the delay in the bidding process for procurement of goods; (ii) Bids and Awards Committee to submit the lacking documents as enumerated and ensure to send out invitation only to qualified observers of procurement activities; and (iii) Municipal Accountant to warrant the completeness of the attached documents before approving the payment.

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) order the barangay officials of the 45 barangays to immediately establish their respective MRFs pursuant to R.A. No. 9003 so that volume of residual wastes will be minimized and final disposal fees paid by the Municipality, reduced; and (ii) follow up the status of deliberation on the approval of the 10-year SWMP of the LGU and promptly effect the required changes, if any, following the advice of the Commission.

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) instruct the concerned officials to ensure preparation/maintenance of LGSF-AM project status reports based on percentage of their completion; (ii) ensure that all conditions set by the DILG in the implementation of LGSF-AM projects are complied with to remain eligible in receiving subsidy from the Assistance to Municipalities Program in succeeding years; (iii) conduct adequate planning and monitoring of projects so as to ensure their timely implementation from bidding to completion and administer the optimal delivery of benefits from these projects; and (iv) warrant compliance on the timely submission of contracts and its relative documents within five days from the contract execution pursuant to the COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the (i) GAD Focal Point and other concerned local officials guarantee that the DILG Regional Office is informed in writing on the revisions/additions of the GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality in accordance with item 6.0 of PCW-DILG -DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 so as to ensure that all programs and projects are approved and/or verified to be GAD responsive; and (ii) ensure that GAD Plan and Utilization/Accomplishment Report are submitted to the Audit Team within the reglementary period provided by COA Circular 2014-001.

We recommended and Management agreed for the (a) Municipal Local School Board to (i) endeavor an optimal utilization of SEF budget in line with the State’s objectives of supplementing the needs of government schools; and (ii) adapt a strategic approach in identifying programs and projects considering their realistic implementation for a specific duration; and (b) Accounting Office to (i) submit immediately the disbursement vouchers with their supporting documents pertaining to the noted transactions; and (ii) observe the timely submission of financial transactions as prescribed by COA Circular 2009-006.

We recommended and Management agreed for the (i) Local Chief Executive, together with the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO), to execute a well-planned programs and activities based on the annual budget of the Municipality, as much as possible; and (ii) Local Chief Executive and members of the Local Finance Committee to strike a balance in maintaining cash surplus without doing away with the implementation of projects that are centered on the delivery of essential public goods and services.
We recommended and Management agreed that all taxes withheld be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed that HDMF monthly premiums deducted be fully remitted within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We reiterated the  previous  year’s  audit  recommendation  and  the
  management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:


     a.  direct  the  Acting  Municipal  Treasurer/Inventory  Committee   to
        initiate the conduct of the  complete  physical  count  of  all  the
        inventories and property, plant and equipment of the Municipality at
        year-end to determine the validity and reliability of  existence  of
        its inventories and properties.  The committee shall  reconcile  the
        results of the  count  with  the  property  records  and  accounting
        records and submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to  the
        Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for  LGUs,
        Volume I.  Coordinated efforts of the employees should be encouraged
        to be able to submit the required reports; and


     b. direct the Municipal Accountant  to  effect  transfer  of  completed
        projects for LGU use from the Trust Fund to  the  General  Fund  and
        prepare the necessary lapsing schedule to support  the  depreciation
        expense so as to ensure the accuracy of the amount provided  and  to
        monitor the correct balance of PPE accounts.

Based  on  the  foregoing  observations,  we  recommended  and   the
  management  agreed  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the   Municipal
  Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:


     a. exert extra  effort  to  account  for  the  unreconciled  difference
        appearing in the bank statements and  financial  statements  and  to
        faithfully present the correct balances of the accounts;


     b. prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record  and  correct  the
        unrecorded amounts and erroneous entries made in the  Cash  in  Bank
        account, respectively;


     c. revert to proper Cash in Bank and payable accounts all stale checks;


     d. if possible, coordinate with the bank to  determine  whether  checks
        claimed by the payees were not yet presented  for  payment.   Secure
        the addresses of the payees for notification of the return of  stale
        checks in their possession for replacement;


     e. for checks which have been stale for many years and that  sufficient
        documents show  that  obligations  no  longer  exist,  consider  the
        reversion of these payables to the  unappropriated  surplus  of  the
        General Fund; and


     f.   henceforth, send written notice to the payees one month before the
        check becomes stale.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:


     a. require the strict and uniform implementation of the  provisions  of
        COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of
        cash advances to all officials and employees of the Municipality;


     b. require all accountable officers and employees  to  settle/liquidate
        all  their  outstanding  cash  advances  by  sending   confirmation,
        notification or demand  letters.   For  unliquidated  cash  advances
        which are non-moving for more than three years, exhaust all remedies
        to collect or demand to liquidate the  unliquidated  cash  advances,
        otherwise, apply for write off in accordance with the guidelines and
        procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December
        29, 2016; and


     c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to  properly  classify  each  cash
        advance transaction in accordance with  COA  Circular  No.  2015-009
        dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require:


     a. proper coordination between the Accounting Office and the  GAD  FPS-
        TWG in the preparation of reports relative to the implementation  of
        the GAD PPAs to  ensure  correctness  of  the  information  provided
        therein before submission to oversight agencies and other  concerned
        offices; and


     b.  the  MSWDO  and  the  Municipal  Accountant   to   submit   written
        explanation/justification and/or the required lacking  documents  to
        prove compliance on  the  aforementioned  provisions  to  prove  the
        regularity and legality of the subject disbursements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to  instruct  the  BAC
  to:


     a. furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Contract of Agreement  and
        its attachments within five working days from the execution  thereof
        as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No.  2009-001  dated
        February 23, 2009;


     b. observe strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A.  No.
        9184  to  ensure  adherence  to  the  principles  of   transparency,
        competitiveness and public monitoring;


     c. submit the lacking documents cited above to the Audit Team to comply
        with the requirements of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No.  9184  and
        address the deficiencies enumerated; and


     d. prepare the required reports/documents  with  extra  care  to  avoid
        errors which could mislead readers and users of the reports.

In view of the above observations, we recommended and the management
  agreed that the:


     a. Municipal Mayor thru the MPDC and LDRRMO (i) prepare the LDRRMFIP in
        accordance with the format prescribed in Annex A of COA Circular No.
        2012-002 and include the list of projects and activities charged  to
        the unexpended LDRRMF of previous  years;  (ii)  properly  plan  the
        programs, projects and activities to be undertaken, fast track their
        implementation and optimize the utilization of  the  LDRRMF  of  the
        Municipality in order to strengthen its  disaster  preparedness  and
        avoid potential damages during disasters;


     b. Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO and the Municipal  Accountant  to
        submit written explanation/justification and/or the required lacking
        documents to prove compliance on the  aforementioned  provisions  to
        prove the regularity and legality of the subject disbursements; and


     c. Municipal Accountant adhere strictly to the accounting and reporting
        guidelines for LDRRM Fund set forth under Items 5.1.10 and 5.1.16 of
        COA Circular 2012-002 on  the  transfer  of  unutilized  LDRRMF  and
        proper  disclosure   thereof   in   the   Municipality’s   financial
        statements, respectively.

We recommended  and  the  Municipal  Mayor  agreed  to  require  the
  Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to:


     a.  Submit  a  satisfactory  explanation  on  the  recording  of  blind
        obligations noted by the Audit Team;


     b. Effect the necessary adjustment/s by reverting to the  general  fund
        the amount obligated as payables without proper documentation;


     c. Ensure that obligations are recorded only  when  goods  are  already
        delivered and services are rendered to the Municipality; and


     d. Record obligations only when they are duly supported with  documents
        to support their validity and existence.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that:


     a.   along with the MPDC and Municipal Engineer,  strictly  follow  the
        provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in the implementation of  PPAs
        funded under the 20% DF.   Closely  monitor  the  implementation  of
        development projects and see to it that priority projects identified
        are promptly implemented in order to deliver  satisfactory  benefits
        to the constituents;


     b.   along with the MPDC,  ensure  that  PPAs  partake  the  nature  of
        investment or capital  expenditures  that  will  contribute  to  the
        attainment of socio-economic development and environmental  outcomes
        of the Municipality;


     c. submit written explanation/justification to prove the regularity and
        legality of the hauling and freight expenses incurred; and


     d.   require the Municipal Accountant to prepare  adjusting  accounting
        entries to correct  the  deficiencies  noted  in  the  recording  of
        transactions.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


    a) Create a new Inventory Committee to conduct  the  complete  physical
       inventory of the Municipality’s properties and  prepare  and  submit
       the corresponding inventory report;


    b) Require the Inventory Committee, the General Services Office and the
       Accounting Office to reconcile all the inventory reports for  proper
       presentation in the Financial Statements; and


    c) Oblige the General Services Office  to  maintain  complete  Property
       Cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct  of  Annual
       Inventory Count and in the reconciliation of  balances  between  the
       Inventory Report and the books of accounts.

Recommendations:


    We recommended the following:

    a)  Local  Chief  Executive  to  direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and
       Municipal Accountant to  check/verify  the  difference  between  the
       Cashbook and Ledger/FS balances of the Cash Local  Treasury  account
       as of December 31, 2019, make necessary adjustments, and henceforth,
       reconcile their reports monthly; and

    b) Management to exert efforts and/or avail of possible legal  remedies
       with  regards  to  the  accountability  of  the  previous  Municipal
       Treasurer.

Recommendations:


    We recommended the following:


    a) Local Chief Executive direct all the  concerned  officials/employees
       to immediately settle their  cash  advances  within  the  prescribed
       period;

    b) Municipal Accountant refrain from granting additional cash  advances
       unless the previous cash advance is liquidated, and coordinate  with
       the  management  of  other  government  agencies   to   direct   the
       officials/employees  previously  assigned  in  the  Municipality  to
       immediately settle their outstanding accounts; and

    c) Management conduct regular and periodic verification,  analysis  and
       validation of the cash  advances,  determine  long  outstanding  and
       dormant receivables and prepare necessary reports together with  the
       supporting documents for the request for write-off, if warranted, as
       indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  together  with  other
    members of the MDRRMC:


    a) Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under the  pertinent  sections
       of JMC No. 2013-1 in the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF
       by not charging the expenses related to the repairs and  maintenance
       of the vehicle and other maintenance and operating expenses  to  the
       said fund;


    b) Stop using LDRRM Fund in procuring equipment  which  are  not  among
       those enumerated in the said JMC and in the DILG Memorandum Circular
       No. 2012-73;


    c) Instruct the members of the Inspectorate Team  to  see  to  it  that
       item/s delivered are personally checked/inspected by them  and  that
       all specifications written in the documents are present and the same
       as the one’s purchased and delivered by the supplier; and


    d) Instruct  the  MDRRM  staff  and  responders  not  to  lend/use  the
       equipment in  any  other  activity  and  to  take  good  care  those
       equipment/supplies so that it will be readily available in  case  of
       calamity/emergency.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive with all the  members  of
    the Municipal Planning and Development Council:


    a) Include specific and identifiable PPAs for  the  20%  DF  which  are
       attainable within the desired time frame in order  to  maximize  the
       allocated funds to have an immediate impact  on  the  socio-economic
       development of the Municipality; and


    b) Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1
       in formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its
       constituents.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the HRMO and other
    responsible officials and employees:


    a) Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular  No.  2009-001  by
       furnishing the COA Office copies of all the contracts in hiring  JOs
       and COS together with its supporting documents within  five  working
       days from execution thereof;


    b) See to it that the duties and responsibilities/scoop of work of each
       JO are stipulated in a specific manner in the contract and make sure
       that all JOs sign in it confirming/agreeing with all the  terms  and
       conditions;


    c) Ensure that all the supporting documents  attached  to  the  DV  are
       properly accomplished and signed by the concerned person; and

    d) Prepare a monthly work/duty scheme that provides a day or  two  rest
       day in a week for each responder/staff  but  ensure  that  there  is
       somebody left to oversee the office and in case all  the  responders
       or those not on duty needs to standby, a  memorandum  from  the  LCE
       requiring them to report for duty  will  form  part  of  their  DTRs
       supporting documents.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  and  all  responsible
    officials and employees strictly adhere to the  provisions  of  Section
    4(1) of PD No. 1445 by seeing to  it  that  all  disbursements  are  in
    pursuance  of  an  appropriation  law  or  other   specific   statutory
    authority.


    We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop the practice of
    giving honorarium or extra remuneration that  are  not  supported  with
    legal basis, to personnel who are assigned to perform additional duties
    and responsibilities.

Recommendations:

    We recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
    Treasurer and  Municipal  Accountant  to  release  the  shares  of  the
    barangays from the proceeds of the basic RPT,  including  the  interest
    thereon, within the prescribed period which is five days after the  end
    of each quarter, pursuant to Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


    a) Instruct the concerned officials and employees to  communicate  with
       the MLGOO and DILG Regional Office regarding the Copy of the List of
       Eligible LGUs and the Notice of Eligibility;


    b)  Instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  see  to   it   that   all
       disbursements are supported with complete documentation; and

    c) Strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2018-01 that  prescribe
       the criteria and conditions for the grant of the  PBB  for the FY
       2018 performance of local government officials and employees.

Recommendations:

    We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  together  with  other
    responsible officials and employees strictly adhere to COA Circular No.
    2012-003 and CSC Memorandum Circular No. 01, series of 2001  by  seeing
    to it that awards/token given to officials and employees coincide  with
    the PRAISE objectives.


    We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the  members  of
    the PRAISE Committee to revisit their LGU PRAISE  and  update/amend/add
    some provisions that will correspond to the needs  of  their  officials
    and employees, if possible.


    We further  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  and  other
    officials concerned to stop the practice  of  giving  rings  and  other
    tokens to officials and employees and also to the guests without  legal
    basis.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the validation and adjustment of the reconciling items presented in the bank reconciliation statements.  Henceforth, adjust all valid reconciling items on a timely basis to ensure the accuracy of the recorded Cash in Bank balance.

We have recommended that management see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects and activities for the benefit of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and
Direct all department heads involved in development programs and projects to monitor implementation of the same and ensure that funds allocated thereon are utilized for projects identified in the approved AIP.

We have recommended that management direct the LDRRMO to include and implement in the LDRRM Investment Plan the deficiencies noted relative to compliance on the minimum standards for disaster preparedness. Likewise, we enjoined collaborative discussion thereon with the Sangguniang Bayan on the implementation and augmentation of the LDRRM Fund, if need be; and consider filling up the vacant position of the LDRRM Officer and detailing other regular or job order employees in the LDRRM Office.
We have recommended that management instruct the Accounting Unit to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 7 of R.A. 9679 on the mandated employer counterpart for HDMF contributions.

We have recommended that management observe the principle of the DILG in conducting future similar activities within the geographical island where the municipality is located in adherence to the principle of prudent use of scarce financial resources of the government.

We have recommended that management provide legal basis citing that the said payment of honoraria to the MBOC shall be sourced from the LGU fund and submit a certificate of non-payment of honoraria from the COMELEC Central Office Accountant.  Otherwise, bill the COMELEC for the total election expenses incurred during the May 13, 2019 National and Local Elections in accordance with Section 10 of the Omnibus Election Code.

We recommended that: 

Treasury Office i) submit to Accounting Office the Reports of Check Issued daily; and ii) ensure that payee has properly filled out the required information in Checks Register; and

Accounting office; i) use the Reports of Checks Issued (RCI) as basis in determining the outstanding checks; ii) revert back to Accounts Payable the stale checks included in Bank Reconciliation Statement; and iii) immediately return to Treasury Office the vouchers with incomplete attachments.

i) Implemented

ii) Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct Municipal Human Resource and Management Office to undertake the control of payroll preparation and issue appropriate certification or recommendation for loan applications of employees in consideration of the required level for net take home pay.

We recommended that the: 
LDRRMO continue to sustain efforts in being compliant with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness as required by the National Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council and extract data/information from the Annual Investment Plan and supply additional data so it can be transformed to an LDRRMFIP for approval of the Local Chief Executive. 

Accountant improve the frequency of submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the COA Auditor and check the completeness of documents before effecting payment.
observations.managementAction: a) Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure the implementation of all GAD PPAs during the year to fully achieve GAD mandate in accordance with Section 28 of Republic Act No. 10155; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain SL per specific PPA under each implementing office to easily provide financial status and accomplishment at any given period; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant and the GAD Focal Point to submit GAD utilization report and accomplishment report within the prescribed period under COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office and the Engineering Office submit the lacking documents above mentioned and adhere to the above cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR.
