observations.recommendation: Financial and Compliance Audit
observations.managementAction: Financial and Compliance Audit
observations.reason: Financial and Compliance Audit

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above. We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accountant submit the required documents and adhere to the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, the LGU has submitted and complied with some of the lacking documents and deficiencies noted for the procurement of goods.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower and technical knowledge of staff assigned in the Office of the Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor of the agency, see to it that disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents before processing the claims and determine whether the beneficiaries were qualified to receive the assistance offered under AICS. Also, we recommended that the transactions be charged to Donations account instead of Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account. In addition, we also recommended that Social Case Study Report must be prepared by the MSWD Officer to be able to determine whether the assistances were based on the needs of the beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers pertaining to AICS were duly supported with required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the provisions given in allowing and disallowing the travel expenses claimed by the officials and employees of the Municipality and see to it that disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents before processing the claims.
observations.reason: For CY 2019, the LGU still incurred lapses in charging claims to Traveling Expenses – Local account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above and submit lacking documents to support the validity and regularity of the disbursements. We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere to the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents before processing the claims.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has submitted and complied with some of the lacking documents and deficiencies noted for the procurement of goods.
observations.reason: Same reasons provided in no. 1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirement of the perpetual inventory system wherein regular purchases shall be coursed thru the inventory account and issuances thereof shall be recorded as they take place except those purchased out of petty cash fund which shall be for immediate use and not for stock. Such case shall be charged immediately to the appropriate expense accounts. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer regularly prepare and submit accurate and complete Summary of Supplies and Materials Issue (SSMI) with supporting Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) pursuant to Section 121 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to the Accounting Unit for proper recording/posting of issuance in the books of accounts and Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs).

On the other hand, we recommended the Municipal Accountant prepare JEVs to adjust/record issuances on the affected accounts and coordinate closely with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure reconciliation of their inventory records, also in respect of physical inventory taking.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of supplies and materials pertaining to Fuel, Oil and Lubricants and Other Supplies Materials were already booked up in their respective inventory accounts.
observations.reason: Other purchases of supplies and materials for CY 2019 were still directly charged to expenses account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through its Inventory Committee, conduct complete physical count of the properties of the Municipality, reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the computation of the depreciation expense with a lapsing schedule so as to ensure the accuracy of the amount provided and to monitor the correct balance of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has issued an Executive Order creating the Inventory Committee.
observations.reason: The RPCPPE and lapsing schedule of depreciation for CYs 2018 and 2019 were not yet submitted to the Audit Team as of date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS strictly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs as required under JMC No 2013-01 and to conduct regular coordination with the implementing office to ensure that plans are undertaken within the target period. Likewise, proper identification and prioritization of PPAs that would effectively address gender issues and concerns be conducted and ensure its inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget to be implemented during the year.
observations.managementAction: Utilization rate for GAD in CY 2019 has increased to 25.40%
observations.reason: The low utilization rate of GAD attributed PPAs was due to major attributions on several road projects that were not implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that completeness of recording transactions charged to the GAD Fund and that the GAD Focal Point Person coordinate with the Accounting Department and reconcile the draft accomplishment report with the accounting records before it is finalized for submission to offices concerned.
observations.reason: Same Audit Observation was noted. A discrepancy of P114,135.00 was still noted between the GAD Accomplishment Report and accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRM Fund to MDRRM Fund Special Trust Fund under the “Trust Liability – DRRM” account.
observations.reason: The unexpended/unobligated balance of QRF and DRRM - MOOE were not yet transferred to Special Trust fund as of date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to account all transactions that affects LDRRMF to eliminate differentiating balances appearing in the report.
observations.reason: Discrepancies were still noted between the LDRRMF Utilization Report and Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above and submit lacking documents to support the validity and regularity of the disbursements. We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere to the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents before processing the claims.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, The LGU has submitted and complied with some of the lacking documents and deficiencies noted for the procurement of goods.
observations.reason: Various procedural lapses were still noted between the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and its Secretariat and Technical Working Group (TWG).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all other concerned officials and employees adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1 in planning and implementing programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.reason:
Utilization rate of the 20% development fund for CY 2019 is only 22.40% 
which may be attributed to the limitation of technical personnel in the Engineering Office and interested bidder who are willing to transact business with the Municipality due to its geographical location.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management review and see to it if the presentation of the Annual Investment Plan is in accordance with the Approved Budget Appropriation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Appropriation for each PPA per AIP and Approved Budget for the 20% DF were already consistent with each other.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above and submit lacking documents to support the validity and regularity of the disbursements. We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere to the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents before processing the claims.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has submitted and complied with some of the lacking documents and deficiencies noted for CY 2019.
observations.reason: Various procedural lapses incurred by the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and its Secretariat and Technical Working Group (TWG) were still observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintains subsidiary ledgers for 20% development fund.
observations.reason: No special accounts were maintained for 20% Development Fund for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintains a special account in the General Fund for the operation of the Water Works System and the Power Supply System of the Municipality through the use of complete set of subsidiary ledger in accordance with Section 313 of RA 7160 and 107 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I to facilitate the audit and verification of the transactions incurred by the said economic enterprise and to enable the Management to readily determine and evaluate its results of operation and financial condition.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still failed to maintain the special accounts under the General Fund for operations of Water Works and Power Supply Systems.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The accountable officers and employees immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances giving more emphasis to the long outstanding cash advances;

The management exert extra efforts to recover outstanding cash advances from Accountable Officers who are no longer in the service nor connected with the Municipality; and

The Municipal Accountant requires strictly an Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities, pursuant to Sections 200 and 256, Vol. I of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual from all personnel who seek retirement, resignation, transfer or claim for death benefits in order that all accountabilities could be appropriately settled. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee unless previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made, pursuant to Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers and schedules for the accounts used for cash advances to facilitate monitoring of the amount outstanding per employee, to ensure that no additional cash advance will be granted to those with outstanding balances and to support the amounts reflected in the financial statements and submit the same to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances which are due for less than a year and those that has been past due for more than 3 years amounting to P20,180.00 and P29,000.00, respectively, were already under process of liquidation.  The former is being settled thru salary deduction.  For unliquidated cash advances of employees which have already been separated from the agency, efforts to enforce collection collect have already been done.
observations.reason: Total cash advances of P683,683.73 remained unliquidated as of year-end. Also, long outstanding cash advances were not yet fully liquidated or settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the balances per books with those per bank, determine the cause of the discrepancy and prepare necessary adjustments in the books for all errors committed in order to reflect the adjusted balance and give a reliable data of Cash in Bank balances in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Same audit observation was noted in CY 2019. The Municipal Accountant still failed to identify the reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) due to absence of documents in relation to the discrepancies. Cash in Bank - LCCA balances of General and Special Education Funds presented in the financial statements compared with the balances appearing in the bank statements were overstated by P10,225,612.29 and P22,671.46, respectively, whereas the Cash in Bank - LCCA balance of Trust Fund was understated by P866,069.15.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the notes to financial statements and submit general ledger and subsidiary ledgers for all funds in accordance with the abovementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: The General and Subsidiary Ledgers were already submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still failed to properly disclose of relevant information and details of accounts in the Notes to FS for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request for the authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off dormant receivable accounts pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: No schedule or document signifying settlement was received from the Municipal Accountant.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipality has secured its approved Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan for the period (2015-2024) from the National Solid Waste Management Commission on June 30, 2015 as per NSWMC Resolution No. 150, Series of 2015.

Executive Order No. 51 series of 2019 entitled Reorganizing the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) of the Municipality of Concepcion, Romblon was issued.

Executive Order No. 38 series of 2020 entitled An Executive Order Designating Focal Person on Solid Waste Management was also issued.

8 out of 9 barangays have already established their MRF.

The Municipality is currently operating a controlled dumpsite instead of an open dumpsite.
observations.reason:
Although some Barangays of the Municipality supported the Solid Waste Management Program by reconstructing their MRFs, some of the provisions of the Act were not properly observed by the concerned officials.

Also, garbage collectors are not yet equipped with proper training on handling/dealing with garbage.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Management adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 10121 and see to it that programs and projects of the Municipality identified in the DRRM Plan were fully implemented in order to strengthen the preparedness and readiness of the municipality in case of calamity.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the transfer of unexpended balance of LDRRMF to Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP for CY 2019 was submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Unexpended LDRRMF of previous years were not incorporated in the LDRRMFIP. The unexpended/unobligated balance of QRF and DRRM - MOOE for CY 2017 and 2018 were not yet transferred to Special Trust fund as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to abide with the regulations of PCW-DILG DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 in submission of the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of recording transactions charged to the GAD Fund and that the GAD Focal Point Person coordinate with the Accounting Department and reconcile the draft accomplishment report with accounting records before it is finalized for submission to offices concerned.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer to discontinue the practice of including MOOE as part of the Continuing Appropriations.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2019 was duly approved by the DILG Regional Office.
observations.reason: Discrepancies between the GAD Accomplishment Report and accounting records were still noted in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in planning & implementing programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above, and submit lacking documents to prove the transaction’s validity, accuracy, regularity and propriety.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, the LGU has submitted and complied with some of the lacking documents and deficiencies noted for the procurement of goods.
observations.reason:
Due to lack of manpower and technical knowledge of staff assigned in the Office of the Municipal Engineer.

Also, the BAC and its TWG and Secretariat lack training and updates on R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR thus, various procedural lapses were still incurred in various procurements for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local School Board to prepare and submit Annual SEF budget by strictly observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF allocation and utilization.
observations.reason: No SEF Budget was submitted for CY 2018 and 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct:
the Municipal Accountant to set up RPT/SET Receivables in accordance with Section 20 of NGAS Manual, Volume I. Analyze the unreconciled discrepancies in the RPT and SET receivables and take up the correction of the noted differences to present accurate and reliable data in the Financial Statements.

the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a duly certified list of Real Property Taxpayers showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and

the Municipal Assessor to furnish the Treasurer with the latest valuations of property of each taxpayer.
observations.managementAction: No discrepancies in the recording of RPT and SET collections were noted during year.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office failed to analyze the unreconciled discrepancies in the RPT and SET receivables noted in prior years and take up the necessary corrections.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor exercise his power to recover the public funds from the accountable employee, as mandated in the aforecited provision of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Partial settlement was already made by the previous OIC- Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: The previous OIC-Municipal Treasurer still has an unsettled balance of P135,317.37 as of this writing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze and review the accounting entries made on the accounts affected, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect appropriate adjustments thereof to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the agency’s financial position.
observations.reason: No reconciliation or adjustments in the accounts was made by the Municipal Accountant. Reciprocal accounts as at December 31, 2019 amounted to P5,629.35 and P16,214,501.19, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit immediately the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms together with the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms for January to December 2015.  The concerned accountable officers should comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume II.  Same care should be instituted on the handling and accounting of accountable forms just like in the control of cash.
observations.managementAction: Verification of the pertinent documents revealed that the Consolidated Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms prepared by the Municipal Treasurer was attached to the submitted Reports of Collections and Deposits (RCDs).
observations.reason: The individual RAAF of the Collectors to support the CRAAF was not attached nor submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the Annual Procurement Plan for CY-2015 in accordance with Section 7 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 so that timing, volume and cost of procurement are clearly defined. The management should design a well-planned program that would eliminate the use of emergency and/or personal canvass in the procurement of supplies, materials and services.
observations.reason: The Annual Procurement Plan for CY 2019 was also not submitted to the Audit Team as of this writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize every voucher for completeness of supporting documents prior to processing of payment in compliance to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445. Also, instruct the BAC Chairman to submit all aforementioned lacking documents to the Audit Team in compliance to Item 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Most, if not all, of the documents noted to be lacking in this particular project were already complied with reference to the current year’s transactions, denoting that the Municipal Accountant is more circumspect in checking completeness of supporting documents before processing claims.
observations.reason: Lacking documents pertaining to the water system project were not submitted in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

2.1.  Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under existing laws, rules and regulations;
2.3.   Management to exert efforts and/or avail of possible legal remedies with regards to the accountability of the previous Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Management:

3.1. Direct all the concerned officials/employees to immediately settle the outstanding cash advances, and all advances must be liquidated at year-end;
observations.reason: The Management already identified the erroneous transactions recorded in the said account but not all necessary adjustments were made.

observations.recommendation: 3.3.   Instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous advance is liquidated, and coordinate with the management of other government agencies to direct the officials/employees previously assigned in the Municipality to immediately settle their outstanding accounts; and

3.4.    Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the cash advances, determine long outstanding and dormant receivable accounts and prepare necessary reports together with the supporting documents for the request for write-off, if warranted, as indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended the following:

4.1. The Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned employees to gather necessary papers/documents and facilitate the transfer of ownership of the donated vehicles to the Municipality; and
4.2. The OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the donated equipment in the books of account upon completion of the necessary documents to support the Municipality’s ownership of the assets.

5.1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other responsible officials communicate with the owners and settle issues and problems concerning Road Right of Way of the projects, if not yet resolved.

5.2. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 by seeing to it that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with the other responsible officials and employees:
6.1.   Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under JMC No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization;

6.3. Ensure that the projects/programs of other government agencies are among the allowable expenditures under the abovementioned JMC before the Municipality commits the allotted counterpart fund under the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with other members of the MDRRMC:

a)	Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under the pertinent sections of JMC No. 2013-1 in the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF; and

b)	Stop the practice of charging to the LDRRM Fund expenses which are administrative in nature.

We recommended the following:

8.1.  Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer (1) to collect the necessary market fees and charges from all vendors who occupy space/area in the market; and (2) to see to it that all stall owners pay their rental fees within the first 20 days of the month otherwise, corresponding surcharges and penalties must be imposed and collected;

8.3.  All concerned officers and employees to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations provided in the Revised Municipal Revenue Code 2017 and Market Code 2012 by collecting corresponding fees and charges, surcharges and penalties from all vendors who do business in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with other responsible officials and employees strictly adhere to Section 88 of PD No. 1445 and avoid giving advance payment for services not yet rendered or for supplies and materials not yet delivered under any contract therefor.

9.2. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other concerned officials and employees see to it that all goods/services procured are done in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and supported with complete documentation.

We recommended the following:

10.1.	The Local Chief Executive together with other responsible officials and employees to strictly adhere to the procedures in the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 by not providing fuel, oil and lubricants to the vehicles which do not bear government plates;
10.2.	Official/employee assigned to approve the DTTs to see to it that all data needed be accomplished, specifically the vehicle plate number and passenger/s name;
10.3.	Drivers to advise their passenger/s to affix their signatures in the approved DTTs after the trip; and

13.1.We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person and other responsible officials to conduct gender analysis and assessment in identifying gender issues and appropriate programs, projects and activities (PPAs) and strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA

We recommended that Management:

15.1. Intensify the tax collection campaign through issuance of follow-up letters/notices of tax collection to delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their tax obligations;

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

16.1. Reconcile the balance of the Subsidiary Ledger and the Financial Statement by identifying the cause of the difference and make necessary adjusting entries;
16.2.  Record transactions for liabilities not falling under any of the specific payable accounts in the Other Payables pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009.

18.1. We recommended that the Municipality observe the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and Section 1 of LBC No. 98 by appropriating and expending personal services within the prescribed 45% limit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned employees to strictly follow the guidelines prescribed by COA Circular No. 96-010 by performing the following:

19.1. The canvasser shall instruct the supplier or its representative that the quotation should not exceed the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) as indicated in the PR to avail the most advantageous price for the government;
19.2.State delivery terms and conditions for clarity of agreement between the parties;
19.3.Indicate all necessary information of the participating suppliers in the RFQs; and

20.1. We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials strictly adhere to the rules and regulations of RA No. 5447, Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and Section 3 of Executive Order No. 356, s. 2004 on the utilization of the Special Education Fund. 

We further recommended that the Local School Board prioritize programs, projects and activities as stipulated in the aforementioned provisions in the preparation of the Annual School Board Budget for the SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the Department Heads/ employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2015 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/ offices and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.

21.2. We also recommended that henceforth, the LGU of Concepcion, shall within sixty (60) days from their receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

22.1. We recommended that fiscal responsibility in all financial transactions of the Municipality shall be exercised by the concerned officers and employees by ensuring that sufficient/relevant documents are attached to the DVs to show that expenses are for public purpose and that the claims are valid.

23.1. We recommended that the Municipality include in the Report of PPAs, the list of all programs and activities with ongoing implementation and those to be implemented during the year, and diligently adhere to the provisions of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

24.1.We recommended that the financial assistance extended to all the officials and employees, including casuals, contractual and job order workers, of the Municipal Government of Concepcion be refunded and the granting of any allowances, incentives or benefits not authorized by any laws or statutory provision be stopped. Moreover, we recommended that expenditures be classified and charged in accordance with the nature and character of the expense.

25.1. We recommended that the accountant verify the completeness and legitimacy of the documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 before certifying in the disbursement vouchers. We further recommended that the variance between the AIR and AR/PAR be refunded

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

27.1. Require the Administrative Officials authorizing the travels and the Drivers to properly accomplish and serially number the Driver’s Trip Tickets;

27.3. Instruct the concerned LGU employees to adopt the prescribed format of the Driver’s Trip Ticket, Requisition and Issue Voucher, Monthly Report of Official Travel, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and diligently observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61.

28.1.We recommended that the LCE require the Human Resource Management Officer, Department Heads and Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA, pertaining to net take home pay in order to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough support for their daily needs until the next pay day.
29.1. We recommended that management cause the refund of the excess of the 2013 PEI in the total amount of ₱1,106,000.00.

30.1. We recommended strict adherence to the provision of Section 9.1 and 9.2 of Budget Circular No. 2012-3. The amount of ₱1,856,000.00, being unauthorized should be refunded by the municipal officials and employees who were recipients of the benefits.

observations.managementAction:
The accountant identified that there were errors in recording the Due from LGU under General Fund Account amounting to P2,300,000.00 summarized as follows: 
Erroneously recorded the fund transfer from General Fund to Trust Fund account the cash counterpart for Project “Concreting Maligubaan Road- June 2018.
Erroneously recorded the fund transfer from General Fund to Trust Fund the cash counterpart for BEMONC (Basic Emergency Obstetrical Newborn Care) - October 2012.
The previous Municipal Bookkeeper erroneously recorded the Fund Transfer from GF to TF in year 2017- cash counterpart of the BUB Project “Construction of Core Local Road. Connecting the Landslide prone area of Brgy. PoganSosoon and Lingatongan.
observations.reason: Remaining unreconciled amount totaled P357,467.30 as of 12/31/2019.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management directs the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the erroneous fund transfers and reconcile the reciprocal accounts. Likewise, we enjoin management to require immediate liquidation of the fund transfers made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already adjusted the affected accounts.
observations.reason: Remaining balance of unreconciled accounts as of 12/31/2019 isreduced to P744,229.20.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit to COA copies of the Memorandum of Agreement on aforementioned fund transfers and effect the submission thereon whenever future fund transfers are involved.
observations.managementAction: MOAs were already submitted
GAD Plan Budget is already in accordance with the standard format using HGDG tool.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the municipal accountant for the timely submission of the bank reconciliation statements and immediately effect adjustments/corrections in the book balance from prior period’s reconciliation statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant already adjusted the reconciling items from 2017-2018.

Bank reconciliation statements were already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal accountant review the existence, validity and accuracy of the outstanding accounts payable and if found to have no valid claims against the local government  or are not covered by perfected contract on record, cause the reversion thereof to the unappropriated surplus. Likewise, we recommend that the municipal accounting office enjoin the maintenance and safekeeping of both manual and electronic files of accounting records as a control measure against any loss.
observations.managementAction:
Payables with no determined creditors were already reverted to the unappropriated surplus through executing the following:

Certification from the previous Municipal Bookkeeper that there were no previous valis claim against the LGU or are not  covered by perfected contracts.
SB Resolution authorizing the Municipal Accountant for the reversion of said unpaid obligations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a) the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (i) create an Inventory Committee, consisting of a representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor, General Services Office (GSO) and Accounting Office; (ii) require each department head to fast track the inventory taking of all properties under their office and submit the inventory report to the committee for validation and verification of the correctness and existence of the property and; (iii) in the event that an inventory committee is created, require it to (1) check the condition of the property, and any damaged, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported on the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable and Waste Material Report (2) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and; (3) immediately perform the required disposal of unserviceable property b) the GSO/Municipal Treasurer (i) maintain Property Cards per class and category of property, plant and equipment indicating acquisition date, the brief descriptions; (ii) revisit  previous years’ records and reconcile with the information at the accounting office, and (iii) update the property issue slip/PAR to fix the custodianship of the property; and c) The Municipal Accountant to drop the cost of unserviceable and obsolete properties items from the books following the process prescribed by the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in Local Government under COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in CY 2018 AAR 
( Audit Observation No. 4)

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) coordinate the efforts of the public and private sectors and implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; (b)  work on the promulgation and implementation of “No Littering” Ordinance; (c) maximize the operation of the MRF and implement the Municipality’s plan of producing organic fertilizer and adopt specific revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its Solid Waste Management Plan; and (d) encourage the full participation of the barangays in the implementation of the R.A. No. 9003.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, together with the Municipal Engineer, (i) to make a careful planning and extensive study of each and every project to be undertaken by the Municipality to ensure that government funds are expended judiciously and government property are protected or safeguarded, and (ii) perform appropriate action to deter the disparagement of the equipment’s value.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that: a. the Inventory Committee conduct the complete physical count of all PPE accounts of the Municipality so that unserviceable/non functional PPEs in the reports can be submitted to the Audit Team; b. the Municipal Accountant reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made; and c. the Municipal Treasurer make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.
observations.reason: In CY 2018, no RPCPP was submitted to the Audit Team. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer did not submit the report of IIRUP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request for the authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off dormant receivable accounts pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.reason: The comment of the Municipal Accountant in last years’ exit conference that the long outstanding receivables will be acted upon was not realized.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing audit observation, we recommended that: a. the accountable officers and employees immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances giving more emphasis to the long outstanding cash advances; b. the management exert extra efforts to recover outstanding cash advances from Accountable Officers who are no longer in the service nor connected with the Municipality; and c. the Municipal Accountant require strictly an Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities, pursuant to Sections 200 and 256, Vol. I of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual from all personnel who seek retirement, resignation, transfer or claim for death benefits in order that all accountabilities could be appropriately settled. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee unless previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made, pursuant to Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers and schedules for the accounts used for cash advances to facilitate monitoring of the amount outstanding per employee, to ensure that no additional cash advance will be granted to those with outstanding balances and to support the amounts reflected in the financial statements and submit same to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Same observation was noted. Long outstanding cash advances were not liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere strictly to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations in the conduct of procurement. Submit copies of government contracts and all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same so that the management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: The municipality submitted to the Audit Team CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in planning & implementing programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that the development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents. We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above, and submit lacking documents to prove the transaction’s validity, accuracy, regularity and propriety.
observations.managementAction: The municipality submitted to the Audit Team

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local School Board to prepare and submit Annual SEF budget by strictly observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF allocation and utilization.
observations.managementAction: No SEF Budget was submitted again for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. the Municipal Accountant to set up RPT/SET Receivables in accordance with the Section 20 of NGAS Manual, Volume I. b. Analyze the unreconciled discrepancies in the RPT and SET receivables and take up the correction of the noted differences to present accurate and reliable data in the Financial Statements. c. the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a duly certified list of Real Property Taxpayers showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and d. the Municipal Assessor to furnish the Treasurer with the latest valuations of property of each taxpayer;
observations.managementAction: Not Same Implemented observation was noted. The Municipal Accountant promised that he will check/trace the difference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances per books with those per bank to determine what caused the huge amount of discrepancy. Prepare the necessary adjustments in the books for all errors committed in order to give a reliable data of Cash in Bank balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Audit Team noted the discrepancies on the individual balances of bank accounts with the balances per General Ledger. The Municipal Accountant in CY 2018 failed to identify the reconciling items in the BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Collecting Officers to: a) Strictly observe the existing rules and regulations governing collections and deposits of government funds to avoid the suspicion that such funds were misused; b) Deposit the Cash Local Treasury balance with the depository bank to prevent further actions, as may be deemed necessary, to be taken against them.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Audit Team noted that the Municipal Treasurer still has undeposited collections amounting to P118,526.00 for all funds as of Dec. 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze and review the accounting entries made on the accounts affected, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect appropriate adjustments thereof to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the agency’s financial position.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant he will find time to work back/trace the difference.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the Annual Procurement Plan for CY-2015 so that timing, volume and cost of procurement are clearly defined and to forestall use of emergency and/or personal canvass.
observations.managementAction: The municipality submitted to the Audit Team the Annual Procurement Plan of the municipality for CY 2018 in connection with the audit of transactions of the municipality. However, no APP was submitted as of this writing to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere strictly to existing rules and regulations on the procurement of goods thru Shopping in the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184. The Accountant, being the agency’s internal auditor, is advised to check the completeness of the supporting documents before signing Box A of the Disbursement Voucher to ascertain the validity and correctness of the claim for payment, pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. Submit lacking documents for the subject procurement to prove validity, regularity and propriety on subject transactions.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
