observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

1.1 Require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to reconcile the discrepancy between the records and reports and make the necessary adjustments in the book of accounts;
1.3 Require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to remove from the books unserviceable PPEs, provided that proper modes of disposal were observed.

2.1 We recommended that the Management appropriate funds and the Municipal Assessor’s Office be directed to exert effort in titling all parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government. The Accounting Office to set up and maintain complete accounting records and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to keep on file complete records of real properties.

2.2 We also recommended that the offices concerned conduct an inventory of all the parcels of land owned by the Municipality and reconcile with the records in the books for proper presentation in the Financial Statements.
3.2 We also recommended that the Management exert efforts and/or avail of possible legal remedies with regards to the accountability of the previous Municipal Treasurer.

4.3 We further recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and concerned municipal employees conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the cash advances, determine long outstanding and dormant receivable accounts and prepare necessary reports together with the supporting documents for the possible request for write-off as indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

5.2 We also recommended that the Municipal officials strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
15.4.Provide all other requirements of the aforementioned Circular

17.2 We also recommended that henceforth, the LGU of Concepcion, shall within sixty (60) days from their receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

22.1 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO to strictly adhere with the provisions of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and must submit regularly to the COA Audit Team the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of the MDRRMF to ascertain the correctness of its utilization.
23.2Designate an LGU employee to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption;

27.2 Likewise, the amounts received by them since the time they were relieved from their original functions shall be refunded to the Municipality. Thus, the former SB Secretary shall refund the RATA he received from March 1 to December 31, 2011 for a total of ₱88,000.00 while the former Municipal Treasurer shall refund a total of ₱96,800.00  representing the RATA she received since February 1 to December 31, 2011.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation to management that he directs the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the erroneous fund transfers and reconcile the reciprocal accounts. Likewise, we enjoin management to require immediate liquidation of the fund transfers made.
observations.managementAction: Prepared Schedules for the Due to/Due from Accounts to foot and easily find the discrepancy.
observations.reason: Not Implemented The accountant is yet to call records to support the reconciliation. This is reiterated on Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management direct the LDRRMO to include and implement in the LDRRM Investment Plan the deficiencies noted relative to compliance on the minimum standards for disaster preparedness. Likewise, we enjoined collaborative discussion thereon with the Sangguniang Bayan on the implementation and augmentation of the LDRRM Fund, if need be.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply and improve some of the noted deficiencies.
observations.reason: Not Implemented The appointment of the LDRRMF officer was disapproved by the Civil Service. No action was taken since no proper turn-over was made to the in-coming designated LDRRMF officer. This is reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
1.8	We recommended that:
Treasury Office i) submit to Accounting Office the Reports of Check Issued daily; and ii) ensure that payee has properly filled out the required information in Checks Register; and
Accounting office; i) use the Reports of Checks Issued (RCI) as basis in determining the outstanding checks; ii) revert back to Accounts Payable the stale checks included in Bank Reconciliation Statement; and iii) immediately return to Treasury Office the vouchers with incomplete attachments.
2.7	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to take the initiative and return the unexpended/unused amount P 1,592,169.98 to the source agencies or to the BTR.

3.10	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (i) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all loans payables have complete subsidiary records and reconcile them with the creditors’ information; (ii) coordinate with Finance Office of the Office of the President for the determination of actual obligation with respect to undocumented loans; and (iii) make representations to the Office of the President for condonation of the interest amounting to ₱6,841,390.75 or negotiation in affordable manner the repayment of the principal obligation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant; a) align the estimation of useful life of the Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality with COA regulations; and b) adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 with regard to the application of residual value on road networks.
4.10	Lastly, we reiterate the audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive should: i) create an inventory team that would conduct physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to COA; and ii) require the Accounting and GSO to complete their property ledger cards and conduct the reconciliation thereon.

5.6	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct Municipal Human Resource and Management Office to undertake the control of payroll preparation and issue appropriate certification or recommendation for loan applications of employees in consideration of the required level for net take home pay.

6.11	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) ensure that the local development fund shall be used only for PPAs authorized by  DILG-DBM JC No. 2017-01 and expenditures for development projects should be aligned and connected to the implementation of development projects, programs and activities; (b) instruct the BAC follow the above provisions on RA 9184 to promote transparency in the procurement process; and (c) direct the responsible officers to submit the approved contracts and purchase orders within five days from execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular 2009-01.

7.8	We recommended that the Management ensure the allotment of adequate insurance budget covering all insurable properties of the Municipality. We further recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the GSO Officer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be secured from GSIS insurance fund.

8.7	We recommended that the:
LDRRMO continue to sustain efforts in being compliant with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness as required by the National Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council and extract data/information from the Annual Investment Plan and supply additional data so it can be transformed to an LDRRMFIP for approval of the Local Chief Executive.
Accountant improve the frequency of submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the COA Auditor and check the completeness of documents before effecting payment.

9.6	 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adhere to the provision of Executive Order 292 and its Omnibus Rules, as amended, by directing the concerned municipal official to immediately install the procured Biometrics Machines to record the actual and correct time of arrival to and departure from office of all municipal employees.

10.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure the implementation of all GAD PPAs during the year to fully achieve GAD mandate in accordance with Section 28 of Republic Act No. 10155; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain SL per specific PPA under each implementing office to easily provide financial status and accomplishment at any given period; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant and the GAD Focal Point to submit GAD utilization report and accomplishment report within the prescribed period under COA Circular No. 2014-001.

11.4	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:  a) Market Supervisor to: i) exert efforts to collect the receivable from stall owners; ii) inspect every stalls and ensure that these were occupied by legal tenants; iii) make recommendations to revise the current Contract of Lease and align the provisions  to the Local Revenue Code or Market Code to be enacted; iv) adhere to the provisions of the Local Revenue Code / Market Code and recommend cancellation of Contract of Lease if the stallholders are in default of payments for maximum of three consecutive months; and v) submit monthly a copy of receivables from stall owners to Accounting Office; and b) Accounting Office to recognize receivables based on the report submitted by the Market Supervisor. We further recommended that the LCE make representation and proposal to Sangguniang Bayan for the amendment or updating of the Local Revenue Code.

12.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to expedite the revision of the 10 Year Solid Waste Management Plan in compliance with R. A. No. 9003 and implement the PPAs prioritized under SWMP.

13.8	We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat, Accounting Office and the Engineering Office submit the lacking documents above mentioned and adhere to the above cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR.
14.1	We recommended that all taxes withheld be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.
15.3	We recommended the management continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
16.2	We recommended that Pag-Ibig premiums deducted be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period provided for under the aforementioned law.
17.3	We recommended the management continuously remit the PHIC premiums within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation
relative to the deficiencies noted above. We further recommended that the
Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accountant to submit the
required documents and adhere to the provisions of the Implementing Rules
and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, as the internal
auditor of the agency, see to it that disbursement vouchers are completely
supported with valid documents before processing the claims and determine
whether the beneficiaries were qualified to receive the assistance offered
under AICS. Also, we recommended that the transactions be charged to
Donations account instead of Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses
account. In addition, we also recommended that Social Case Study Report
must be prepared by the MSWD Officer to be able to determine whether the
assistances were based on the needs of the beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the
provisions given in allowing and disallowing the travel expenses claimed by
the officials and employees of the Municipality and see to it that
disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents
before processing the claims.

We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation
relative to the deficiencies noted above and submit lacking documents to
support the validity and regularity of the disbursements. We further
recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere to the provisions
of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in
all stages of procurement and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that
disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents
before processing the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to comply with the requirement of the perpetual inventory
system wherein regular purchases shall be coursed thru the inventory
account and issuances thereof shall be recorded as they take place except
those purchased out of petty cash fund which shall be for immediate use and
not for stock. Such case shall be charged immediately to the appropriate
expense accounts.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer regularly
prepare and submit accurate and complete Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issue (SSMI) with supporting Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS)
pursuant to Section 121 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to the Accounting
Unit for proper recording/posting of issuance in the books of accounts and
Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs).

On the other hand, we recommended the Municipal Accountant
prepare JEVs to adjust/record issuances on the affected accounts and
coordinate closely with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure reconciliation of
their inventory records, also in respect of physical inventory taking.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through its
Inventory Committee, conduct complete physical count of the
properties of the Municipality, reconcile the results of the count with
the accounting records and submit the complete and accurate
RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual
on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the
computation of the depreciation expense with a lapsing schedule so as
to ensure the accuracy of the amount provided and to monitor the
correct balance of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Management submit a written
explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above and submit lacking
documents to support the validity and regularity of the disbursements. We
further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere to the
provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act
No. 9184 in all stages of procurement and the Municipal Accountant to see
to it that disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid
documents before processing the claims.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to transfer the
unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRM Fund to MDRRM
Fund Special Trust Fund under the “Trust Liability – DRRM” account.

We recommended the Municipal Budget Officer and the
Municipal Accountant to account all transactions that affects
LDRRMF to eliminate differentiating balances appearing in the
report.

We recommended that the Management submit a written
explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above and submit
lacking documents to support the validity and regularity of the
disbursements. We further recommended that the Bids and Awards
Committee adhere to the provisions of the Implementing Rules and
Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement
and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers
are completely supported with valid documents before processing the
claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all other
concerned officials and employees adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-
DBM JMC 2017-1 in planning and implementing programs/projects/activities
funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that development projects
identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the
benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the Management review and see to it if the
presentation of the Annual Investment Plan is in accordance with the
Approved Budget Appropriation of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management submit a written
explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above and submit lacking
documents to support the validity and regularity of the disbursements.
We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere to
the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic
Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement and the Municipal Accountant to
see to it that disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid
documents before processing the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintains
subsidiary ledgers for 20% development fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintains a special
account in the General Fund for the operation of the Water Works System
and the Power Supply System of the Municipality through the use of
complete set of subsidiary ledger in accordance with Section 313 of RA 7160
and 107 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I to facilitate the audit and
verification of the transactions incurred by the said economic enterprise and
to enable the Management to readily determine and evaluate its results of
operation and financial condition.

We recommended that:
a. The accountable officers and employees immediately liquidate their
outstanding cash advances giving more emphasis to the long outstanding
cash advances;
b. The management exert extra efforts to recover outstanding cash advances
from Accountable Officers who are no longer in the service nor connected
with the Municipality; and
c. The Municipal Accountant requires strictly an Office Clearance from
Money and Property accountabilities, pursuant to Sections 200 and 256,
Vol. I of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual from all
personnel who seek retirement, resignation, transfer or claim for death
benefits in order that all accountabilities could be appropriately settled.
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or
employee unless previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a
proper accounting thereof is made, pursuant to Section 4.1.2 of COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and maintain Subsidiary
Ledgers and schedules for the accounts used for cash advances to
facilitate monitoring of the amount outstanding per employee, to ensure
that no additional cash advance will be granted to those with outstanding
balances and to support the amounts reflected in the financial statements
and submit the same to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the
balances per books with those per bank, determine the cause of the
discrepancy and prepare necessary adjustments in the books for all errors
committed in order to reflect the adjusted balance and give a reliable data of
Cash in Bank balances in the financial statements.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Create a new Inventory Committee to conduct  the  complete  physical
   inventory of the Municipality’s properties and  prepare  and  submit
   the corresponding inventory report;

b) Require the Inventory Committee, the General Services Office and the
   Accounting Office to reconcile all the inventory reports for  proper
   presentation in the Financial Statements; and

c) Oblige the General Services Office  to  maintain  complete  property
   cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct  of  Annual
   Inventory Count and in the reconciliation of  balances  between  the
   Inventory Report and the books of accounts.

Recommendations:

We recommended the following:

a) Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer  to  deposit
   all collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily  or
   not later than the next banking day as required under existing laws,
   rules and regulations;


b)  Local  Chief  Executive  to  direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and
   Municipal Accountant to  check/verify  the  difference  between  the
   Cashbook and Ledger/FS balances of the Cash Local  Treasury  account
   as of December 31, 2018, make necessary adjustments, and henceforth,
   reconcile their reports monthly; and


c) Management to exert efforts and/or avail of possible legal  remedies
   with  regards  to  the  accountability  of  the  previous  Municipal
   Treasurer.

Recommendations:


We recommended that the Management:


a) Direct all the concerned officials/employees to  immediately  settle
   the outstanding cash advances, and all advances must  be  liquidated
   at year-end;

b) Establish a strong internal control and create a policy or  standard
   operating procedures in the grant, utilization  and  liquidation  of
   cash advance which provides for  the  sanctions/penalties  for  non-
   compliance thereon subject to existing relevant laws, rules and
   regulations;

c)  Instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  refrain   from   granting
   additional cash advances unless the previous advance is  liquidated,
   and coordinate with the management of other government  agencies  to
   direct  the   officials/employees   previously   assigned   in   the
   Municipality to immediately settle their outstanding accounts; and


d) Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis  and  validation
   of  the  cash  advances,  determine  long  outstanding  and  dormant
   receivable accounts and prepare necessary reports together with the
   supporting documents for the request for write-off, if warranted, as
   indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendations:


We recommended the following:


a) The Local Chief Executive  to  direct  the  concerned  employees  to
   gather necessary papers/documents and  facilitate  the  transfer  of
   ownership of the donated vehicles to the Municipality; and


b) The OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the donated equipment in  the
   books of account upon  completion  of  the  necessary  documents  to
   support the Municipality’s ownership of the assets.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  and  other  responsible
officials communicate with the owners and settle  issues  and  problems
concerning Road Right of Way of the projects, if not yet resolved.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4 (6) of  PD
No. 1445 and COA Circular  No.  2012-001  by  seeing  to  it  that  all
disbursements are supported with complete documentation.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with  the  other
responsible officials and employees:

 
a) Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under JMC No. 2017-1  of  the
    DILG and DBM in  the  appropriation  and  utilization  of  the  20%
    Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization;

 b) See to it that projects planned under the 20% Development Fund have
    specific purpose and indicate in the Annual Investment Plan a brief
    description of the project, its  exact  location,  target  date  of
    implementation and the corresponding budget; and


 c) Ensure that the projects/programs of other government agencies  are
    among the  allowable  expenditures  under  the  abovementioned  JMC
    before the Municipality commits the allotted counterpart fund under
    the 20% DF.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  together  with  other
members of the MDRRMC:


a) Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under the  pertinent  sections
   of JMC No. 2013-1  in  the  appropriation  and  utilization  of  the
   LDRRMF; and

b) Stop the practice of charging to the LDRRM Fund expenses  which  are
   administrative in nature.

Recommendations:

We recommended the following:


a) Local Chief Executive to  direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  (1)  to
   collect the necessary market fees and charges from all  vendors  who
   occupy space/area in the market; and (2) to see to it that all stall
   owners pay their rental fees within the first 20 days of  the  month
   otherwise, corresponding surcharges and penalties  must  be  imposed
   and collected;
    b) Sangguniang Bayan to consider the need to  revise  the  Market  Code
       2012 to increase the rates of fees and charges; and


c) All concerned officers and employees to strictly adhere to the rules
   and regulations provided in the Revised Municipal Revenue Code  2017
   and Market Code 2012 by collecting corresponding fees  and  charges,
   surcharges and penalties from all vendors who do business in the
   Municipality.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  together  with  other
responsible officials and employees strictly adhere to Section 88 of PD
No. 1445 and avoid giving advance payment for services not yet rendered
or for supplies and materials not  yet  delivered  under  any  contract
therefor.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other  concerned
officials and employees see to it that all goods/services procured  are
done in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA No.  9184  and  supported
with complete documentation.

Recommendations:

We recommended the following:


a) The Local Chief Executive together with other responsible  officials
   and employees to strictly adhere to the procedures in the Manual  on
   Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor  Vehicles  prescribed
   in COA Circular No. 77-61 by not providing fuel, oil and  lubricants
   to the vehicles which do not bear government plates;

b) Official/employee assigned to approve the DTTs to see to it that all
   data needed be accomplished, specifically the vehicle  plate  number
   and passenger/s name;

c) Drivers to advise their passenger/s to affix their signatures in the
   approved DTTs after the trip; and

d) Monthly reports related to fuel consumption submitted to the COA for
   audit.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management directs the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the erroneous fund transfers and reconcile the reciprocal accounts. Likewise, we enjoin management to require immediate liquidation of the fund transfers made.
We recommended that the management submit to COA copies of the Memorandum of Agreement on aforementioned fund transfers and effect the submission thereon whenever future fund transfers are involved.

We have recommended that the management direct the LDRRMO to include and implement in the LDRRM Investment Plan the deficiencies noted relative to compliance on the minimum standards for disaster preparedness. Likewise, we enjoined collaborative discussion thereon with the Sangguniang Bayan on the implementation and augmentation of the LDRRM Fund, if need be.

We recommended that a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer observe proper accounting and reporting of RPT and SET as prescribed under the NGAS; b) Municipal Treasurer reconcile his records with that of the Municipal Assessor to determine the appropriate amount of taxes for recording purposes; and c) MTO to submit a Certified List of Taxpayers to support the receivables recorded by the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: The management already started to prepare the certified list of taxpayers. They were also in the process of reconcile the RPT as SET accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management instruct: a) the Municipal Treasurer to locate the missing disbursement voucher with supporting documents amounting to ₽303,803.57  and submit them immediately the Municipal Accountant for proper recording and reporting; and b) instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to improve their control measures in managing disbursement vouchers and documents in consideration of the controls embedded in the procedures contained in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Planning Development Officer, continue to (a) formulate and invest in high-impact capital expenditures projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund; and (b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the projects to maximize the developmental benefits of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.reason: The management implemented the remaining PPAs for 20%, but overlooked the regulations and charged improper expenditures for the 20% EDF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Head of LDRRMO (i) to prepare LDRRMP; (ii) to monitor the monthly utilization of LDRRMF and the corresponding Accomplishment Report; and (iii) to observe strictly the laws rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund to ensure that the desired services and benefits are delivered to the constituents before, during and after disaster/calamities; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare, certify and submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in prescribed format under Annex E of COA Circular No. 2014-002.
observations.reason: The management was compliant with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness but still failed on the submission of monthly report on sources & utilization.

observations.recommendation: We require the Property Offices of both the LGU and the DepEd to coordinate and strengthen the controls on assets accountability and accurately monitor the custodianship of government property.

We recommended that management allocate the mandatory minimum appropriation or funding for LCPC and instruct the council members to (i) ensure the proper implementation of the formulated PPAs in accordance with the above law and regulations; and (ii) consider outsourcing or partnership with private organizations and entities that provide sponsorship or financial assistance for the implementation of LCPC PPAs.

While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU to support gender equality and women empowerment, we recommend that the Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group (i) Endeavor to capacitate themselves through attendance to trainings under GFPS for more responsive planning; and (ii) Facilitate the establishment of the GAD Database to aid in gender analysis and provide baseline data on the status of women and men in the LGU.
observations.reason: The municipal officials were able to comply with the establishment of GAD Database, but failed on its reporting on its PPAs & accomplishments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 relevant to appropriation, entitlement, release, and use of the confidential funds.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor a) relieve immediately the attending clerk (Ms. Tiglao) from collecting functions; b) require Ms. Tiglao to restitute the amount of P1,842.50 representing the difference between the amount of receivable collected (P1,902.50) and the altered amount of P60.00 reported as collection during the day; and c) direct the Municipal Treasurer to instruct all division heads as well as the Liquidating Officer to carefully (i) check and review the accuracy of the collections being remitted during the day and the corresponding accountability for accountable forms prior to receipt and signing of the Report of Collections and Deposits in accordance with the above accounting regulations and (ii) depart from delegating collection functions to unqualified employee or officer.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all collectors to remit intact their daily collections to avoid loss, misplacement or unwarranted appropriation for personal expenses or benefits.  Likewise, we also recommend that the collectors exercise care and diligence in performing their job, especially in filling up and issuance of official receipts and in reporting their accountability for accountable forms in the Report of Collections and Deposits.
We recommended that management (i) secure the original OR/CR of the subject vehicles, and; (ii) coordinate with the dealers and LTO for the immediate registration of the motor vehicles.
observations.reason: The management were able to register a few of its motor vehicles, but still failed to secure the original OR/CR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR in the procurement activities and submit immediately the above required documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the notes to financial statements and submit the general ledger and subsidiary ledgers for all funds in accordance with the abovementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: The General Ledgers and Subsidiary Ledgers were submitted to the Audit Team
observations.reason: however, disclosures of relevant information and details of accounts in the Notes to financial statements was not implemented by the Municipal Accountant for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: a. Conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of the plan and other relevant laws within the Municipality’s jurisdiction to ensure the viability and effectiveness of the solid waste management program; b. Designate SWM Coordinator who will be the implementing arm of the municipality’s SWM plan and who shall directly supervise all solid waste operations and activities of the municipality; c. Require other barangays to re-establish and effectively maintain their MRFs and encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of wastes; d. Reconstitute the membership of the SWMB to conform to the membership requirement of RA 9003 and to optimize the involvement of civil society as envisioned in the law.
observations.managementAction: Although some Barangays of the Municipality supported the Solid Waste Management Program by reconstructing their MRFs, some of the provisions of the Act were not properly observe by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality allocate no less than 1% of the total budget appropriation in compliance to the above provisions of DILG MC 2012-120 and Section 15 of R.A. 9344.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to abide with the regulations of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 in submission of the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of recording transactions charged to the GAD Fund and that the GAD Focal Point Person coordinate with the Accounting Department and reconcile the draft accomplishment report with accounting records before it is finalized for submission to offices concerned. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer discontinue the practice of including MOOE as part of the Continuing Appropriations.
observations.reason: Inconsistencies were again noted in the report and disbursements out of GAD Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer immediately the Monthly Report of Accountability together with the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms for January to December 2015. The concerned accountable officers should comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume II. Same care should be instituted on the handling and accounting of accountable forms just like in the control of cash.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Verification of the Report of Collections and Deposits revealed that the Consolidated Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms every month prepared by the Mun. Treasurer were attached to the RCD. However the individual CRAAF of the Collectors to support the CRAAF were not attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with paragraph e, Section 4 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 10 of the same Manual, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with the NGAS Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor exercise his power to recover the public funds from the accountable employee, as mandated by the aforecited provision of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: In CY 2018 the former OIC-Municipal Treasurer made settlement of her undeposited collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for the operation of the Water Works System of the Municipality economic enterprise through the use of complete set of subsidiary ledger in accordance with Section 313 of RA 7160 and Sections 106 & 107 of NGAS Manual. Vol. I to facilitate the audit and verification of the transactions incurred by the said economic enterprises and to enable the Management to readily determine and evaluate its results of operation and financial condition.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised compliance on the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize every voucher for completeness of supporting documents prior to processing of payment in compliance to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445. Also, instruct the BAC Chairman to submit all aforementioned lacking documents stated in the AOM. Submit all lacking documents stated in the AOM.

observations.reason: In CY 2018, the municipality failed to submit all lacking documents stated in the AOM.

observations.recommendation: 1.2 Direct the GSO and the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the PPE and to maintain Property Cards;

3.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under existing laws, rules and regulations;

4.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive demand the liquidation of the unliquidated cash advances; direct the establishment of a strong internal control and create a policy or standard operating procedure in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances which provides for the sanctions/penalties for non-compliance thereon subject to existing relevant laws, rules and regulations; and strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

4.2 We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant should not allow the grant of new cash advance if liquidation of the previous cash advance is not submitted. 5.1 We recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of charging expenses to the 20% Development Fund which cater only to the needs of specific individuals and/or associations and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures.

14.1 We recommended that the Municipality observe the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and Section 1 of LBC No. 98 by appropriating and expending personal services within the prescribed 45% limit.

15.1. The canvasser shall instruct the supplier or its representative that the quotation should not exceed the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) as indicated in the PR to avail the most advantageous price for the government;
15.2.State delivery terms and conditions for clarity of agreement between the parties;
15.3.Indicate all necessary information of the participating suppliers in the RFQs; and

16.1 We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials strictly adhere to the rules and regulations of RA No. 5447, Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and Section 3 of Executive Order No. 356, s. 2004 on the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

17.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the Department Heads/ employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2015 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/ offices and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.

18.1 We recommended that fiscal responsibility in all financial transactions of the Municipality shall be exercised by the concerned officers and employees by ensuring that sufficient/relevant documents are attached to the DVs to show that expenses are for public purpose and that the claims are valid.

19.1 We recommended that the Municipality shall include in the Report of PPAs, the list of all programs and activities with ongoing implementation and those to be implemented during the year, and diligently adhere to the provisions of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

20.1 We recommended that the financial assistance extended to all the officials and employees, including casuals, contractual and job order workers, of the Municipal Government of Concepcion be refunded and the granting of any allowances, incentives or benefits not authorized by any laws or statutory provision be stopped. Moreover, we recommended that expenditures be classified and charged in accordance with the nature and character of the expense.

21.1 We recommended that the accountant verify the completeness and legitimacy of the documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 before certifying in the disbursement vouchers. We further recommended that the variance between the AIR and AR/PAR be refunded
23.1 Require the Administrative Officials authorizing the travels and the Drivers to properly accomplish and serially number the Driver’s Trip Tickets;

24.1 We recommended that the LCE require the Human Resource Management Officer, Department Heads and Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA, pertaining to net take home pay in order to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough support for their daily needs until the next pay day.
25.1 We recommended that management cause the refund of the excess of the 2013 PEI in the total amount of ₱1,106,000.00.

26.1 We recommended strict adherence to the provision of Section 9.1 and 9.2 of Budget Circular No. 2012-3. The amount of ₱1,856,000.00, being unauthorized should be refunded by the municipal officials and employees who were recipients of the benefits.
27.1 We recommended that Management stop paying RATA to the former SB Secretary and Municipal Treasurer until they actually assume and perform the functions of the position.

We recommended that management instruct the municipal accountant for the timely submission of the bank reconciliation statements and immediately effect adjustments/corrections in the book balance from prior period’s reconciliation statements.

.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct 	the submission of the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team using the prescribed format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012


We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the transfer of the amount due to the Calamity Fund to present reliable balance in the Financial Statement and so that the amount may be used for the purpose for which it was intended.

observations.recommendation:

the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a duly certified list of Real Property Taxpayers showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and

the Municipal Assessor to furnish the Treasurer with the latest valuations of property of each taxpayer;

Stop the practice of charging to the LDRRMF expenses which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities;

observations.recommendation: 1.15	We recommended that a) the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (i) create an Inventory Committee, consisting of a representative from the Office of the Municipal Mayor, General Services Office (GSO) and Accounting Office; (ii) require each department head to fast track the inventory taking of all properties under their office and submit the inventory report to the committee for validation and verification of the correctness and existence of the property and; (iii) in the event that an inventory committee is created, require it to (1) check the condition of the property, and any damaged, obsolete and unserviceable property shall be reported on the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable and Waste Material Report (2) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and; (3) immediately perform the required disposal of unserviceable property b) the GSO/Municipal Treasurer (i) maintain Property Cards per class and category of property, plant and equipment indicating acquisition date, the brief descriptions; (ii) revisit  previous years’ records and reconcile with the information at the accounting office, and (iii) update the property issue slip/PAR to fix the custodianship of the property; and c) The Municipal Accountant to drop the cost of unserviceable and obsolete properties items from the books following the process prescribed by the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in Local Government under COA Circular No. 92-386.

2.12		We recommended that a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer observe proper accounting and reporting of RPT and SET as prescribed under the NGAS; b) Municipal Treasurer reconcile his records with that of the Municipal Assessor to determine the appropriate amount of taxes for recording purposes; and c) MTO to submit a Certified List of Taxpayers to support the receivables recorded by the Accounting Office.

3.9 	We recommended that the management instruct: a) the Municipal Treasurer to locate the missing disbursement voucher with supporting documents amounting to ₽303,803.57  and submit them immediately the Municipal Accountant for proper recording and reporting; and b) instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to improve their control measures in managing disbursement vouchers and documents in consideration of the controls embedded in the procedures contained in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

4.8	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Planning Development Officer, continue to (a) formulate and invest in high-impact capital expenditures projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund; and (b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the projects to maximize the developmental benefits of the intended beneficiaries.

5.12    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Head of LDRRMO (i) to prepare LDRRMP; (ii) to monitor the monthly utilization of LDRRMF and the corresponding Accomplishment Report; and (iii) to observe strictly the laws rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund to ensure that the desired services and benefits are delivered to the constituents before, during and after disaster/calamities; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare, certify and submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in prescribed format under Annex E of COA Circular No. 2014-002.
6.5	We require the Property Offices of both the LGU and the DepEd to coordinate and strengthen the controls on assets accountability and accurately monitor the custodianship of government property.

7.8 We recommended that management allocate the mandatory minimum appropriation or funding for LCPC and instruct the council members to (i) ensure the proper implementation of the formulated PPAs in accordance with the above law and regulations; and (ii) consider outsourcing or partnership with private organizations and entities that provide sponsorship or financial assistance for the implementation of LCPC PPAs.

8.6	While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU to support gender equality and women empowerment, we recommend that the Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group (i) Endeavor to capacitate themselves through attendance to trainings under GFPS for more responsive planning; and (ii) Facilitate the establishment of the GAD Database to aid in gender analysis and provide baseline data on the status of women and men in the LGU.

9.10	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 relevant to appropriation, entitlement, release, and use of the confidential funds.

10.14	We recommended that Municipal Mayor a) relieve immediately the attending clerk (Ms. Tiglao) from collecting functions; b) require Ms. Tiglao to restitute the amount of P1,842.50 representing the difference between the amount of receivable collected (P1,902.50) and the altered amount of P60.00 reported as collection during the day; and c) direct the Municipal Treasurer to instruct all division heads as well as the Liquidating Officer to carefully (i) check and review the accuracy of the collections being remitted during the day and the corresponding accountability for accountable forms prior to receipt and signing of the Report of Collections and Deposits in accordance with the above accounting regulations and (ii) depart from delegating collection functions to unqualified employee or officer.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all collectors to remit intact their daily collections to avoid loss, misplacement or unwarranted appropriation for personal expenses or benefits.  Likewise, we also recommend that the collectors exercise care and diligence in performing their job, especially in filling up and issuance of official receipts and in reporting their accountability for accountable forms in the Report of Collections and Deposits.
12.5 	We recommended that management (i) secure the original OR/CR of the subject vehicles, and; (ii) coordinate with the dealers and LTO for the immediate registration of the motor vehicles.

13.17  We reiterate our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (a) coordinate the efforts of the public and private sectors and implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; (b)  work on the promulgation and implementation of “No Littering” Ordinance; (c) maximize the operation of the MRF and implement the Municipality’s plan of producing organic fertilizer and adopt specific revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its Solid Waste Management Plan; and (d) encourage the full participation of the barangays in the implementation of the R.A. No. 9003.

14.6	We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR in the procurement activities and submit immediately the above required documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, together with the Municipal Engineer, (i) to make a careful planning and extensive study of each and every project to be undertaken by the Municipality to ensure that government funds are expended judiciously and government property are protected or safeguarded, and (ii) perform appropriate action to deter the disparagement of the equipment’s value.
16.1	We recommended that all taxes withheld be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period.
17.3	We recommended the management to continuously remit the GSIS premiums within the prescribed period.
18.2	We recommended that Philhealth premiums deducted be fully remitted intact within the prescribed period provided for under the aforementioned law.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that:
the Inventory Committee conduct the complete physical count of all PPE accounts of the Municipality so that the corresponding reports can be submitted to the Audit Team;
the Municipal Accountant reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made; and
the Municipal Treasurer make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the notes to financial statements and submit general ledger and subsidiary ledgers for all funds in accordance with the abovementioned regulations.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request for the authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off dormant receivable accounts pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Management adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 10121 and see to it that programs and projects of the Municipality identified in the DRRM Plan were fully implemented in order to strengthen the preparedness and readiness of the municipality in case of calamity.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the transfer of unexpended balance of LDRRMF to Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere strictly to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations in the conduct of procurement. Submit copies of government contracts and all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same so that the management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
We recommended that the Municipality allocate no less than 1% of the total budget appropriation in compliance to the above provisions of DILG MC No. 2012-120 and Section 15 of R.A. 9344.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to abide with the regulations of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 in submission of the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of recording transactions charged to the GAD Fund and that the GAD Focal Point Person coordinate with the Accounting Department and reconcile the draft accomplishment report with accounting records before it is finalized for submission to offices concerned.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer to discontinue the practice of including MOOE as part of the Continuing Appropriations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


a. Require the Municipal Accountant  and  the  Inventory  Committee  to
   reconcile the discrepancy between the records and reports  and  make
   the necessary adjustments in the book of accounts;


b. Direct the GSO and the Inventory Committee  to  conduct  a  complete
   physical count of the PPE and to maintain Property Cards; and


c. Require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries
   to remove from the books unserviceable PPEs,  provided  that  proper
   modes of disposal were observed.

We recommended that the Management appropriate funds and the  Municipal
Assessor’s Office be directed to exert effort in titling all parcels of
land owned by the Municipal Government. The Accounting Office to set up
and maintain complete accounting records and the Municipal  Treasurer’s
Office to keep on file complete records of real properties.

We also recommended that the offices concerned conduct an inventory  of
all the parcels of land owned by the Municipality  and  reconcile  with
the records in the books  for  proper  presentation  in  the  Financial
Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  OIC-Municipal
Treasurer  to  deposit  all  collections  intact  with  the  authorized
depository bank daily or  not  later  than  the  next  banking  day  as
required under existing laws, rules and regulations;

We also recommended that the Management exert efforts and/or  avail  of
possible legal remedies with  regards  to  the  accountability  of  the
previous Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive demand the liquidation of
the unliquidated cash advances; direct the establishment  of  a  strong
internal control and create a policy or standard operating procedure in
the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances which  provides
for the  sanctions/penalties  for  non-compliance  thereon  subject  to
existing relevant laws, rules and regulations; and strictly observe the
provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant should not  allow
the grant of new cash advance  if  liquidation  of  the  previous  cash
advance is not submitted.  Regular  monitoring  and  reporting  of  the
status of unliquidated cash advances shall be  prepared  and  furnished
the Local Chief Executive for his information and appropriate action.

We further recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and  concerned
municipal employees conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis
and validation of the cash advances,  determine  long  outstanding  and
dormant receivable accounts and prepare necessary reports together with
the supporting documents for the  possible  request  for  write-off  as
indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipality  stop  the  practice  of  charging
expenses to the 20% Development Fund which cater only to the  needs  of
specific individuals and/or associations and do not partake the  nature
of investment/capital expenditures.

We also recommended that the Municipal officials strictly adhere to the
guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of  the  DILG
and DBM in the appropriation and utilization  of  the  20%  Development
Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended that the Management:


a. Stop the practice of charging to the LDRRMF expenses which  are  not
   related to and do not support disaster risk management activities;


b. Allocate a budget for the administrative expenses  under  the  LDRRM
   Office;


c. Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under the  pertinent  sections
   of JMC No. 2013-1  in  the  appropriation  and  utilization  of  the
   LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  OIC-Municipal
Budget Officer to allocate 1% of the  IRA  for  the  strengthening  and
implementation of programs, projects and activities of the LCPC for the
promotion of children’s welfare and protection in the locality.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  direct  the  GAD  Focal
Person and other responsible officials to conduct gender  analysis  and
assessment in  identifying  gender  issues  and  appropriate  programs,
projects and activities (PPAs) and strictly adhere to the provisions of
JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions  of  JMC  No.
2017-3  in  order  to  timely  provide  the  basic  services   to   the
constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:


a. Intensify the tax collection campaign through issuance of  follow-up
   letters/notices of tax collection to delinquent taxpayers to  remind
   them of their tax obligations;

b. Coordinate/make representations with the Punong Barangays  and  seek
   their assistance to personally deliver the above notices/letters  to
   concerned individuals in the locality  and  advise  them  to  settle
   their accounts; and

c.  Seek  advice  of  the  Provincial  Treasurer’s   Office   regarding
   applicable remedies on the  Comprehensive  Agrarian  Reform  Program
   (CARP) covered properties.

We recommended that management instruct the municipal accountant for the timely submission of the bank reconciliation statements and immediately effect adjustments/corrections in the book balance from prior period’s reconciliation statements.

We recommended that the municipal accountant review the existence, validity and accuracy of the outstanding accounts payable and if found to have no valid claims against the local government  or are not covered by perfected contract on record, cause the reversion thereof to the unappropriated surplus. Likewise, we recommended that the municipal accounting office enjoin the maintenance and safekeeping of both manual and electronic files of accounting records as a control measure against any loss.

We have reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management directs the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the erroneous fund transfers and reconcile the reciprocal accounts. Likewise, we enjoin management to require immediate liquidation of the fund transfers made.

We have reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the the Agency Head to instruct the Municipal Accountant to extend earnest efforts to locate the records containing the journal entries on the recorded government equity and verify the breakdown to be transferred to the General Fund. In addition, he should direct the accountant to send confirmation letters to identified source agencies to provide basis to reconcile the equity account with adjustment in appropriate account(s).

We recommended that the management direct the LDRRMO to include and implement in the LDRRM Investment Plan the deficiencies noted relative to compliance on the minimum standards for disaster preparedness. Likewise, we enjoined collaborative discussion thereon with the Sangguniang Bayan on the implementation and augmentation of the LDRRM Fund, if need be.

We recommended that management prepare plans, programs and activities specifically for the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and implement the same for the benefit and enjoyment of constituent- children.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer comply and limit their functions to those prescribed by the Treasury Operations Manual, so as to ensure segregation of duties and uphold the system of checks and balances.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor
and other officials stop the
practice of utilizing 20%
Development Fund and 5% LDRRM Fund
to other purposes for which the
funds were created. We further
recommended that the preparation of
the Annual Investment Plan should be
compliant with the provisions
enumerated in DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, RA
No. 10121 and DILG Memorandum
Circular No. 2012 – 73 (A) and
ensure the utilization of the Fund
in accordance with the plan.

In view of the above, we recommended
and management agreed with the
following:

Observe strictly the laws, rules and
regulations on the use of calamity
fund to ensure that the desired
services and benefits can be
delivered to the constituents in
times of calamities;

Instruct the MDRRMO to utilize the
LDRRMF only for the specific purpose
for which it has been appropriated,
particularly programs and activities
that support pre-disaster and
disaster risk management as mandated
under R.A. No. 10121; and

Instruct the MDRRMO to strictly
comply with the reporting
requirements stipulated in RA No.
1021 and COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended and management agreed
that the Local School Board (LSB)
strictly comply with the provisions
of R.A. No. 5447, R.A. No. 7160, LOI
1462 and relevant DepEd-DBM-DILG
Joint Circulars in the preparation of
the SEF Annual Budget and
identify the priority expenditures
pursuant thereto treating on the
utilization of the Special Education
Fund to avoid disallowances in
audit.

We recommended and management agreed
that the Bids and Awards Committee
members:

Thoroughly read and examine the
documents submitted by the bidders
to determine whether the same were
the documents called for in the
Instruction to Bidders or not;

Consider as “failed” all bids that
are not compliant with the documents
for eligibility and technical
requirements; and

Keep abreast with the amendments on
the Implementing Rules and
Regulations of R.A. 9184 and
immediately implement the same.

We recommended and management agreed
that the provisions of the Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations
of the Republic Act No. 9184, or the
Act providing for the Modernization,
Standardization and Regulation of
Procurement Activities of the
Government be strictly adhered to by
the Municipality.

We recommended and management agreed
that the Municipal Accountant
require the Supplier/Contractor to
post the necessary warranty security
in any acceptable form for goods
delivered and infrastructures
implemented as prescribed under
Section 62 of the IRR-A of RA No.
9184 before final payment is made in
order that the Municipality is
assured of compensation in case of
faulty or defective projects.

We recommended and management agreed
that the management require the
local SB Members to submit documents
to support actual performance of
work for their claim for RATA in
conformity with the aforementioned
rules and regulations.

We recommended and management agreed
that the BAC adhere to the provision
of Section 37.4.2 of R.A. No. 9184
as regards to posting of copies of
Notice to Proceed and the approved
contracts on PhilGEPS and website of
the municipality, if any.

We recommended and management agreed
that the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly
comply with the regulation on the
rendition of accounts and financial
reports in order not to delay the
verification and post-audit
activities; and (b) observe the due
dates of submission of the required
financial reports and documents to
the Audit Team.

We recommended and management agreed
that the Bids and Awards Committee
invite observers to the procurement
process in accordance with Sec. 13.1
and 13.2 of the RIRR of R.A. No.    9184 and ensure that observers from
both the  private sector  and the
NGO are invited to witness the
bidding.

We recommend that the Management through the GAD Council:

Ensure adequate allocation for Gender and Development fund and see to it that the fund is optimally utilized; and

Establish GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Item 5.1 of the above-mentioned JMC; and
observations.reason: As per accomplishment report submitted by GAD Focal person, the GAD Budget of the municipality for CY 2017 was fully utilized showing GAD expenditures totaling P2,145,625.12 against total budget amounting to P2,166,567.18. However, based on the List submitted by the Municipal Accountant, total GAD expenditure was P1,543,990, with a discrepancy of P601,635.12.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor stop the grant of loans to Municipal Officials and Employees as it is contrary to  Section 305 (b) of R.A. 7160 also known as Local Government Code instead utilize the fund for the implementation of programs/projects that would benefit the entire public
observations.managementAction: The Municipality stopped providing loans to officials and employees for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with paragraph e, Section 4 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 10 of the same Manual, Volume II.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant submitted  copy of SL for General Fund only just before the exit conference started.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management allocate one percent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment for Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and strictly observe other pertinent provisions of RA 9443 for compliance
observations.reason: The Municipality  appropriated only .08% of its IRA for LCPC, short by .02%, therefore, programs and activities for children may not be fully attained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances per books with those per bank to determine what caused the huge amount of discrepancy. Prepare the necessary adjustments in the books for all errors committed in order to give a reliable data of Cash in Bank balances in the Financial Statements
observations.reason: Before the exit conference started on March 23, 2018, the Municipal Accountant  submitted  the General Ledger for general fund only, thus, the Audit Team could not verify the accuracy of CIB bank balance per book for all funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor exercise his power to recover the public funds from the accountable employee, as mandated in the aforecited provision of RA 7160.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, the former OIC-Municipal Treasurer deposited P275,000.00  under various deposit slip with JEV no. 17-01-058 leaving a balance of P135,317.37.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the strict and uniform implementation of the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to all officials and employees of the municipality;

Require all accountable officers and employees to settle/liquidate all their outstanding cash advances by sending confirmation, notification or demand letters. 

In case of failure to settle/liquidate their cash advances, cause or order the withholding of the payment of their salaries as provided for under Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and impose the sanctions provided under the penal provision of Section 128 of PD No. 1445 and/or study the possibility of filing the appropriate charges to enforce settlement thereof in accordance with Section 9.3.3 of the same Circular and Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code; and 		
Require an Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities, pursuant to Sections 200 and 256, Vol. I of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual from all personnel who seek retirement, resignation, transfer or claim for death benefits in order that all accountabilities could be appropriately settled.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, unliquidated cash advances reduced to P732,685.02. The Municipal Accountant issued Demand Letters to the Accountable Officers with unliquidated cash advances copy furnished the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit immediately the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms together with the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms for January to December 2015.  The concerned accountable officers should comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume II.  Same care should be instituted on the handling and accounting of accountable forms just like in the control of cash.
observations.reason: Verification of the Report of Collections and Deposits revealed that the  Consolidated Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms every month prepared by the Mun. Treasurer  were attached to the RCD.   However   the individual RAAF of the Collectors to support the CRAAF were not attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the CY-2015 SAAOB and SCBAA to prevent further actions, as may be deemed necessary, to be taken against him.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Budget Officer submitted SCBAA for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Direct the GSO and the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the PPE; and
Reclassify the amount of ₱546,063.49 by debiting the Other Payables and crediting Trust Liabilities; and

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445, to fully liquidate/collect all long outstanding cash advances, and prepare demand letters for the immediate settlement of cash advances of employees who are still active in the service.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Accountant demand for the liquidation or refund of the cash advances of the accountable officers and inform the employers of those who transferred to another municipality or other government agencies.
We further recommended that all liquidation documents be submitted immediately to the Accounting Office for proper recording and to avoid unliquidated cash advances as of year end.
Establish a GAD Database as source of information for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;
Conduct gender analysis and assessment in identifying gender issues and appropriate PPAs; and
Ensure submission of the Annual GPB and GAD AR to the DILG Regional Office VI for review and approval to ensure compliance with the guidelines.
Allocate a budget for the administrative expenses under the LDRRM Office; and

Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under the pertinent sections of RA No. 10121, NDRRMC, DBM and the DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF.

The canvasser shall instruct the supplier or its representative that the quotation should not exceed the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) as indicated in the PR to avail the most advantageous price for the government;
Properly date and number the POs for systematic monitoring of purchased transactions;
State delivery terms and conditions for clarity of agreement between the parties;

We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials strictly adhere to the rules and regulations of RA No. 5447, Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and Section 3 of Executive Order No. 356, s. 2004 on the utilization of the Special Education Fund. 

We further recommended that the Local School Board shall prioritize programs, projects and activities as stipulated in the aforementioned provisions in the preparation of the Annual School Board Budget for the SEF.

We recommended that fiscal responsibility in all financial transactions of the Municipality shall be exercised by the concerned officers and employees by ensuring that sufficient/relevant documents are attached to the DVs to show that expenses are for public purpose and that the claims are valid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the employees responsible to comply with Sections 17.2 and 18 of RA No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations. 

We also recommended that all applicable provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR be strictly followed by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality shall include in the Report of PPAs, the list of all programs and activities with ongoing implementation and those to be implemented during the year, and diligently adhere to the provisions of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the financial assistance extended to all the officials and employees, including casuals, contractual and job order workers, of the Municipal Government of Concepcion be refunded and the granting of any allowances, incentives or benefits not authorized by any laws or statutory provision be stopped. Moreover, we recommended that expenditures be classified and charged in accordance with the nature and character of the expense.

We have recommended to management to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the required reports and accounts observing the provisions of aforementioned law and regulations. Likewise, we enjoin management to provide personnel augmentation in the accounting and treasury office to handle the preparation and submission of the reports

The Accounting Dept. took necessary measures to fast track the preparation/submission of the financial reports.

Augmentation of personnel in the acctg office has been made fort he purpose of the Preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee through the Municipal General Services Officer, to supply all the necessary information needed in the report to come up with a more accurate and reliable report.

We also recommended that the Inventory Committee coordinate with the Accounting Office for those items which the General Services Office does not have any  information for an accurate and complete report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the accountable officer make representation with the Office of the Mayor for the repair of the equipment so it could be used during rescue operations.  However, the cost of repair should not be more than 30% of the current market price of the same or similar equipment.

We recommended also that the Local Chief Executive ascertain if due diligence was exercised by the accountable officer when the boat was brought to Subic, Zambales for testing/training.  Otherwise, the accountable officer should be held liable for the equipment.
