observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management be guided by Item 5 of JMC 2013-1 in the allocation/ utilization of the LDRRMF with the goal and objective to strengthen the capacities of the LGU, together with the different barangays, to build the disaster resilience of communities, to institutionalize arrangements and measures for reducing disaster risks, and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels. 


We recommended and management concurred to instruct the LDRRMO to strictly comply with the reporting requirements stipulated in R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), as chairman of the MDRRMC, cause the selection and accreditation of CSOs in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the aforementioned M.C.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive initiate the creation of a GAD Focal Point System in order to promote women’s human rights and eliminate gender discrimination in the systems, structures, policies, programs, processes and procedures of the Municipality, if any.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to carefully review during post-qualification all documents submitted by the Lowest Calculated Bidders in the future biddings of the Municipality to ensure that they comply with and are responsive to all requirements and conditions of the biddings.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (a) coordinate the efforts of the public and private sectors and implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; (b)  work on the promulgation and implementation of “No Littering” Ordinance; (c) maximize the operation of the MRF and implement the Municipality’s plan of producing organic fertilizer from biodegradable wastes and adopt specific revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its Solid Waste Management Plan; and (d) encourage the full participation of the barangays in the implementation of the R.A. No. 9003.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts of the Municipality so that the corresponding report can be submitted to the Audit Team;  and

Thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.

In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully analyze and review the accounting entries made on the accounts affected, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect appropriate adjustments thereof to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the agency’s financial position.

observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for not implementing audit recommendations .

No. 9184.

Submit lacking documents for the subject procurement to prove validity, regularity and propriety on subject transactions.

observations.recommendation:

4.1 We recommended that the Local School Board prepare the SEF Budget in accordance with RA No. 5447 and the local government unit stop the practice of charging expenses that are not within the programs/activities of the RA.

5.1 We recommended that fiscal responsibility in all financial transactions of the Municipality shall be exercised by the concerned officers and employees by ensuring that sufficient/relevant documents are attached to the DVs to show that expenses are for public purpose and that the claims are valid.

observations.recommendation: 6.2 We also recommended that all applicable provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR be strictly followed by the Municipality.

13.1 We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan strictly observe the PS limitation provided in Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and item 9.0 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3 in the grant of PEI. We further recommended that the PEI granted to each of the officers and employees of the LGU- Concepcion be refunded.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in the CY 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: 16.1 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO to strictly adhere with the provisions of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and must submit regularly to the COA Audit Team the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of the MDRRMF to ascertain the correctness of its utilization.
observations.managementAction: There is no report being submitted on the Utilization and Sources of MDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that management facilitate the processing of documents needed for the issuance by the Registry of Deeds of the Transfer Certificate of Title of the mentioned land/s in the municipality’s name. Exert effort to retrieve the documents needed and coordinate with the concerned government agencies to facilitate titling of the lots. Likewise, we recommended and management agreed that the municipal accountant recognize the lots in the books of accounts as soon as the required documents are submitted to the accounting office to reflect the corrected balance of land account and fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended and management agreed that management pre-terminates its existing loans to minimize expenditure of additional interest expense and in the future, in-house fund resources be considered prior to availing outside fund resources.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the designated Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain reports/property records such as RPCPPE and Property Cards for proper monitoring of properties purchased, donated, transferred or disposed so that reconciliation of accounting records with physical count will be made. Likewise, facilitate the conduct of complete physical count of all the properties of the Municipality with the Auditor as witness.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor and other officials stop the practice of utilizing 20% Development Fund and 5% LDRRM Fund to other purposes for which the funds were created. We further recommended that the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan should be compliant with the provisions enumerated in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, RA No. 10121 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012 – 73 (A) and ensure the utilization of the Fund in accordance with the plan.
The management said that the 20% Economic Development Plan and 5% LDRRM Fund will be strictly utilized in accordance with the plan starting CY 2017. They will discontinue the practice of using these funds to other purposes.

In view of the above, we recommended and management agreed with the following:
Observe strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the use of calamity fund to ensure that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents in times of calamities;
Instruct the MDRRMO to utilize the LDRRMF only for the specific purpose for which it has been appropriated, particularly programs and activities that support pre-disaster and disaster risk management as mandated under R.A. No. 10121; and
Instruct the MDRRMO to strictly comply with the reporting requirements stipulated in RA No. 1021 and COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local School Board (LSB) strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 5447, R.A. No. 7160, LOI 1462 and relevant DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto treating on the utilization of the Special Education Fund to avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended and management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee members:
Thoroughly read and examine the documents submitted by the bidders to determine whether the same were the documents called for in the Instruction to Bidders or not;
Consider as “failed” all bids that are not compliant with the documents for eligibility and technical requirements; and
Keep abreast with the amendments on the Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 and immediately implement the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion; and (b) ensure that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that these projects could serve their purposes to the intended beneficiaries.
The part of the fund was set aside as counterpart of BUB but no fund was downloaded by the National Government. Several projects are dependent with the National Government, hence the projects were hold/unimplemented. This 2017, several project will be implemented as per the management.

We recommended and management agreed that the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184, or the Act providing for the Modernization, Standardization and Regulation of Procurement Activities of the Government be strictly adhered to by the Municipality.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant require the Supplier/Contractor to post the necessary warranty security in any acceptable form for goods delivered and infrastructures implemented as prescribed under Section 62 of the IRR-A of RA No. 9184 before final payment is made in order that the Municipality is assured of compensation in case of faulty or defective projects.

We recommended and management agreed that the management require the local SB Members to submit documents to support actual performance of work for their claim for RATA in conformity with the aforementioned rules and regulations.

We recommended and management agreed that the BAC adhere to the provision of Section 37.4.2 of R.A. No. 9184 as regards to posting of copies of Notice to Proceed and the approved contracts on PhilGEPS and website of the municipality, if any.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended and management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee invite observers to the procurement process in accordance with Sec. 13.1 and 13.2 of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 and ensure that observers from both the  private sector  and the NGO are invited to witness the bidding.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require all municipal officials involved in the implementation of GAD programs, projects and policies to improve the proficiencies of the GFPS and TWG by creating GAD Database and GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) coordinate the efforts of the public and private sectors and implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; (b)  work on the promulgation and implementation of “No Littering” Ordinance; (c) maximize the operation of the MRF and implement the Municipality’s plan of producing organic fertilizer from biodegradable wastes and adopt specific revenue-generating measures to promote and ensure the viability of its Solid Waste Management Plan; and (d) encourage the full participation of the barangays in the implementation of the R.A. No. 9003.
The management said that they will review the no littering ordinance. Also, they will purchase fifteen mini dump trucks to collect to different barangays and two dump trucks to transport the waste. All barangays in CY 2017 will be required to have their own MRFs.
We recommended that management continue remitting the taxes withheld as required by the BIR Revenue Regulations.

We recommended that:
the Supply Officer prepare Property Ledger Cards for each category of PPE to facilitate proper monitoring of the Municipality’s properties.
the Inventory Committee conduct the required physical count of all Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts of the Municipality so that the corresponding report can be submitted to the Audit Team;
the Municipal Accountant reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.  In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances per books with those per bank to determine what caused the huge amount of discrepancy. Prepare the necessary adjustments in the books for all errors committed in order to give a reliable data of Cash in Bank balances in the Financial Statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exercise his power to impose collection of the unsettled accountability from the accountable employee, as mandated in the aforecited provision.

We recommended that:
the Municipal Accountant set up RPT/SET Receivables in accordance with Section 20 of NGAS Manual, Volume I. Analyze the unreconciled discrepancies in the RPT and SET receivables and take up the correction of the noted differences to present accurate and reliable data in the Financial Statements.
the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a duly certified list of Real Property Taxpayers showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and
the Municipal Assessor furnish the Treasurer with the latest valuations of property of each taxpayer;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the grant of loans to Municipal officials and employees as it is contrary to  Section 305 (b) of     R.A. 7160 also known as Local Government Code instead, utilize the fund for the implementation of programs/projects that would benefit the entire public.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team using the prescribed format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the transfer of the amount due to the Calamity Fund to present reliable balance in the Financial Statement and so that the amount may be used for the purpose for which it was intended.
We recommended that the Management see to it that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund is optimally utilized for its intended purpose.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursements be supported with complete documentation as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommended that the Management submit written explanation relative to the deficiency noted. See to it that all subsequent disbursements be supported with appropriations.

We recommended that the Gender and Development Council:
ensure Gender and Development fund is optimally utilized for its purpose; and
establish GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Item 5.1 of the above-mentioned JMC; and
We recommended that the Local School Board prepare and submit Annual SEF budget by strictly observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF allocation and utilization.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with Section 4 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 10 of the same Manual, Volume II.

We recommended that the Management adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1 in planning and implementing programs/ projects/ activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents; and
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert to 20% development fund the total amount of P221,710.00, representing the payment for programs/projects not in line with the purpose to which the fund was intended, and to maintain subsidiary ledgers for 20% development fund.

We recommended that the Management adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1 in planning and implementing programs/projects/ activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents; and
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert to 20% development fund the total amount of P221,710.00, representing the payment for programs/projects not in line with the purpose to which the fund was intended, and to maintain subsidiary ledgers for 20% development fund.

We recommended that the Management adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1 in planning and implementing programs/ projects/ activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents; and
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert to 20% development fund the total amount of P221,710.00, representing the payment for programs/projects not in line with the purpose to which the fund was intended, and to maintain subsidiary ledgers for 20% development fund.

We recommended that the management allocate one percent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment for Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and strictly observe other pertinent provisions of RA 9443 for compliance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to reconcile the discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments in the book of accounts;
Direct the GSO and the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the PPE; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to remove from the books unserviceable PPEs, provided that proper modes of disposal were observed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall make necessary adjusting entries to rectify the misstatement by debiting the account – Receivables - Disallowances/Charges and crediting Due from Officers and Employees in the amount of ₱1,449,998.85.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Reconcile the balance of the Subsidiary Ledger and the Financial Statement by identifying the cause of the difference and make necessary adjusting entries;
Reclassify the amount of ₱546,063.49 by debiting the Other Payables and crediting Trust Liabilities; and
Record transactions for liabilities not falling under any of the specific payable accounts in the Other Payables pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445, to fully liquidate/collect all long outstanding cash advances, and prepare demand letters for the immediate settlement of cash advances of employees who are still active in the service.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Accountant demand for the liquidation or refund of the cash advances of the accountable officers and inform the employers of those who transferred to another municipality or other government agencies.
We further recommended that all liquidation documents be submitted immediately to the Accounting Office for proper recording and to avoid unliquidated cash advances as of year end.

We recommended that:
The LEE refund the amount of Fund Transfers to the General Fund of the Municipality;
The Municipality consider the action/s to be taken as enumerated in Section 3.4 of LBC No. 100; and
The Municipality shall conduct feasibility study to determine whether the operation of the LEE shall be discontinued and consider the reintegration of its functions and services into the regular LGU operations.

We recommended that the Municipality observe the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and Section 1 of LBC No. 98 by appropriating and expending personal services within the prescribed 45% limit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person and other responsible officials to:
Establish a GAD Database as source of information for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;
Conduct gender analysis and assessment in identifying gender issues and appropriate PPAs; and
Ensure submission of the Annual GPB and GAD AR to the DILG Regional Office VI for review and approval to ensure compliance with the guidelines.

We recommended that the Management:
Stop the practice of charging to the LDRRMF expenses which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities;
Allocate a budget for the administrative expenses under the LDRRM Office; and
Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under the pertinent sections of RA No. 10121, NDRRMC, DBM and the DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of charging expenses to the 20% Development Fund which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures.
We also recommended that the Municipal officials strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials strictly adhere to the rules and regulations of RA No. 5447, Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and Section 3 of Executive Order No. 356, s. 2004 on the utilization of the Special Education Fund.
We further recommended that the Local School Board shall prioritize programs, projects and activities as stipulated in the aforementioned provisions in the preparation of the Annual School Board Budget for the SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2015 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/offices and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.
We also recommended that henceforth, the LGU of Concepcion, shall within sixty (60) days from their receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

We have recommended to management to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the required reports and accounts observing the provisions of aforementioned law and regulations. Likewise, we enjoin management to provide personnel augmentation in the accounting and treasury office to handle the preparation and submission of the reports.

We have recommended to the Agency Head to instruct the Municipal Accountant to extend earnest efforts to locate the records containing the journal entries on the recorded government equity and verify the breakdown to be transferred to the General Fund. In addition, he should direct the accountant to send confirmation letters to identified source agencies to provide basis to reconcile the equity account with adjustment inappropriate account(s).

We have recommended to management that he directs the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the erroneous fund transfers and reconcile the reciprocal accounts. Likewise, we enjoin management to require immediate liquidation of the fund transfers made.

We recommended that management (a) comply with the guidelines on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; (b) under no circumstance shall it grant additional cash advances to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; (c) inquire from the COA Central Office on the status of the liquidation of the Confidential Fund in order to have a basis for closing the account; and (d) exert all efforts to enforce the settlement of the unliquidated cash advances of DepEd  employees so that the long outstanding cash advances under SEF in the total amount of P23,634.00 be settled.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the accounts of the municipality as required by pertinent regulations for timely evaluation and audit of the transactions of the Municipality. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer or the department head concerned facilitate the implementation of various programs and projects, the funds of which have already been transferred to the LGU in order for the constituents to avail and use the benefits and services that could be derived therefrom at the earliest possible time.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management faithfully comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 to effectively control fuel, gasoline and oil expenditures to avoid wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption by implementing the following corrective measures: 

Exercise tighter control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets, a summary of which shall be made at the end of the month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels for audit purposes; 

Management submit to the Auditor the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels to the Auditor for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period; and 

Stop giving fuel to privately owned motor vehicles; ensure that only government-owned motor vehicles are issued gasoline for official business.

We recommended that management comply with the minimum limit of P3,000.00 for employees’ monthly net take home pay pursuant to Section 48 of GAA 2015. We likewise recommended that statutory deductions be given preference over other deductions.
We recommended that the Peace and Order Council be judicious in the preparation and/or review of the POP of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive endeavor to maximize the use of the 20% of the Internal Revenue Allotment for development projects and ensure that the fund is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes to uplift the well-being of the constituents.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to properly identify and carefully plan the projects needed by the constituents to avoid reprogramming of projects and balances during the year.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that local officials ensure that projects and activities being paid for out of the Special Education Fund are within the projects and activities contained under R.A. 5447 and among the priority projects listed under Sec. 100(c), R.A. No.7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and all personnel involved in planning to carefully plan the implementation of the municipal programs, projects and activities (PPA) by setting a time frame or period within which the PPAs should be implemented so that before the year ends, all projects have been bidded and implemented.  In this manner, all expenses incurred during the year could be taken up in the accounts of that year.
We recommended that management comply with the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its implementing rules and regulations specifically Section 53.5 of the revised IRR.
We recommended that the LGU officials comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to avoid suspension of the transactions and the penalty prescribed by law under 4.1 of the circular.

observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer deposited the collections leaving only a balance of P5,581.84 at year end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the strict and uniform implementation of the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to all officials and employees of the municipality;

Require all accountable officers and employees to settle/liquidate all their outstanding cash advances by sending confirmation, notification or demand letters. 

In case of failure to settle/liquidate their cash advances, cause or order the withholding of the payment of their salaries as provided for under Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and impose the sanctions provided under the penal provision of Section 128 of PD No. 1445 and/or study the possibility of filing the appropriate charges to enforce settlement thereof in accordance with Section 9.3.3 of the same Circular and Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code; and	
	Require an Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities, pursuant to Sections 200 and 256, Vol. I of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual from all personnel who seek retirement, resignation, transfer or claim for death benefits in order that all accountabilities could be appropriately settled.
observations.reason: The concerned accountable officers settled  their  cash advances, resulting to more than 50% of last year balance were liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the LGUs bank accounts together with the paid checks, debit/credit memos for verification as provided for under Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit all monthly and quarterly accounting and financial reports and their corresponding statements within the prescribed period as required under the above-mentioned provisions in order for the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the management of the deficiencies noted, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the schedules of the subject accounts showing the complete details or breakdown to facilitate the review of the correctness of the account balances.
observations.reason: Same observation was noted and included in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the CY-2015 SAAOB and SCBAA to prevent further actions, as may be deemed necessary, to be taken against him.

We recommended that management submit the Annual Procurement Plan for CY- 2015 in accordance with Section 7 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 so that timing, volume and cost of procurement are clearly defined. The management should design a well-planned program that would eliminate the use of emergency and/or personal canvass in the procurement of supplies, materials and services.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the proper appropriation of five percent (5%) of the estimated revenue from regular sources as its LDRRM Fund to support disaster risk management activities pursuant to the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of R.A. No. 10121 dated September 10, 2010.



We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team using the prescribed format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. 


We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere strictly with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Prepare the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected account in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.reason: The municipality’s appropriation for  LDRRMF for CY 2016  is in accordance with IRR of RA N0. 10212 dated 9/10/2010, however, the LDRRMFIP for its DRRM program and the Report on its utilization were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
1.1 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of PPE annually to prove its existence, determine the condition and update the valuation thru the following procedures:

Physically count all existing PPE and provide its description. Secure other information such as person accountable, date of purchase, brand, model, serial no., engine no., etc. and determine if unserviceable or not.

Based on the information gathered in procedure no. 1 determine if there are accounting records for these PPEs.  Create a PPELC for each PPE with accounting records or data, identify and determine its total cost and compute a separate depreciation schedule.

Identify the accountable employee for each PPE and issue a Property Acknowledgement Receipt.
observations.managementAction:
Partial physical count was conducted. 

Audit finding reiterated in the CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: 1.3 We also recommended that the Inventory Report shall be submitted as required by regulations.
2.1 We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant submit subsidiary ledgers or detailed schedules of her working papers on the conversion of accounts to ascertain its correctness.

3.1 We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445, to fully liquidate/collect all long outstanding cash advances, or prepare demand letters for the immediate settlement of those cash advances of accountable officers and employees who are still active in the service.
observations.managementAction: The balance of cash advance decreased. Reiterated in the CY 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: 6.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the employees responsible to comply with Sections 17.2 and 18 of RA No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Suspension already issued. The LGU already submitted the required documents and decision will be made after evaluation.

observations.recommendation: 7.1 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1) instruct the persons concerned to immediately look into the unimplemented development projects under the 20% Development Fund and determine causes of non-implementation; 2) formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement development projects; and 3) review the costing of projects programmed to conform to the present conditions.
7.2 We also recommended that implementing departments regularly monitor expenses against appropriation so as not to exceed the allotted budget.

8.1 We recommended that the LCE create its Municipal GFPS that shall spearhead the preparation of the annual GPB and GAD AR and observe strictly the provisions of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

9.1 We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: The LGU addressed the problem using the Materials Recovery Facility.

observations.recommendation: 10.1 We recommended that the Municipality shall include in the Report of PPAs, the list of all programs and activities with ongoing implementation and those to be implemented during the year, and diligently adhere to the provisions of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Not all PPAs are reported in the quarterly reports.

observations.recommendation: 11.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under existing regulations of the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: Collections of the present OIC – Municipal Treasurer were deposited regularly except for the undeposited collections of the previous Municipal Treasurer which are deducted from her salary.

observations.recommendation: 12.1 We recommended that the LCE (1) formulate, install and implement a sound internal control system in the management of cash; (2) direct the Municipal Treasurer to use the cash vault and install a security system for the safekeeping of collections; (3) designate  disbursing officers for cash payments; (4) grant cash advance only to a duly designated disbursing officer and ensure that there will be no transfer of cash advance from one accountable officer to another; and (5) order the municipal treasurer to perform his functions accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The LGU purchased a Cash Vault, installed a security system in the form of a CCTV Camera that is now in use and the LGU also installed grills on doors and windows.

observations.recommendation: 14.1 We recommended that the financial assistance extended to all the officials and employees, including casuals, contractual and job order workers, of the Municipal Government of Concepcion be refunded and the granting of any allowances, incentives or benefits not authorized by any laws or statutory provision be stopped. Moreover, we recommended that expenditures be classified and charged in accordance with the nature and character of the expense.
observations.managementAction: Officials and employees started refunding the amount thru payroll deduction.

observations.recommendation: 15.1 We recommended that the LGU-Concepcion (1) formulate and implement uniform standard operating procedures in procurement including the use of alternative modes consistent with the provisions of RA No. 9184, its Revised IRR, and other existing laws, rules and regulations; (2) verify, validate and ascertain licenses, permits and certificates of the supplier before awarding the contract; and (3) direct the responsible persons that verification and actual count of the goods delivered be made before signing the AIR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor a) require the Administrative Officials authorizing the travels and the Drivers to properly accomplish and serially number the Driver’s Trip Tickets; b) designate an LGU employee to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; c) instruct the concerned LGU employees to adopt the prescribed format of the Driver’s Trip Ticket, Requisition and Issue Voucher, Monthly Report of Official Travel, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and diligently observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: There are still improperly accomplished Drivers Trip Tickets and Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption  is not consistently submitted on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation: 17.2 We also recommended that the GSO stop the existing practice in the procurement of gasoline/fuel, oil and lubricant and observe the mode of procurement provided in RA No. 9184. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Treasurer check the information and amount provided in the OR issued by the supplier. We further recommended the installation of a sound internal control system in order to prevent wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures on fuel consumption.

18.1 We recommended that the LCE require the Human Resource Management Officer, Department Heads and Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA, pertaining to net take home pay in order to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough support for their daily needs until the next pay day.
observations.managementAction: Already implemented but same audit findings occur due to increase in minimum net take home pay from ₱3,000.00 to ₱4,000.00.

observations.recommendation: 19.1 We recommended that management cause the refund of the excess of the 2013 PEI in the total amount of ₱1,106,000.00.
observations.managementAction:
Notice of Disallowance was issued and appeal from the management was received.  Decision affirming the Disallowance on the CY 2013 PEI was rendered by COA RO VI.
Some of the employees refunded the amount of disallowance thru deduction from the retirement claim.

observations.recommendation: 20.1 We recommended strict adherence to the provision of Section 9.1 and 9.2 of Budget Circular No. 2012-3. The amount of ₱1,856,000.00, being unauthorized should be refunded by the municipal officials and employees who were recipients of the benefits.
observations.managementAction: Employees partially refunded the disallowance thru deduction from the monthly salary.

observations.recommendation: 21.1 We recommended that Management stop paying RATA to the former SB Secretary and Municipal Treasurer until they actually assume and perform back the functions of the position. Likewise, the amounts received by them since the time they were relieved from their original functions shall be refunded to the Municipality. Thus, the former SB Secretary shall refund the RATA he received from March 1 to December 31, 2011 for a total of ₱88,000.00 while the former Municipal Treasurer shall refund a total of ₱96,800.00  representing the RATA she received since February 1 to December 31, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Personnel not entitled to the payment of RATA were no longer paid but no refunds were made.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following to the Agency:

To strictly maintain and update Subsidiary Ledger and Property Card for each item of PPE.

To strictly conduct an actual physical inventory-taking of all PPE acquired at least once a year through the Inventory Committee.

To strictly prepare Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment in accordance with the NGAS Manual.

To properly place Property Identification Card/ Property Tag to each item of PPE.

To renew the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) every three years.

To send to actual trainings and seminars the Property Officer/Municipal Treasurer regarding the keeping, maintenance and disposition of PPE for proper guidance in property management.

To properly install strong internal control in safeguarding the government’s properties.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015
Finding No. 3 | Subsidiary ledgers were maintained and a physical inventory taking was conducted by the Municipal Accounting Office
observations.reason: Property Cards were already maintained and physical inventory taking was already conducted; however, no reconciliation was made on the physical count versus the subsidiary ledgers. The GSO also has not made a separate inventory taking and record-keeping

observations.recommendation:
Require the management to explain why the obligation of the Municipality to the GSIS was not recorded.
Direct the Municipal Accountant:

b.1 To immediately record the obligations to the Government Service Insurance System under the account “Due to GSIS”.

b.2 To make adjustments to the account “Due to GSIS” for the amount of interest paid to the System.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015
Finding No. 4 | Municipal Accountant will effect drawing of the JEV to record the liability. An appropriation was also passed to gradually pay-off the obligation.
observations.reason: The obligation to GSIS was already taken up and monthly amortizatipayments were made from 2015-2016.

observations.recommendation:
Management should enforce strictly the provisions on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
	
Direct the Accountant to refrain from giving additional cash advances unless the previous ones are settled. 

Monitor the liquidation and send written reminders to be duly noted/approved by the Municipal Mayor within ten (10) days before the 30-day period will lapse.

Retrieve the list of prior years unsettled cash advances and determine the status of accountable officers thereon if they can still be located, otherwise request from the Commission on Audit for the write-off of accounts.

Demand settlement and/or cause the withholding of the amount due the concerned officials and employees to compel them to settle their account, pursuant to Section 16 of Executive Order No. 248, as amended.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015
Finding No. 5 | Local Chief Executive manifested to hold travel orders to employees and officials having unliquidated cash advances. The same instruction was directed to the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer not to sign and issue checks for persons with pending cash advances.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the supplemental/amended ordinance covering the inclusion of the amount of P1,820,000.00 incurred as equity for the implementation of Agri-Pinoy Corn Program of DA-RFO.

If no ordinance was enacted for the purpose, to return the amount of P1,820,000.00 to the 20% MDF to be used in funding development projects.

To stop the practice of utilizing funds for programs/activities/projects not included in the approved budget.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give utmost priority in the preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely supervise the accounting personnel tasked to prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements.

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct monthly reconciliation of the cash accounts to facilitate smooth preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements.	

Advise the Municipal Mayor to augment the personnel implement in the accounting office.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015
Finding No. 7 | Municipal Accountant promised to submit the bank reconciliations on all accounts by February 24, 2017
observations.reason: Re-iterated

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management should inquire with the National Treasury on the procedure for the return of the unexpended balance of PDAF and to ultimately return the amount to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund of government in compliance with the Decision of the Supreme Court in the case of Belgica et al. vs. Ochoa et al. and COA Circular No. No. 2015-001 dated January 29, 2015.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015
Finding No. 8 | Promised to present Official Receipt from the Bureau of Treasury confirming the return of the amount.
observations.reason: Check no. 1308947 was already drawn by the Municipal Treasurer amounting to P 400,000 under Voucher No.100-2017-02-109 but is yet to be transmitted and signed by the LCE

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the treasury and accounting office should exert full efforts in identifying and analyzing the possible cause of the negative balance and effect the necessary adjusting journal entry in the books of accounts. The Official Receipts issued corresponding to the amount of P11, 862 should be identified and be taken-up in the books.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015
Finding no. 9 | Municipal Treasurer have agreed that a certification of the official receipt pertaining to the cash overdraft can no longer be traced or located be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the drawing of he JEV
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant already effected the adjustment attached by the certification from the municipal treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
The LGU should specifically assign somebody to man the preparation and monitoring of the said reports in the absence of a General Services Officer.

The person assigned should then verify monthly the official travels and make sure that all trip tickets are properly and completely filled up

Prepare and submit the reports to the Auditor as required in the regulation to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the month.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015
Finding No. 10 | Appointed a new supply officer whose reports of fuel consumption for the month of March to December 2016 were already submitted to the office of the auditor on February 09, 2017
observations.reason: The GSO/Supply Officer already submitted to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travel.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) shall determine the LGU properties not yet covered by insurance and recommend to the local legislative body the amount necessary for insurance coverage for appropriation.

The local legislative body shall include in the appropriation ordinance of the municipality the amount necessary for the insurance coverage of LGU properties as recommended by the LCE.

The Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer shall facilitate the application with the GSIS of an insurance coverage of all its properties that the municipality has an insurable interest to insure that any loss or damage to the same will be indemnified by the insurer.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015
Finding No. 11 | Management already designated a liaison officer to the GSIS but follow-up as to the estimated insurance cost is yet to be made so that the estimate be the basis of the Municipal Treasurer to make representation to the Sangguniang Bayan for the earmarking and passing of an appropriation
observations.reason: Management appointed a liason officer. They have already processed an application with GSIS with supported documents to process the insured properties. Forms were already filled up but is yet to be finalized and signed by the LCE. They have manifested to submit the form and accompanying documentsthis year.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following: 

All items in the Municipality’s Approved Annual Budget should be made and presented with particularity in order to afford this office the ascertainment that all disbursements that pass in audit can be traced and identified, and allow us to validate and verify as to its legality and propriety.

The Municipality should provide the details of each line item of the budget before the same shall be passed for enactment.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015
Finding No. 12 | Concerned officials agreed to submit utilization report and Reports on Check Issued to determine the Breakdown of the lumped appropriation
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the management should:

Procure common-use supplies from the PS-DBM in accordance with the regulations.

Submit the required certifications from PS-DBM to support the validity of the disbursements; otherwise these shall be disallowed in audit.

Observe strictly the policies and procedures under the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 in the procurement of common-use supplies.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015
Finding No. 13 | Municipal Treasurer agreed to comply citing that checks for payment will not be issued if the certificate of non-availability will not be attached with the Disbursement Voucher.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented as common-use supplies are already procured from DBM- PS

observations.recommendation:

The municipality should exert extra effort for the execution of the deed of donation by the legitimate owners to support the claim of ownership or they may take necessary steps to strengthen ownership over the property

The management is advised to request the Land Registration Office of the Province to consider the Comprehensive Use Plan of the Municipality to be the basis of titling on the subject land.
